nodes.en: The study of matter, energy, motion, and the fundamental forces governing them, from subatomic particles to the large-scale structure of the universe. Covers mechanics, quantum theory, thermodynamics, electromagnetism, relativity, and nuclear, particle, astro-, geo- and biophysics; distinguished from Chemistry by its focus on fundamental interactions and physical law rather than the transformation of substances.
nodes.cs: Studium hmoty, energie, pohybu a základních sil, které je řídí, od subatomárních částic po velkorozměrovou strukturu vesmíru. Zahrnuje mechaniku, kvantovou teorii, termodynamiku, elektromagnetismus, relativitu a jadernou, částicovou, astro-, geo- a biofyziku; od chemie se liší důrazem na fundamentální interakce a fyzikální zákony, nikoli na přeměny látek.
nodes.brief:
Physics is the natural science of matter, energy, motion, and the fundamental forces that govern them, from subatomic particles to the large-scale structure of the universe. It seeks the most general laws of nature: how bodies move under forces, how energy transforms and dissipates, how fields propagate, and how matter behaves at the smallest and largest scales. Its method combines precise measurement with mathematical formalism, and its results supply the quantitative core that every other natural science builds on.

Physics sits beside [[01.02]] within the Natural Sciences: physics asks how fundamental interactions and physical law operate, while chemistry asks how substances combine and transform by applying those same laws to atoms and bonds. Internally physics spans a classical and a modern regime — [[01.01.02]] governs the probabilistic behavior of matter at atomic scale, and [[01.01.05]] revises the classical picture of space and time at high speed and strong gravity — with further subfields carrying it outward into astro-, geo- and biophysics, which is why it also borders [[01.05]] on questions of stellar and planetary structure.

The mathematical language in which physical law is stated comes from [[02.01]]: calculus, differential equations and linear algebra are inseparable from physics's own formalism, and physics has in turn driven mathematics forward, from Newton's calculus to the geometry general relativity demands. For a broad survey of the field, see the [Encyclopaedia Britannica entry on physics](https://www.britannica.com/science/physics-science) and the reference tables at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/HFrame.html).

nodes.en: Motion and equilibrium of macroscopic bodies under forces, described by Newtonian, Lagrangian and Hamiltonian formulations. Spans kinematics, dynamics, statics, fluid mechanics and oscillations and waves — the deterministic regime valid at everyday scales and speeds, before quantum or relativistic effects matter.
nodes.cs: Pohyb a rovnováha makroskopických těles pod působením sil, popsané newtonovskou, lagrangeovskou a hamiltonovskou formulací. Zahrnuje kinematiku, dynamiku, statiku, mechaniku tekutin a kmity a vlnění — deterministický režim platný v běžných měřítkách a rychlostech, kde se ještě neprojevují kvantové ani relativistické efekty.
nodes.brief:
Classical Mechanics is the physics of motion and equilibrium in macroscopic bodies under applied forces, formulated through the Newtonian, Lagrangian and Hamiltonian frameworks. It holds wherever speeds stay far below light and scales stay far above the atomic — the deterministic regime that governs everyday engineering, ballistics and celestial orbits before quantum uncertainty or relativistic corrections become significant.

Within [[01.01]], Classical Mechanics is organized into five subfields that build on one another: [[01.01.01.01]] describes motion without reference to its causes, [[01.01.01.02]] adds the forces that produce it, and Statics, Fluid Mechanics and Oscillations & Waves extend the same Newtonian machinery to bodies at rest, to continuous media, and to periodic motion. It borders [[01.01.02]], which replaces its deterministic trajectories with probabilistic wavefunctions once scales shrink to the atomic, and [[01.01.05]], which corrects its absolute space and time once speeds approach that of light.

Classical mechanics remains the working physics of most engineering disciplines and the historical starting point of the field — its calculus-based formalism, refined by Lagrange and Hamilton, is documented in depth at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/mechanics.html) and in the [Feynman Lectures on Physics](https://www.feynmanlectures.caltech.edu/I_toc.html).

nodes.en: Describing motion — position, velocity, acceleration and trajectories — without reference to the forces causing it. Covers motion equations, projectile, circular and relative motion, and reference frames; the causal explanation of motion belongs to Dynamics.
nodes.cs: Popis pohybu — polohy, rychlosti, zrychlení a trajektorií — bez ohledu na síly, které jej způsobují. Zahrnuje pohybové rovnice, šikmý vrh, kruhový a relativní pohyb a vztažné soustavy; příčinné vysvětlení pohybu patří do dynamiky.
nodes.brief:
Kinematics is the geometric description of motion — position, velocity, acceleration and trajectory — treated independently of the forces that produce it. It supplies the vocabulary and mathematical machinery (equations of motion, the geometry of projectile and circular paths, transformations between reference frames) that the rest of [[01.01.01]] Classical Mechanics builds on: [[01.01.01.02]] Dynamics adds Newton's laws and force to explain why bodies move as kinematics describes, while [[01.01.01.03]] Statics is the special case of kinematics in which acceleration and velocity are held at zero.

Within its own subfield, kinematics covers motion equations for uniform and uniformly accelerated motion, projectile motion as the superposition of horizontal and vertical components, circular motion and its centripetal description, relative motion between observers, and the choice of reference frame that makes a given trajectory simplest to describe. It borders [[01.01.01.05]] Oscillations & Waves, where the same descriptive tools — position and velocity as functions of time — are applied to periodic rather than translational motion.

Kinematics is typically the first topic taught in any mechanics course precisely because it makes no claims about cause: it describes what happens, leaving dynamics to explain why. A thorough treatment of its equations and graphical methods is available at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/mechanics.html#mechcon) and in the [MIT OpenCourseWare Classical Mechanics materials](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/8-01sc-classical-mechanics-fall-2016/).

nodes.en: The mathematical relations linking position, velocity, acceleration and time for uniformly accelerated and general motion — the standard kinematic (SUVAT) equations and their derivation via calculus. Used to solve for unknown motion quantities in one and more dimensions.
nodes.cs: Matematické vztahy spojující polohu, rychlost, zrychlení a čas pro rovnoměrně zrychlený i obecný pohyb — standardní kinematické rovnice a jejich odvození pomocí diferenciálního počtu. Slouží k výpočtu neznámých pohybových veličin v jedné i více dimenzích.

nodes.en: Two-dimensional motion of a body launched into a gravitational field, combining uniform horizontal velocity with uniformly accelerated vertical fall. Covers trajectory shape, range, time of flight and maximum height, typically neglecting or explicitly modelling air resistance.
nodes.cs: Dvourozměrný pohyb tělesa vrženého v tíhovém poli, který kombinuje rovnoměrný vodorovný pohyb s rovnoměrně zrychleným svislým pádem. Zahrnuje tvar trajektorie, dolet, dobu letu a maximální výšku, obvykle při zanedbání nebo explicitním modelování odporu vzduchu.

nodes.en: Motion along a circular path: angular velocity, period and frequency, and the centripetal acceleration directed toward the centre that keeps the body on the curve. Includes uniform and non-uniform cases and the relations between angular and linear quantities.
nodes.cs: Pohyb po kružnici: úhlová rychlost, perioda a frekvence a dostředivé zrychlení směřující do středu, které udržuje těleso na křivce. Zahrnuje rovnoměrný i nerovnoměrný případ a vztahy mezi úhlovými a obvodovými veličinami.

nodes.en: How the observed velocity and trajectory of a body change between observers moving with respect to each other — velocity addition and relative-velocity problems such as river crossings, moving platforms and pursuit. Focuses on transforming motion between observers; the coordinate systems themselves are covered by Reference frames.
nodes.cs: Jak se pozorovaná rychlost a trajektorie tělesa mění mezi navzájem se pohybujícími pozorovateli — skládání rychlostí a úlohy o relativní rychlosti, například přeplouvání řeky, pohybující se plošiny nebo pronásledování. Soustředí se na převod pohybu mezi pozorovateli; samotné souřadnicové soustavy pokrývá uzel Vztažné soustavy.

nodes.en: Coordinate systems from which motion is measured: inertial versus non-inertial frames, Galilean transformations, and the fictitious forces (centrifugal, Coriolis) that appear in accelerating frames. Complements Relative motion, which handles the velocity arithmetic between frames.
nodes.cs: Souřadnicové soustavy, z nichž se pohyb měří: inerciální versus neinerciální soustavy, Galileiho transformace a zdánlivé síly (odstředivá, Coriolisova) vznikající ve zrychlujících soustavách. Doplňuje uzel Relativní pohyb, který řeší počítání s rychlostmi mezi soustavami.

nodes.en: Why bodies move as they do: forces and the laws relating them to changes of motion. Covers Newton's laws, concrete force interactions, work and energy, momentum and collisions — the causal counterpart to Kinematics' pure description of motion.
nodes.cs: Proč se tělesa pohybují právě tak, jak se pohybují: síly a zákony spojující je se změnami pohybu. Zahrnuje Newtonovy zákony, konkrétní silová působení, práci a energii, hybnost a srážky — příčinný protějšek čistě popisné kinematiky.
nodes.brief:
Dynamics explains why bodies move as they do: it studies the forces acting on a body and the laws relating force to changes of motion. Where [[01.01.01.01]] Kinematics describes trajectories without asking what produces them, dynamics supplies the causal machinery — Newton's three laws, the classification of concrete forces (gravity, friction, tension, normal force), and the derived concepts of work, energy, and momentum that let a mechanics problem be solved without tracking force at every instant.

Its core results cascade into the rest of [[01.01.01]] Classical Mechanics: the equilibrium condition of [[01.01.01.03]] Statics is the zero-net-force, zero-net-torque limit of dynamics, and [[01.01.01.05]] Oscillations & Waves applies Newton's second law to restoring forces to derive simple harmonic motion. Collisions and momentum conservation, covered here, are the discrete-time counterpart of the continuous force analysis that dominates the rest of the field.

Dynamics is the branch most directly tested against experiment — planetary orbits, rocket propulsion, and everyday collisions all reduce to its equations. A systematic treatment of Newton's laws, work-energy theorems and momentum conservation is available at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/newt.html) and in the [Feynman Lectures on Physics, Volume I](https://www.feynmanlectures.caltech.edu/I_09.html).

nodes.en: The three laws of motion — inertia, force equals mass times acceleration, and action–reaction — as the axiomatic core of classical dynamics. Covers their statements, ranges of validity, and application to free-body problem solving.
nodes.cs: Tři pohybové zákony — zákon setrvačnosti, zákon síly (F = ma) a zákon akce a reakce — jako axiomatické jádro klasické dynamiky. Zahrnuje jejich znění, meze platnosti a použití při řešení úloh pomocí silových diagramů.

nodes.en: The concrete forces of mechanics — gravity, normal force, friction, tension, spring and drag forces — their origins, models and representation in free-body diagrams. Characterizes individual interactions; the general laws that combine them are covered by Newton's laws.
nodes.cs: Konkrétní síly mechaniky — tíha, normálová síla, tření, tahová síla, pružinové a odporové síly — jejich původ, modely a znázornění v silových diagramech. Charakterizuje jednotlivé interakce; obecné zákony, které je spojují, pokrývá uzel Newtonovy zákony.

nodes.en: Work done by forces, kinetic and potential energy, power, the work–energy theorem and conservation of mechanical energy — scalar tools for solving motion problems without tracking forces through time.
nodes.cs: Práce sil, kinetická a potenciální energie, výkon, věta o práci a energii a zákon zachování mechanické energie — skalární nástroje pro řešení úloh o pohybu bez sledování sil v čase.

nodes.en: Linear momentum and impulse, conservation of momentum in isolated systems, and centre-of-mass motion. Provides the vector conservation law that the Collisions node applies to impact events.
nodes.cs: Hybnost a impuls síly, zákon zachování hybnosti v izolovaných soustavách a pohyb těžiště. Poskytuje vektorový zákon zachování, který uzel Srážky aplikuje na rázové děje.

nodes.en: Impact events between bodies: elastic, inelastic and perfectly inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions, the coefficient of restitution, and which quantities — momentum, kinetic energy — each case conserves.
nodes.cs: Rázové děje mezi tělesy: pružné, nepružné a dokonale nepružné srážky v jedné a dvou dimenzích, koeficient restituce a to, které veličiny — hybnost, kinetická energie — se v jednotlivých případech zachovávají.

nodes.en: Equilibrium of bodies at rest: balancing forces and torques, centre of gravity, stability, and the analysis of rigid structures such as beams, trusses and supports. The zero-acceleration limit of dynamics, foundational for engineering structures.
nodes.cs: Rovnováha těles v klidu: vyvažování sil a momentů, těžiště, stabilita a analýza tuhých konstrukcí, jako jsou nosníky, příhradové konstrukce a podpěry. Limitní případ dynamiky s nulovým zrychlením, základ inženýrských konstrukcí.
nodes.brief:
Statics studies bodies and structures in mechanical equilibrium — systems whose net force and net torque are both zero, so nothing accelerates. Its central tools are the vector balance of forces and moments, the location of a body's centre of gravity, and the criteria for stability, applied to rigid structures such as beams, trusses, cables and supports.

Statics is the zero-acceleration limit of [[01.01.01.02]] Dynamics: every equilibrium equation it uses is Newton's second law with acceleration set to zero, so it inherits its force vocabulary directly from dynamics rather than developing a separate one. Within [[01.01.01]] Classical Mechanics it sits alongside [[01.01.01.01]] Kinematics, which shares its interest in position and geometry but allows motion, and [[01.01.01.04]] Fluid Mechanics, which extends equilibrium analysis from rigid bodies to continuous media.

Because its results translate directly into load-bearing calculations, statics is foundational to civil and mechanical engineering — truss analysis, beam bending and structural stability all originate here. See [HyperPhysics on equilibrium](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/torq2.html) and the open [Engineering Mechanics: Statics](https://open.umn.edu/opentextbooks/textbooks/engineering-mechanics-statics) textbook for worked treatments.

nodes.en: Behaviour of liquids and gases at rest and in motion: pressure, buoyancy, continuity, Bernoulli's principle, viscosity and turbulence. Treats matter as a continuum, in contrast to the rigid-body focus of its sibling mechanics nodes.
nodes.cs: Chování kapalin a plynů v klidu i v pohybu: tlak, vztlak, rovnice kontinuity, Bernoulliho princip, viskozita a turbulence. Pojímá látku jako kontinuum, na rozdíl od sourozeneckých uzlů mechaniky zaměřených na tuhá tělesa.
nodes.brief:
Fluid Mechanics studies liquids and gases as continuous media rather than as collections of discrete rigid bodies: pressure and buoyancy at rest, and continuity, Bernoulli's principle, viscosity and turbulence once the fluid is in motion. This continuum treatment is what separates it from the rest of [[01.01.01]] Classical Mechanics, whose other branches model matter as rigid or point-like bodies.

Fluid statics — pressure, buoyancy, Archimedes' principle — is the direct continuum analogue of [[01.01.01.03]] Statics, replacing discrete force balance with pressure distributed over a surface, while fluid dynamics extends [[01.01.01.02]] Dynamics' force-and-acceleration reasoning to flowing continua via the Navier–Stokes equations. Viscous and turbulent flow, where the simple Bernoulli approximation breaks down, connects fluid mechanics to the dissipative, often chaotic behaviour also explored in [[01.01.01.05]] Oscillations & Waves.

Fluid mechanics underlies aerodynamics, hydraulic engineering, meteorology and cardiovascular physiology alike. See [HyperPhysics on fluid mechanics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/pflu.html) and MIT OpenCourseWare's [Fluid Mechanics](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/2-25-advanced-fluid-mechanics-fall-2013/) course materials for a fuller treatment.

nodes.en: Periodic motion and its propagation: simple harmonic motion, pendulums, damped and driven oscillations, resonance, and mechanical waves including sound — superposition, interference, standing waves and the Doppler effect. Electromagnetic waves belong under Electromagnetism.
nodes.cs: Periodický pohyb a jeho šíření: harmonický pohyb, kyvadla, tlumené a buzené kmity, rezonance a mechanické vlnění včetně zvuku — superpozice, interference, stojaté vlny a Dopplerův jev. Elektromagnetické vlny patří pod Elektromagnetismus.
nodes.brief:
Oscillations & Waves studies periodic motion and its propagation through a medium: simple harmonic motion, pendulums, damped and driven oscillators, resonance, and mechanical waves — including sound — with their superposition, interference, standing-wave patterns and the Doppler effect. It is the branch of [[01.01.01]] Classical Mechanics devoted to motion that repeats rather than accumulates displacement.

A simple harmonic oscillator is [[01.01.01.02]] Dynamics applied to a restoring force proportional to displacement, so its equations of motion follow directly from Newton's second law; damping and driving then extend that same force analysis with dissipative and time-varying terms. Once oscillation is coupled from one point in a medium to the next, it becomes a mechanical wave, sharing its kinematic description — position as a function of time and space — with [[01.01.01.01]] Kinematics. Electromagnetic waves, though mathematically similar, belong to Electromagnetism rather than here, since they require no material medium to propagate.

Resonance and wave interference explain phenomena from bridge oscillation failures to musical acoustics. See [HyperPhysics on oscillations](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/shm.html) and the [Feynman Lectures on Physics, Volume I, on waves](https://www.feynmanlectures.caltech.edu/I_47.html) for a thorough treatment.

nodes.en: Physics of matter and energy at atomic and subatomic scales, where states are wavefunctions, observables are quantized and measurement outcomes are probabilistic. Spans foundational principles and interpretation, quantum field theory, quantum computing and quantum optics.
nodes.cs: Fyzika hmoty a energie v atomových a subatomárních měřítkách, kde jsou stavy vlnovými funkcemi, pozorovatelné veličiny kvantované a výsledky měření pravděpodobnostní. Zahrnuje základní principy a interpretaci, kvantovou teorii pole, kvantové počítání a kvantovou optiku.
nodes.brief:
Quantum Mechanics is the physics of matter and energy at atomic and subatomic scales, where a system's state is a wavefunction, its observables are quantized, and measurement outcomes are inherently probabilistic rather than determined in advance. It replaces the deterministic trajectories of [[01.01.01]] once scales shrink to the size of atoms, and its predictions — verified to extraordinary precision — underlie the modern understanding of chemical bonding, spectroscopy and condensed matter.

Within [[01.01]], the field spans [[01.01.02.01]], which addresses the interpretation and axioms of the theory itself, and [[01.01.02.02]], which extends it to fields and relativistic particles; the applied branches Quantum Computing and Quantum Optics exploit superposition and entanglement for computation and light–matter interaction respectively. It borders [[01.01.07]], whose Standard Model is built on quantum field theory, sharing the same formal foundation one level further down in scale.

The theory's postulates, formal apparatus and historical development are surveyed at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/quacon.html) and in the [Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy's entry on quantum mechanics](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/qm/).

nodes.en: The conceptual and mathematical core of quantum theory: superposition, entanglement, the uncertainty principle, the measurement problem, Bell inequalities and interpretations such as Copenhagen, many-worlds and pilot-wave. Deals with what the formalism means, not with its technological applications.
nodes.cs: Pojmové a matematické jádro kvantové teorie: superpozice, provázanost, princip neurčitosti, problém měření, Bellovy nerovnosti a interpretace, jako je kodaňská, mnohosvětová nebo teorie pilotní vlny. Zabývá se tím, co formalismus znamená, nikoli jeho technologickými aplikacemi.
nodes.brief:
Quantum Foundations is the conceptual and mathematical core of quantum theory: superposition, entanglement, the uncertainty principle, the measurement problem, Bell's inequalities, and the competing interpretations — Copenhagen, many-worlds, pilot-wave — that try to say what the formalism means. It asks what quantum mechanics IS, as distinct from how it is put to use.

Within [[01.01.02]] Quantum Mechanics, foundations is the axiomatic root that the applied branches draw on without needing to resolve: [[01.01.02.02]] Quantum Field Theory takes superposition and quantization as given and extends them to relativistic fields, while [[01.01.02.03]] Quantum Computing exploits superposition and entanglement as engineering resources rather than philosophical puzzles. [[01.01.02.04]] Quantum Optics likewise builds photon-level experiments — many of them direct tests of Bell inequalities — on the foundational framework developed here.

Because interpretation questions remain genuinely unsettled, this is also where physics and philosophy of science most directly meet. See the [Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy's entry on quantum mechanics](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/qm/) and [HyperPhysics on quantum concepts](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/quacon.html#quacon) for an overview of the formalism and its interpretations.

nodes.en: The union of quantum mechanics and special relativity in which particles are excitations of fields: field quantization, Feynman diagrams, renormalization, and the gauge theories underlying the Standard Model. The theoretical machinery behind Particle Physics.
nodes.cs: Spojení kvantové mechaniky a speciální relativity, v němž jsou částice excitacemi polí: kvantování polí, Feynmanovy diagramy, renormalizace a kalibrační teorie, na nichž stojí standardní model. Teoretický aparát, o který se opírá částicová fyzika.
nodes.brief:
Quantum Field Theory unites quantum mechanics with special relativity by treating particles as excitations of underlying fields rather than as point objects moving through space. Its machinery — field quantization, Feynman diagrams, renormalization, and the gauge theories that organize the electromagnetic, weak and strong interactions — forms the theoretical basis of the Standard Model of particle physics.

Where [[01.01.02.01]] Quantum Foundations asks what superposition and measurement mean, quantum field theory takes those postulates as settled machinery and builds relativistic particle physics on top of them, so the two branches divide interpretation from application within [[01.01.02]] Quantum Mechanics. Its gauge-theory formalism is also the direct theoretical ancestor of the Standard Model developed in particle physics, and the quantized electromagnetic field it introduces underlies the photon-level phenomena studied in [[01.01.02.04]] Quantum Optics, making it the bridge between quantum mechanics proper and both high-energy and optical physics.

Feynman diagrams and renormalization, first developed to tame QFT's divergent integrals, are now standard tools across theoretical physics. See the [Feynman Lectures on Physics](https://www.feynmanlectures.caltech.edu/) for the conceptual groundwork and the [Particle Data Group's review](https://pdg.lbl.gov/) for the Standard Model results QFT predicts.

nodes.en: Information processing with quantum systems: qubits, gates and circuits, algorithms such as Shor's and Grover's, quantum error correction, and hardware platforms (superconducting, trapped-ion, photonic). Where quantum theory becomes a computational technology.
nodes.cs: Zpracování informací pomocí kvantových systémů: qubity, hradla a obvody, algoritmy jako Shorův a Groverův, kvantová korekce chyb a hardwarové platformy (supravodivé, iontové pasti, fotonické). Místo, kde se kvantová teorie stává výpočetní technologií.
nodes.brief:
Quantum Computing treats quantum mechanical systems as a computational resource: information is encoded in qubits, manipulated by quantum gates and circuits, and processed by algorithms — Shor's for factoring, Grover's for search — that outperform their best-known classical counterparts. Quantum error correction and physical hardware platforms (superconducting circuits, trapped ions, photonics) turn the theory into engineering.

It is the point where [[01.01.02.01]] Quantum Foundations' superposition and entanglement stop being interpretive puzzles and become resources to be engineered and protected from decoherence, making quantum computing the applied, technological sibling of foundations within [[01.01.02]] Quantum Mechanics. Its photonic hardware platform overlaps directly with [[01.01.02.04]] Quantum Optics, which supplies the techniques for generating and manipulating the entangled photon states some quantum computers and quantum communication systems rely on.

Quantum computing is one of the most active applied-physics fields today, with steady progress on error correction and qubit counts. See the [Qiskit textbook](https://qiskit.org/learn) for an implementation-oriented introduction and [IBM Quantum](https://www.ibm.com/quantum) for the algorithmic foundations and current hardware roadmaps.

nodes.en: Quantum behaviour of light and its interaction with matter: photons, coherent and squeezed states, cavity quantum electrodynamics, lasers at the quantum level, and photonic implementations of entanglement and quantum communication.
nodes.cs: Kvantové chování světla a jeho interakce s hmotou: fotony, koherentní a stlačené stavy, dutinová kvantová elektrodynamika, lasery na kvantové úrovni a fotonické realizace provázanosti a kvantové komunikace.
nodes.brief:
Quantum Optics studies the quantum behaviour of light and its interaction with matter: individual photons, coherent and squeezed states, cavity quantum electrodynamics, the quantum-level operation of lasers, and photonic implementations of entanglement and quantum communication. It treats the electromagnetic field itself as a quantum system rather than as a classical wave.

Its photon-based experiments are frequently the most direct tests of the entanglement and Bell-inequality questions raised in [[01.01.02.01]] Quantum Foundations, and its squeezed and entangled photon states supply one of the hardware platforms used by [[01.01.02.03]] Quantum Computing and quantum communication. The quantized electromagnetic field it works with is a direct application of [[01.01.02.02]] Quantum Field Theory's field-quantization formalism to a single, experimentally accessible field.

Quantum optics experiments — from single-photon interference to satellite-based quantum key distribution — have repeatedly closed loopholes in tests of quantum theory. See the [Optica (formerly OSA) quantum optics resources](https://www.optica.org/) and [HyperPhysics on the quantum nature of light](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/quantum/qm.html) for further reading.

nodes.en: Heat, temperature, work and entropy: the four laws of thermodynamics, thermodynamic cycles and engines, phase transitions, and the statistical-mechanics view linking macroscopic quantities to microscopic behaviour.
nodes.cs: Teplo, teplota, práce a entropie: čtyři termodynamické zákony, termodynamické cykly a tepelné stroje, fázové přechody a statisticko-mechanický pohled spojující makroskopické veličiny s mikroskopickým chováním.
nodes.brief:
Thermodynamics is the physics of heat, temperature, work and entropy — the four laws that govern how energy is exchanged, converted and irreversibly degraded in any physical system. It supplies the macroscopic account of thermodynamic cycles, heat engines and phase transitions, and connects to the statistical-mechanics view in which these bulk quantities emerge from the collective behaviour of vast numbers of particles.

Within [[01.01]], Thermodynamics complements [[01.01.01]]: where classical mechanics tracks the trajectories of individual bodies, thermodynamics and its statistical-mechanical foundation describe what happens when the number of particles becomes too large to track individually, a view shared with [[01.01.02]]'s treatment of quantum statistics. It also underlies [[01.01.04]]'s account of resistive heating and electromagnetic energy dissipation, and parallels [[01.02.04]], which applies the same laws to chemical reactions and phase equilibria.

The four laws, their historical derivation and worked examples of thermodynamic cycles are covered at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/thermo/thermo.html) and in the [Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy's entry on the philosophy of statistical mechanics](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/statphys-statmech/).

nodes.name: Laws of Thermodynamics
nodes.en: The four foundational laws that govern energy, heat and entropy in every physical system, from the zeroth law defining temperature to the third law's absolute-zero limit.
nodes.cs: Čtyři základní zákony, jimiž se řídí energie, teplo a entropie v každém fyzikálním systému — od nultého zákona definujícího teplotu po třetí zákon a mez absolutní nuly.

nodes.name: Zeroth Law & Temperature
nodes.en: Defines thermal equilibrium and temperature: if two systems are each in equilibrium with a third, they are in equilibrium with each other, making temperature a consistent, transitive property.
nodes.cs: Definuje tepelnou rovnováhu a teplotu: jsou-li dva systémy v rovnováze se třetím, jsou v rovnováze i navzájem, což činí z teploty konzistentní, tranzitivní veličinu.
nodes.brief:
Defines thermal equilibrium and temperature: if two systems are each in equilibrium with a third, they are in equilibrium with each other, making temperature a consistent, transitive property.

### Concepts
- **Thermal equilibrium** — State in which no net heat flows between systems in contact, so their temperatures are equal.
- **Temperature scales** — Empirical (Celsius, Fahrenheit) and absolute (Kelvin) scales measuring the degree of thermal agitation.
- **Thermometry** — Measurement of temperature via a property that varies reproducibly with it, such as gas volume or resistance.
- **Transitivity of equilibrium** — The logical basis of the zeroth law that lets temperature label equivalence classes of systems.

nodes.name: First Law & Internal Energy
nodes.en: Energy conservation for thermodynamic systems: the change in internal energy equals heat added minus work done, so heat and work are two forms of the same conserved quantity.
nodes.cs: Zachování energie pro termodynamické systémy: změna vnitřní energie se rovná dodanému teplu zmenšenému o vykonanou práci, takže teplo a práce jsou dvě formy téže zachovávané veličiny.
nodes.brief:
Energy conservation for thermodynamic systems: the change in internal energy equals heat added minus work done, so heat and work are two forms of the same conserved quantity.

### Concepts
- **Internal energy** — The total microscopic kinetic and potential energy of a system, a state function.
- **Heat and work** — The two path-dependent ways energy crosses a system boundary.
- **Work of expansion** — Pressure-volume work done by or on a gas as its volume changes.
- **Enthalpy** — The combination U + pV, convenient for processes at constant pressure.
- **Heat capacity** — The heat required to raise a system's temperature by one degree, at constant volume or pressure.

nodes.name: Second Law & Entropy
nodes.en: Entropy of an isolated system never decreases, setting the direction of spontaneous change and the irreversibility of natural processes; it forbids perfect conversion of heat into work.
nodes.cs: Entropie izolovaného systému nikdy neklesá, což určuje směr samovolných dějů a nevratnost přírodních procesů; zakazuje dokonalou přeměnu tepla na práci.
nodes.brief:
Entropy of an isolated system never decreases, setting the direction of spontaneous change and the irreversibility of natural processes; it forbids perfect conversion of heat into work.

### Concepts
- **Entropy** — A state function measuring the dispersal of energy and the number of accessible microstates.
- **Clausius statement** — Heat does not spontaneously flow from a colder to a hotter body.
- **Kelvin-Planck statement** — No cyclic process can convert heat entirely into work with no other effect.
- **Reversible vs irreversible** — Idealised quasi-static processes conserve total entropy; real processes generate it.
- **Arrow of time** — The thermodynamic asymmetry that distinguishes past from future via increasing entropy.

nodes.name: Third Law & Absolute Zero
nodes.en: As temperature approaches absolute zero the entropy of a perfect crystal approaches a constant minimum, implying absolute zero is unattainable in finitely many steps.
nodes.cs: Když se teplota blíží absolutní nule, entropie dokonalého krystalu se blíží konstantnímu minimu, z čehož plyne, že absolutní nuly nelze dosáhnout konečným počtem kroků.
nodes.brief:
As temperature approaches absolute zero the entropy of a perfect crystal approaches a constant minimum, implying absolute zero is unattainable in finitely many steps.

### Concepts
- **Nernst heat theorem** — Entropy change of a reaction approaches zero as temperature approaches absolute zero.
- **Residual entropy** — Nonzero ground-state entropy in disordered solids such as glasses or ice.
- **Unattainability** — No finite sequence of processes can cool a system to exactly absolute zero.
- **Cryogenics** — The production and study of very low temperatures approaching the absolute-zero limit.

nodes.name: Thermodynamic Potentials & Relations
nodes.en: The state functions — internal energy, enthalpy, Helmholtz and Gibbs free energy — and the Maxwell relations and Legendre transforms that connect them.
nodes.cs: Stavové funkce — vnitřní energie, entalpie, Helmholtzova a Gibbsova volná energie — a Maxwellovy relace a Legendreovy transformace, které je propojují.

nodes.name: Free Energies
nodes.en: Helmholtz and Gibbs free energies combine energy and entropy into potentials whose minima locate equilibrium at constant temperature-volume or temperature-pressure, and whose changes bound available work.
nodes.cs: Helmholtzova a Gibbsova volná energie spojují energii a entropii do potenciálů, jejichž minima určují rovnováhu při konstantní teplotě a objemu či teplotě a tlaku a jejichž změny omezují využitelnou práci.
nodes.brief:
Helmholtz and Gibbs free energies combine energy and entropy into potentials whose minima locate equilibrium at constant temperature-volume or temperature-pressure, and whose changes bound available work.

### Concepts
- **Helmholtz free energy** — F = U - TS, minimised at equilibrium at constant temperature and volume.
- **Gibbs free energy** — G = H - TS, minimised at equilibrium at constant temperature and pressure.
- **Spontaneity criterion** — A process is spontaneous when the relevant free energy decreases.
- **Chemical potential** — The change in free energy when one particle is added, driving mass transfer and reaction.
- **Maximum work** — The free-energy change sets the greatest useful work extractable from a process.

nodes.name: Maxwell Relations & State Functions
nodes.en: Equality of mixed second derivatives of the thermodynamic potentials yields the Maxwell relations, linking hard-to-measure quantities such as entropy changes to measurable pressure, volume and temperature data.
nodes.cs: Rovnost smíšených druhých derivací termodynamických potenciálů dává Maxwellovy relace, které spojují obtížně měřitelné veličiny, jako jsou změny entropie, s měřitelnými daty o tlaku, objemu a teplotě.
nodes.brief:
Equality of mixed second derivatives of the thermodynamic potentials yields the Maxwell relations, linking hard-to-measure quantities such as entropy changes to measurable pressure, volume and temperature data.

### Concepts
- **State function** — A quantity determined by the current state, independent of the path taken to reach it.
- **Exact differential** — The mathematical property of state functions ensuring path-independent changes.
- **Legendre transform** — The operation generating one potential from another by swapping conjugate variables.
- **Conjugate variables** — Paired quantities such as pressure-volume and temperature-entropy whose product is energy.
- **Response functions** — Measurable derivatives such as compressibility and thermal expansion linked by the relations.

nodes.name: Heat Engines & Cycles
nodes.en: How cyclic processes convert heat into work, the efficiency limits imposed by the second law, and the idealised cycles that model real engines and refrigerators.
nodes.cs: Jak cyklické děje přeměňují teplo na práci, meze účinnosti dané druhým zákonem a idealizované cykly modelující reálné motory a chladicí zařízení.

nodes.name: Carnot Cycle & Efficiency
nodes.en: The Carnot cycle of two isothermal and two adiabatic steps is the most efficient possible heat engine between two reservoirs, its efficiency fixed by their temperatures alone and independent of the working substance.
nodes.cs: Carnotův cyklus ze dvou izotermických a dvou adiabatických dějů je nejúčinnějším možným tepelným strojem mezi dvěma zásobníky; jeho účinnost je dána pouze jejich teplotami a nezávisí na pracovní látce.
nodes.brief:
The Carnot cycle of two isothermal and two adiabatic steps is the most efficient possible heat engine between two reservoirs, its efficiency fixed by their temperatures alone and independent of the working substance.

### Concepts
- **Carnot efficiency** — The maximum efficiency 1 - T_cold/T_hot achievable between two heat reservoirs.
- **Reversible cycle** — An idealised cycle run infinitely slowly so total entropy is conserved.
- **Isothermal process** — A step at constant temperature during which heat is exchanged with a reservoir.
- **Adiabatic process** — A step with no heat exchange, so all energy change is mechanical work.
- **Carnot's theorem** — No engine between two reservoirs can exceed the efficiency of a reversible one.

nodes.name: Practical Engine & Refrigeration Cycles
nodes.en: Real engines and refrigerators approximate idealised cycles — Otto, Diesel, Brayton, Rankine and the reversed vapour-compression cycle — each trading efficiency, power and practicality for a given application.
nodes.cs: Reálné motory a chladicí zařízení aproximují idealizované cykly — Ottův, Dieselův, Braytonův, Rankinův a obrácený kompresní chladicí cyklus — z nichž každý pro danou aplikaci volí kompromis mezi účinností, výkonem a praktičností.
nodes.brief:
Real engines and refrigerators approximate idealised cycles — Otto, Diesel, Brayton, Rankine and the reversed vapour-compression cycle — each trading efficiency, power and practicality for a given application.

### Concepts
- **Otto cycle** — The idealised cycle of a spark-ignition petrol engine.
- **Diesel cycle** — The compression-ignition cycle with heat added at constant pressure.
- **Rankine cycle** — The steam power cycle used in most thermal and nuclear power stations.
- **Brayton cycle** — The gas-turbine and jet-engine cycle of continuous compression, combustion and expansion.
- **Refrigeration cycle** — A reversed cycle that moves heat from cold to hot using input work.
- **Coefficient of performance** — The ratio of useful heating or cooling to the work input of a heat pump.

nodes.name: Phase Transitions & Equilibria
nodes.en: Changes of state and coexistence of phases: phase diagrams, latent heat, the Clausius-Clapeyron relation, and the classification and criticality of transitions.
nodes.cs: Změny skupenství a koexistence fází: fázové diagramy, latentní teplo, Clausiova–Clapeyronova rovnice a klasifikace a kritičnost přechodů.

nodes.name: Phase Diagrams & Coexistence
nodes.en: Phase diagrams map which state of matter is stable at each pressure and temperature, with coexistence lines where phases meet, a triple point where three coexist, and a critical point where liquid and gas become indistinguishable.
nodes.cs: Fázové diagramy znázorňují, které skupenství je stabilní při daném tlaku a teplotě, s koexistenčními křivkami, kde se fáze setkávají, trojným bodem, kde koexistují tři fáze, a kritickým bodem, kde se kapalina a plyn stávají nerozlišitelnými.
nodes.brief:
Phase diagrams map which state of matter is stable at each pressure and temperature, with coexistence lines where phases meet, a triple point where three coexist, and a critical point where liquid and gas become indistinguishable.

### Concepts
- **Triple point** — The unique pressure and temperature at which solid, liquid and gas coexist.
- **Critical point** — The endpoint of the liquid-gas line beyond which the two phases merge.
- **Clausius-Clapeyron relation** — The equation giving the slope of a coexistence line from latent heat and volume change.
- **Latent heat** — The heat absorbed or released during a phase change at constant temperature.
- **Gibbs phase rule** — The relation fixing the number of independent variables among coexisting phases.

nodes.name: Critical Phenomena & Order Parameters
nodes.en: Near a continuous phase transition, fluctuations grow to all scales and thermodynamic quantities follow universal power laws set by critical exponents, independent of microscopic detail — the domain of universality classes.
nodes.cs: V blízkosti spojitého fázového přechodu narůstají fluktuace na všech škálách a termodynamické veličiny se řídí univerzálními mocninnými zákony určenými kritickými exponenty, nezávislými na mikroskopických detailech — jde o oblast tříd univerzality.
nodes.brief:
Near a continuous phase transition, fluctuations grow to all scales and thermodynamic quantities follow universal power laws set by critical exponents, independent of microscopic detail — the domain of universality classes.

### Concepts
- **Order parameter** — A quantity that is zero in one phase and nonzero in the other, marking the transition.
- **Critical exponents** — The universal powers describing how quantities diverge or vanish near the critical point.
- **Universality** — The independence of critical behaviour from microscopic details, set by symmetry and dimension.
- **First- vs second-order** — Transitions with a latent heat and jump versus continuous ones with diverging susceptibility.
- **Correlation length** — The distance over which fluctuations are correlated, diverging at the critical point.

nodes.name: Statistical Mechanics
nodes.en: The microscopic foundation of thermodynamics: how the statistics of many particles gives rise to macroscopic laws through ensembles, the partition function and entropy as counting.
nodes.cs: Mikroskopický základ termodynamiky: jak statistika mnoha částic vede k makroskopickým zákonům pomocí souborů, partiční funkce a entropie jako počítání stavů.

nodes.name: Statistical Ensembles
nodes.en: Statistical mechanics computes macroscopic properties by averaging over ensembles of microstates — microcanonical at fixed energy, canonical at fixed temperature, grand canonical at fixed chemical potential — that reproduce thermodynamics.
nodes.cs: Statistická mechanika počítá makroskopické vlastnosti průměrováním přes soubory mikrostavů — mikrokanonický při dané energii, kanonický při dané teplotě, velký kanonický při daném chemickém potenciálu — které reprodukují termodynamiku.
nodes.brief:
Statistical mechanics computes macroscopic properties by averaging over ensembles of microstates — microcanonical at fixed energy, canonical at fixed temperature, grand canonical at fixed chemical potential — that reproduce thermodynamics.

### Concepts
- **Microstate** — A complete specification of the positions and momenta of all particles.
- **Microcanonical ensemble** — The set of microstates at fixed energy, volume and particle number.
- **Canonical ensemble** — The distribution of a system in thermal contact with a heat bath at fixed temperature.
- **Grand canonical ensemble** — The distribution allowing exchange of both energy and particles with a reservoir.
- **Boltzmann distribution** — The probability of a microstate falling off exponentially with its energy over kT.

nodes.name: Partition Function & Entropy
nodes.en: The partition function sums Boltzmann factors over all microstates and is the generating function from which every thermodynamic quantity follows; Boltzmann's entropy S = k ln W ties entropy to the count of accessible microstates.
nodes.cs: Partiční funkce sčítá Boltzmannovy faktory přes všechny mikrostavy a je vytvořující funkcí, z níž plyne každá termodynamická veličina; Boltzmannova entropie S = k ln W spojuje entropii s počtem dostupných mikrostavů.
nodes.brief:
The partition function sums Boltzmann factors over all microstates and is the generating function from which every thermodynamic quantity follows; Boltzmann's entropy S = k ln W ties entropy to the count of accessible microstates.

### Concepts
- **Partition function** — The sum over states weighting each by its Boltzmann factor, encoding all thermodynamics.
- **Boltzmann entropy** — Entropy as the logarithm of the number of microstates times Boltzmann's constant.
- **Free energy from Z** — The Helmholtz free energy given directly by minus kT times the log of the partition function.
- **Equipartition theorem** — Each quadratic degree of freedom carries an average energy of one half kT.
- **Fluctuations** — Statistical variations of energy and particle number, related to heat capacity and compressibility.

nodes.name: Quantum Statistics
nodes.en: Indistinguishable quantum particles obey Fermi-Dirac or Bose-Einstein statistics, explaining electron degeneracy in metals and stars, blackbody radiation, and Bose-Einstein condensation, with classical statistics as the dilute limit.
nodes.cs: Nerozlišitelné kvantové částice se řídí Fermiho–Diracovou nebo Boseho–Einsteinovou statistikou, což vysvětluje degeneraci elektronů v kovech a hvězdách, záření černého tělesa a Boseho–Einsteinovu kondenzaci, přičemž klasická statistika je jejich zředěnou limitou.
nodes.brief:
Indistinguishable quantum particles obey Fermi-Dirac or Bose-Einstein statistics, explaining electron degeneracy in metals and stars, blackbody radiation, and Bose-Einstein condensation, with classical statistics as the dilute limit.

### Concepts
- **Fermi-Dirac statistics** — The distribution of fermions obeying the Pauli exclusion principle.
- **Bose-Einstein statistics** — The distribution of bosons, which can occupy the same state without limit.
- **Degeneracy pressure** — The pressure from the exclusion principle supporting white dwarfs and neutron stars.
- **Blackbody radiation** — The thermal photon spectrum explained by quantising electromagnetic modes.
- **Bose-Einstein condensate** — A macroscopic occupation of the ground state at very low temperature.

nodes.name: Kinetic Theory & Transport
nodes.en: The dynamics of molecular motion and how it carries mass, momentum and energy: the kinetic theory of gases and the transport processes of diffusion, viscosity and conduction.
nodes.cs: Dynamika molekulárního pohybu a jak přenáší hmotu, hybnost a energii: kinetická teorie plynů a transportní děje difuze, viskozity a vedení tepla.

nodes.name: Kinetic Theory of Gases
nodes.en: Treating a gas as many colliding point particles derives pressure, temperature and the ideal-gas law from mechanics, and gives the Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution of molecular speeds and the mean free path between collisions.
nodes.cs: Pojetí plynu jako mnoha srážejících se hmotných bodů odvozuje tlak, teplotu a stavovou rovnici ideálního plynu z mechaniky a dává Maxwellovo–Boltzmannovo rozdělení rychlostí molekul a střední volnou dráhu mezi srážkami.
nodes.brief:
Treating a gas as many colliding point particles derives pressure, temperature and the ideal-gas law from mechanics, and gives the Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution of molecular speeds and the mean free path between collisions.

### Concepts
- **Ideal gas law** — The relation pV = NkT linking pressure, volume and temperature of a dilute gas.
- **Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution** — The statistical distribution of molecular speeds in a gas at equilibrium.
- **Mean free path** — The average distance a molecule travels between successive collisions.
- **Pressure from momentum** — Gas pressure as the momentum transfer of molecules striking the walls.
- **Real gas equations** — Corrections such as the van der Waals equation accounting for molecular size and attraction.

nodes.name: Transport Processes
nodes.en: Gradients of concentration, velocity or temperature drive fluxes of matter, momentum and heat — diffusion, viscosity and thermal conduction — each described by a linear transport law and a molecular transport coefficient.
nodes.cs: Gradienty koncentrace, rychlosti nebo teploty vyvolávají toky látky, hybnosti a tepla — difuzi, viskozitu a vedení tepla — z nichž každý popisuje lineární transportní zákon a molekulární transportní koeficient.
nodes.brief:
Gradients of concentration, velocity or temperature drive fluxes of matter, momentum and heat — diffusion, viscosity and thermal conduction — each described by a linear transport law and a molecular transport coefficient.

### Concepts
- **Diffusion** — Net transport of particles down a concentration gradient, described by Fick's laws.
- **Viscosity** — The transport of momentum across a velocity gradient in a flowing fluid.
- **Thermal conduction** — The transport of heat down a temperature gradient, described by Fourier's law.
- **Onsager reciprocity** — Symmetry relations among coupled transport coefficients near equilibrium.
- **Non-equilibrium thermodynamics** — The framework describing entropy production in systems with steady fluxes.

nodes.en: Electric and magnetic phenomena unified by Maxwell's equations: electrostatics, currents and circuits, magnetism, electromagnetic induction, and electromagnetic waves including light. Covers both the classical field theory and its applications in electrical technology.
nodes.cs: Elektrické a magnetické jevy sjednocené Maxwellovými rovnicemi: elektrostatika, proudy a obvody, magnetismus, elektromagnetická indukce a elektromagnetické vlny včetně světla. Zahrnuje klasickou teorii pole i její aplikace v elektrotechnice.
nodes.brief:
Electromagnetism is the physics of electric and magnetic phenomena unified by Maxwell's equations: electrostatics, steady currents and circuits, magnetism, electromagnetic induction, and the electromagnetic waves — including visible light — that these fields support. It covers both the classical field theory and its direct technological applications in electrical engineering and telecommunications.

Within [[01.01]], the requirement that Maxwell's equations hold in every inertial frame was the direct historical motivation for [[01.01.05]], which reconciled electromagnetism with the relativity of motion; at the opposite end, the quantum theory of the electromagnetic field belongs to [[01.01.02.04]], and the exchange of thermal energy between charged systems ties it to [[01.01.03]]. The same field equations describe the geomagnetic field investigated by [[01.01.09]], which treats Earth's magnetism as a large-scale electromagnetic phenomenon.

Maxwell's equations, their derivation and applications to circuits and waves are documented at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/emcon.html) and the [Feynman Lectures on electromagnetism](https://www.feynmanlectures.caltech.edu/II_toc.html).

nodes.name: Electrostatics
nodes.en: The physics of stationary electric charge: Coulomb's law, the electric field and potential, Gauss's law, and the behaviour of conductors and capacitors.
nodes.cs: Fyzika nehybného elektrického náboje: Coulombův zákon, elektrické pole a potenciál, Gaussův zákon a chování vodičů a kondenzátorů.

nodes.name: Coulomb's Law & Electric Force
nodes.en: The force between point charges is proportional to their product and inversely proportional to the square of their separation, directed along the line joining them; this inverse-square law is the foundation of electrostatics.
nodes.cs: Síla mezi bodovými náboji je úměrná jejich součinu a nepřímo úměrná druhé mocnině jejich vzdálenosti a míří podél jejich spojnice; tento zákon převrácené druhé mocniny je základem elektrostatiky.
nodes.brief:
The force between point charges is proportional to their product and inversely proportional to the square of their separation, directed along the line joining them; this inverse-square law is the foundation of electrostatics.

### Concepts
- **Electric charge** — The conserved property of matter that is the source of electromagnetic force, quantised in units of e.
- **Coulomb's law** — The inverse-square force law between two point charges.
- **Superposition** — The net force from many charges as the vector sum of individual Coulomb forces.
- **Permittivity** — The constant characterising how a medium reduces the electrostatic force within it.

nodes.name: Electric Field & Potential
nodes.en: The electric field assigns a force-per-charge vector to every point in space, and the electric potential its energy-per-charge; the field is the gradient of the potential, and field lines and equipotentials visualise both.
nodes.cs: Elektrické pole přiřazuje každému bodu prostoru vektor síly na jednotku náboje a elektrický potenciál energii na jednotku náboje; pole je gradientem potenciálu a siločáry a ekvipotenciály obojí znázorňují.
nodes.brief:
The electric field assigns a force-per-charge vector to every point in space, and the electric potential its energy-per-charge; the field is the gradient of the potential, and field lines and equipotentials visualise both.

### Concepts
- **Electric field** — The force per unit charge experienced by a test charge at a point.
- **Electric potential** — The potential energy per unit charge, measured in volts.
- **Field-potential relation** — The field equals minus the spatial gradient of the potential.
- **Dipole** — A pair of equal and opposite charges whose field falls off faster than a single charge.
- **Equipotential surface** — A surface of constant potential, everywhere perpendicular to the field.

nodes.name: Gauss's Law
nodes.en: The electric flux through any closed surface equals the enclosed charge divided by the permittivity; this restatement of the inverse-square law exploits symmetry to compute fields of charged spheres, lines and planes.
nodes.cs: Elektrický tok libovolnou uzavřenou plochou se rovná uzavřenému náboji dělenému permitivitou; toto přeformulování zákona převrácené druhé mocniny využívá symetrie k výpočtu polí nabitých koulí, přímek a rovin.
nodes.brief:
The electric flux through any closed surface equals the enclosed charge divided by the permittivity; this restatement of the inverse-square law exploits symmetry to compute fields of charged spheres, lines and planes.

### Concepts
- **Electric flux** — The amount of electric field passing through a surface.
- **Gauss's law** — The relation between the flux through a closed surface and the charge it encloses.
- **Gaussian surface** — An imaginary closed surface chosen to exploit symmetry in field calculations.
- **Divergence form** — The differential statement that the field's divergence equals charge density over permittivity.

nodes.name: Conductors & Capacitance
nodes.en: In a conductor charge redistributes until the interior field vanishes and the surface is equipotential; capacitance measures the charge stored per unit voltage between conductors, storing energy in the electric field.
nodes.cs: Ve vodiči se náboj přerozdělí, dokud vnitřní pole nezmizí a povrch není ekvipotenciální; kapacita udává náboj uložený na jednotku napětí mezi vodiči a ukládá energii v elektrickém poli.
nodes.brief:
In a conductor charge redistributes until the interior field vanishes and the surface is equipotential; capacitance measures the charge stored per unit voltage between conductors, storing energy in the electric field.

### Concepts
- **Electrostatic shielding** — The cancellation of interior fields by a conductor, the basis of the Faraday cage.
- **Capacitance** — The ratio of stored charge to the voltage between two conductors.
- **Capacitor** — A device of two conductors storing energy in the field between them.
- **Field energy density** — The energy stored per unit volume in an electric field, proportional to its square.
- **Dielectric in a capacitor** — An insulating filling that raises capacitance by reducing the internal field.

nodes.name: Magnetostatics
nodes.en: The physics of steady currents and their magnetic fields: the Biot-Savart and Ampère laws, magnetic force, and the magnetic properties of matter.
nodes.cs: Fyzika ustálených proudů a jejich magnetických polí: Biotův–Savartův a Ampérův zákon, magnetická síla a magnetické vlastnosti látek.

nodes.name: Magnetic Field & Force
nodes.en: Moving charges and currents create magnetic fields and feel forces in them: the Lorentz force acts perpendicular to both velocity and field, curving charged trajectories and driving the operation of motors.
nodes.cs: Pohybující se náboje a proudy vytvářejí magnetické pole a působí na ně v něm síla: Lorentzova síla míří kolmo k rychlosti i poli, zakřivuje dráhy nabitých částic a pohání motory.
nodes.brief:
Moving charges and currents create magnetic fields and feel forces in them: the Lorentz force acts perpendicular to both velocity and field, curving charged trajectories and driving the operation of motors.

### Concepts
- **Lorentz force** — The force qv×B on a charge moving through a magnetic field, plus the electric part.
- **Magnetic field** — The vector field produced by currents and felt by moving charges, denoted B.
- **Cyclotron motion** — The circular path of a charged particle in a uniform magnetic field.
- **Force on a current** — The force on a current-carrying wire in a field, the basis of the electric motor.
- **Magnetic dipole moment** — The measure of a current loop's strength as a source of magnetic field.

nodes.name: Ampère's & Biot-Savart Laws
nodes.en: The Biot-Savart law gives the field of an arbitrary current element, while Ampère's law relates the field circulating around a loop to the enclosed current, providing a symmetry shortcut for fields of wires, solenoids and toroids.
nodes.cs: Biotův–Savartův zákon dává pole libovolného proudového elementu, zatímco Ampérův zákon spojuje pole cirkulující kolem smyčky s uzavřeným proudem a poskytuje symetrickou zkratku pro pole vodičů, solenoidů a toroidů.
nodes.brief:
The Biot-Savart law gives the field of an arbitrary current element, while Ampère's law relates the field circulating around a loop to the enclosed current, providing a symmetry shortcut for fields of wires, solenoids and toroids.

### Concepts
- **Biot-Savart law** — The field contribution of an infinitesimal current element.
- **Ampère's law** — The relation between the field's circulation around a loop and the enclosed current.
- **Solenoid** — A coil producing a nearly uniform field along its axis.
- **Magnetic flux** — The amount of magnetic field passing through a surface.
- **No magnetic monopoles** — The law that magnetic field lines have no sources or sinks, so flux through any closed surface is zero.

nodes.name: Magnetism in Matter
nodes.en: Materials respond to fields through the alignment of atomic magnetic moments, classified as diamagnetic, paramagnetic and ferromagnetic, the last retaining magnetisation below the Curie temperature and underlying permanent magnets.
nodes.cs: Látky reagují na pole uspořádáním atomárních magnetických momentů a dělí se na diamagnetické, paramagnetické a feromagnetické, přičemž poslední si pod Curieovou teplotou udržují magnetizaci a tvoří základ permanentních magnetů.
nodes.brief:
Materials respond to fields through the alignment of atomic magnetic moments, classified as diamagnetic, paramagnetic and ferromagnetic, the last retaining magnetisation below the Curie temperature and underlying permanent magnets.

### Concepts
- **Diamagnetism** — Weak repulsion from a field, present in all matter, from induced opposing moments.
- **Paramagnetism** — Weak attraction from the partial alignment of permanent atomic moments.
- **Ferromagnetism** — Strong, retained magnetisation from spontaneous alignment in magnetic domains.
- **Curie temperature** — The temperature above which ferromagnetic order is lost to thermal agitation.
- **Hysteresis** — The lag of magnetisation behind the applied field, storing energy in magnetic memory.

nodes.name: Electrodynamics
nodes.en: Time-varying fields and their unification: electromagnetic induction, the displacement current, and Maxwell's equations as the complete classical field theory.
nodes.cs: Časově proměnná pole a jejich sjednocení: elektromagnetická indukce, posuvný proud a Maxwellovy rovnice jako úplná klasická teorie pole.

nodes.name: Electromagnetic Induction
nodes.en: A changing magnetic flux through a circuit induces an electromotive force, with the induced current opposing the change; Faraday's law of induction and Lenz's rule govern generators, transformers and inductors.
nodes.cs: Měnící se magnetický tok obvodem indukuje elektromotorické napětí, přičemž indukovaný proud se změně brání; Faradayův zákon indukce a Lenzovo pravidlo řídí generátory, transformátory a cívky.
nodes.brief:
A changing magnetic flux through a circuit induces an electromotive force, with the induced current opposing the change; Faraday's law of induction and Lenz's rule govern generators, transformers and inductors.

### Concepts
- **Faraday's law** — The induced electromotive force equals minus the rate of change of magnetic flux.
- **Lenz's law** — The induced current flows so as to oppose the flux change that causes it.
- **Inductance** — The property relating induced voltage to the rate of change of current.
- **Transformer** — A device coupling two coils by shared flux to change alternating voltage.
- **Eddy currents** — Circulating currents induced in bulk conductors by changing fields.

nodes.name: Maxwell's Equations
nodes.en: Four equations — Gauss's laws for electricity and magnetism, Faraday's law, and the Ampère-Maxwell law with its displacement current — unify all classical electromagnetism and predict self-propagating electromagnetic waves.
nodes.cs: Čtyři rovnice — Gaussovy zákony pro elektřinu a magnetismus, Faradayův zákon a Ampérův–Maxwellův zákon s posuvným proudem — sjednocují celý klasický elektromagnetismus a předpovídají samovolně se šířící elektromagnetické vlny.
nodes.brief:
Four equations — Gauss's laws for electricity and magnetism, Faraday's law, and the Ampère-Maxwell law with its displacement current — unify all classical electromagnetism and predict self-propagating electromagnetic waves.

### Concepts
- **Displacement current** — Maxwell's term letting a changing electric field act as a source of magnetic field.
- **Field unification** — The demonstration that electric and magnetic fields are aspects of one electromagnetic field.
- **Continuity equation** — Charge conservation implied by the structure of Maxwell's equations.
- **Electromagnetic potentials** — The scalar and vector potentials from which the fields are derived, with gauge freedom.
- **Wave prediction** — The result that the equations admit waves travelling at the speed of light.

nodes.name: Electromagnetic Waves & Optics
nodes.en: The propagation of electromagnetic fields as waves: the electromagnetic spectrum, energy and momentum transport, and the optics of reflection, refraction and interference.
nodes.cs: Šíření elektromagnetického pole ve formě vln: elektromagnetické spektrum, přenos energie a hybnosti a optika odrazu, lomu a interference.

nodes.name: Electromagnetic Waves & Spectrum
nodes.en: Oscillating electric and magnetic fields propagate as transverse waves at the speed of light, spanning a continuous spectrum from radio to gamma rays and carrying energy and momentum through the Poynting vector and radiation pressure.
nodes.cs: Kmitající elektrické a magnetické pole se šíří jako příčné vlny rychlostí světla, pokrývají spojité spektrum od rádiových vln po gama záření a přenášejí energii a hybnost prostřednictvím Poyntingova vektoru a tlaku záření.
nodes.brief:
Oscillating electric and magnetic fields propagate as transverse waves at the speed of light, spanning a continuous spectrum from radio to gamma rays and carrying energy and momentum through the Poynting vector and radiation pressure.

### Concepts
- **Speed of light** — The propagation speed of electromagnetic waves in vacuum, a universal constant.
- **Electromagnetic spectrum** — The full range of wavelengths from radio through visible light to gamma rays.
- **Poynting vector** — The directional energy flux carried by an electromagnetic wave.
- **Polarization** — The orientation of the wave's electric-field oscillation, linear, circular or elliptical.
- **Radiation pressure** — The momentum transferred by electromagnetic waves onto surfaces they strike.

nodes.name: Reflection, Refraction & Interference
nodes.en: At boundaries light reflects and refracts according to the laws of reflection and Snell's law, while its wave nature produces interference and diffraction; these govern lenses, mirrors, gratings and imaging.
nodes.cs: Na rozhraních se světlo odráží a láme podle zákona odrazu a Snellova zákona, zatímco jeho vlnová povaha vytváří interferenci a difrakci; tyto jevy řídí čočky, zrcadla, mřížky a zobrazování.
nodes.brief:
At boundaries light reflects and refracts according to the laws of reflection and Snell's law, while its wave nature produces interference and diffraction; these govern lenses, mirrors, gratings and imaging.

### Concepts
- **Snell's law** — The relation between angles of incidence and refraction at a boundary via refractive indices.
- **Total internal reflection** — Complete reflection above a critical angle, the basis of optical fibres.
- **Interference** — The superposition of waves producing bright and dark fringes.
- **Diffraction** — The bending and spreading of waves around obstacles and through apertures.
- **Dispersion** — The variation of refractive index with wavelength, splitting white light into colours.

nodes.name: Electromagnetism in Matter
nodes.en: How materials respond to fields: polarization and dielectrics, magnetization and magnetic materials, conduction, and the resulting macroscopic field equations.
nodes.cs: Jak látky reagují na pole: polarizace a dielektrika, magnetizace a magnetické materiály, vedení proudu a výsledné makroskopické rovnice pole.

nodes.name: Dielectrics & Polarization
nodes.en: An electric field polarises a dielectric by displacing bound charge, weakening the internal field and defining the permittivity; the displacement field D separates free from bound charge in the macroscopic equations.
nodes.cs: Elektrické pole polarizuje dielektrikum posunutím vázaného náboje, čímž oslabuje vnitřní pole a definuje permitivitu; pole elektrické indukce D odděluje volný náboj od vázaného v makroskopických rovnicích.
nodes.brief:
An electric field polarises a dielectric by displacing bound charge, weakening the internal field and defining the permittivity; the displacement field D separates free from bound charge in the macroscopic equations.

### Concepts
- **Polarization** — The dipole moment per unit volume induced in a dielectric by a field.
- **Electric displacement** — The field D combining the applied field and the medium's polarization.
- **Relative permittivity** — The factor by which a dielectric reduces the field, also called the dielectric constant.
- **Bound charge** — The effective surface and volume charge arising from aligned dipoles.
- **Dielectric breakdown** — The field strength at which an insulator becomes conducting.

nodes.name: Circuits & Electromagnetic Technology
nodes.en: The engineering physics of circuits and devices: Ohm's and Kirchhoff's laws, alternating current, and the electromagnetic principles behind motors, generators and antennas.
nodes.cs: Technická fyzika obvodů a zařízení: Ohmův a Kirchhoffovy zákony, střídavý proud a elektromagnetické principy motorů, generátorů a antén.

nodes.name: Circuit Laws & AC Analysis
nodes.en: Ohm's law relates voltage, current and resistance, Kirchhoff's laws enforce charge and energy conservation at nodes and loops, and alternating-current analysis uses impedance and phasors to handle capacitors and inductors.
nodes.cs: Ohmův zákon spojuje napětí, proud a odpor, Kirchhoffovy zákony zajišťují zachování náboje a energie v uzlech a smyčkách a analýza střídavého proudu používá impedanci a fázory k popisu kondenzátorů a cívek.
nodes.brief:
Ohm's law relates voltage, current and resistance, Kirchhoff's laws enforce charge and energy conservation at nodes and loops, and alternating-current analysis uses impedance and phasors to handle capacitors and inductors.

### Concepts
- **Ohm's law** — The proportionality of current to voltage through a resistance.
- **Kirchhoff's laws** — Conservation of current at nodes and of voltage around loops.
- **Impedance** — The generalisation of resistance to alternating-current circuits with reactance.
- **Resonance** — The frequency at which an inductor and capacitor exchange energy with minimal impedance.
- **Power in circuits** — The rate of energy transfer, with real and reactive parts in AC systems.

nodes.name: Motors, Generators & Antennas
nodes.en: Electromagnetic induction and the force on currents underlie machines that convert between electrical and mechanical energy — motors and generators — while accelerating charges in antennas radiate the electromagnetic waves of communication.
nodes.cs: Elektromagnetická indukce a síla na proudy tvoří základ strojů přeměňujících elektrickou a mechanickou energii — motorů a generátorů — zatímco zrychlované náboje v anténách vyzařují elektromagnetické vlny pro komunikaci.
nodes.brief:
Electromagnetic induction and the force on currents underlie machines that convert between electrical and mechanical energy — motors and generators — while accelerating charges in antennas radiate the electromagnetic waves of communication.

### Concepts
- **Electric motor** — A machine converting electrical energy to rotation via force on current loops.
- **Electric generator** — A machine converting mechanical motion to electricity by induction.
- **Antenna** — A conductor that radiates or receives electromagnetic waves from accelerating charge.
- **Waveguide** — A structure that confines and directs electromagnetic waves along its length.
- **Electromagnetic radiation from acceleration** — The principle that only accelerating charges radiate energy as waves.

nodes.en: Einstein's theories of space, time and gravity: special relativity (invariance of the speed of light, time dilation, length contraction, mass–energy equivalence) and general relativity (curved spacetime, black holes, gravitational waves, cosmological models).
nodes.cs: Einsteinovy teorie prostoru, času a gravitace: speciální relativita (invariance rychlosti světla, dilatace času, kontrakce délek, ekvivalence hmotnosti a energie) a obecná relativita (zakřivený prostoročas, černé díry, gravitační vlny, kosmologické modely).
nodes.brief:
Relativity is Einstein's physics of space, time and gravity, in two parts. Special relativity establishes the invariance of the speed of light and its consequences — time dilation, length contraction and mass–energy equivalence — for observers in uniform motion; general relativity extends this to gravitation itself, describing it as curved spacetime and predicting black holes, gravitational waves and the large-scale evolution of the universe.

Within [[01.01]], special relativity grew directly out of [[01.01.04]]'s requirement that Maxwell's equations hold in every inertial frame, and it revises the absolute space and time assumed by [[01.01.01]] once speeds approach that of light. General relativity's predictions are the working physics of [[01.01.08]], where black holes, gravitational lensing and cosmological expansion are observed and modelled, and its curved-spacetime description of strong gravity complements [[01.01.06]]'s account of the nuclear forces that bind matter at the opposite, subatomic extreme.

Special and general relativity are introduced with worked derivations at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/relativ.html) and in the [Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy's entry on the philosophy of general relativity](https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/spacetime-theories/).

nodes.name: Special Relativity
nodes.en: Einstein's 1905 theory of space and time in inertial frames: the constancy of the speed of light, the relativity of simultaneity, time dilation and length contraction, and the Lorentz transformation.
nodes.cs: Einsteinova teorie prostoru a času z roku 1905 v inerciálních soustavách: stálost rychlosti světla, relativita současnosti, dilatace času a kontrakce délek a Lorentzova transformace.

nodes.name: Postulates & Relativity of Simultaneity
nodes.en: Special relativity rests on two postulates — the laws of physics are the same in all inertial frames and the speed of light is invariant — from which simultaneity becomes relative: events simultaneous in one frame need not be in another.
nodes.cs: Speciální teorie relativity stojí na dvou postulátech — fyzikální zákony jsou stejné ve všech inerciálních soustavách a rychlost světla je invariantní — z nichž plyne relativita současnosti: události současné v jedné soustavě nemusí být současné v jiné.
nodes.brief:
Special relativity rests on two postulates — the laws of physics are the same in all inertial frames and the speed of light is invariant — from which simultaneity becomes relative: events simultaneous in one frame need not be in another.

### Concepts
- **Principle of relativity** — The laws of physics take the same form in every inertial frame.
- **Invariance of light speed** — The speed of light in vacuum is the same for all observers regardless of motion.
- **Inertial frame** — A reference frame in which a free body moves in a straight line at constant speed.
- **Relativity of simultaneity** — Whether two separated events are simultaneous depends on the observer's motion.

nodes.name: Time Dilation & Length Contraction
nodes.en: Moving clocks run slow and moving rods contract along their motion, by the Lorentz factor; these reciprocal effects preserve the invariant speed of light and are confirmed by decaying particles and precision clocks.
nodes.cs: Pohybující se hodiny jdou pomaleji a pohybující se tyče se ve směru pohybu zkracují o Lorentzův faktor; tyto vzájemné jevy zachovávají invariantní rychlost světla a potvrzují je rozpadající se částice a přesné hodiny.
nodes.brief:
Moving clocks run slow and moving rods contract along their motion, by the Lorentz factor; these reciprocal effects preserve the invariant speed of light and are confirmed by decaying particles and precision clocks.

### Concepts
- **Time dilation** — The slowing of a moving clock as seen from a frame in which it moves.
- **Length contraction** — The shortening of a moving object along its direction of motion.
- **Lorentz factor** — The factor gamma quantifying dilation and contraction, growing without bound near light speed.
- **Proper time** — The time measured by a clock carried along a worldline, an invariant.
- **Twin paradox** — The asymmetric ageing of a travelling twin, resolved by the acceleration of the return.

nodes.name: Lorentz Transformation & Velocity Addition
nodes.en: The Lorentz transformation mixes space and time coordinates between inertial frames, replacing the Galilean transformation; it yields the relativistic velocity-addition rule that keeps the speed of light as an unreachable limit.
nodes.cs: Lorentzova transformace mísí prostorové a časové souřadnice mezi inerciálními soustavami a nahrazuje Galileovu transformaci; dává relativistické pravidlo skládání rychlostí, které udržuje rychlost světla jako nedosažitelnou mez.
nodes.brief:
The Lorentz transformation mixes space and time coordinates between inertial frames, replacing the Galilean transformation; it yields the relativistic velocity-addition rule that keeps the speed of light as an unreachable limit.

### Concepts
- **Lorentz transformation** — The linear transformation of coordinates between inertial frames preserving the light speed.
- **Velocity addition** — The rule combining velocities so their sum never exceeds the speed of light.
- **Relativistic Doppler effect** — The frequency shift of light from relative motion, including a transverse component.
- **Invariant interval** — The spacetime separation of events, agreed on by all inertial observers.

nodes.name: Relativistic Dynamics
nodes.en: Mechanics at high speed: relativistic momentum and energy, the mass-energy equivalence, four-vectors, and the union of space and time into Minkowski spacetime.
nodes.cs: Mechanika při vysokých rychlostech: relativistická hybnost a energie, ekvivalence hmoty a energie, čtyřvektory a sjednocení prostoru a času do Minkowského časoprostoru.

nodes.name: Mass-Energy Equivalence
nodes.en: Energy and mass are equivalent, related by E = mc squared, so a body's rest energy is enormous and any energy has inertia; this underlies nuclear binding energy, particle creation and the energetics of stars.
nodes.cs: Energie a hmota jsou ekvivalentní, spojené vztahem E = mc na druhou, takže klidová energie tělesa je obrovská a každá energie má setrvačnost; to je základem jaderné vazebné energie, vzniku částic a energetiky hvězd.
nodes.brief:
Energy and mass are equivalent, related by E = mc squared, so a body's rest energy is enormous and any energy has inertia; this underlies nuclear binding energy, particle creation and the energetics of stars.

### Concepts
- **Rest energy** — The energy mc squared a body possesses by virtue of its mass alone.
- **Relativistic momentum** — Momentum that grows faster than velocity and diverges near light speed.
- **Energy-momentum relation** — The invariant relation E squared = (pc) squared + (mc squared) squared.
