- **Massless particles** — Particles such as photons that carry energy and momentum but no rest mass and move at light speed.
- **Binding energy** — The mass deficit released when bound systems form, powering nuclear reactions.

nodes.name: Minkowski Spacetime & Four-Vectors
nodes.en: Space and time unite into four-dimensional Minkowski spacetime with an indefinite metric; physical quantities become four-vectors and tensors whose invariant magnitudes all observers agree on, making the laws manifestly frame-independent.
nodes.cs: Prostor a čas se spojují do čtyřrozměrného Minkowského časoprostoru s indefinitní metrikou; fyzikální veličiny se stávají čtyřvektory a tenzory, na jejichž invariantních velikostech se shodnou všichni pozorovatelé, čímž jsou zákony zjevně nezávislé na soustavě.
nodes.brief:
Space and time unite into four-dimensional Minkowski spacetime with an indefinite metric; physical quantities become four-vectors and tensors whose invariant magnitudes all observers agree on, making the laws manifestly frame-independent.

### Concepts
- **Minkowski spacetime** — The flat four-dimensional continuum of special relativity with a light-cone structure.
- **Worldline** — The path of a particle through spacetime.
- **Light cone** — The surface separating causally connected events from those that cannot interact.
- **Four-vector** — A four-component quantity transforming under the Lorentz transformation, such as four-momentum.
- **Causality** — The ordering of cause and effect preserved because no signal outpaces light.

nodes.name: General Relativity
nodes.en: Einstein's geometric theory of gravity: the equivalence principle, curved spacetime, geodesic motion, and the field equations relating curvature to energy and momentum.
nodes.cs: Einsteinova geometrická teorie gravitace: princip ekvivalence, zakřivený časoprostor, pohyb po geodetikách a rovnice pole spojující křivost s energií a hybností.

nodes.name: Equivalence Principle
nodes.en: Gravitational and inertial mass are identical, so a freely falling observer feels no gravity and a uniform acceleration is locally indistinguishable from a gravitational field; this equivalence is the seed of general relativity.
nodes.cs: Gravitační a setrvačná hmotnost jsou totožné, takže volně padající pozorovatel gravitaci necítí a rovnoměrné zrychlení je lokálně nerozlišitelné od gravitačního pole; tato ekvivalence je zárodkem obecné teorie relativity.
nodes.brief:
Gravitational and inertial mass are identical, so a freely falling observer feels no gravity and a uniform acceleration is locally indistinguishable from a gravitational field; this equivalence is the seed of general relativity.

### Concepts
- **Equivalence principle** — The local equivalence of a gravitational field and an accelerating frame.
- **Free fall** — Motion under gravity alone, in which an observer feels weightless.
- **Gravitational time dilation** — The slowing of clocks deeper in a gravitational potential.
- **Gravitational redshift** — The lengthening of a photon's wavelength as it climbs out of a gravity well.

nodes.name: Curved Spacetime & Field Equations
nodes.en: Gravity is the curvature of spacetime produced by energy and momentum; matter tells spacetime how to curve through Einstein's field equations, and spacetime tells matter how to move along geodesics.
nodes.cs: Gravitace je zakřivení časoprostoru způsobené energií a hybností; hmota určuje, jak se má časoprostor zakřivit, prostřednictvím Einsteinových rovnic pole, a časoprostor určuje hmotě, jak se má pohybovat po geodetikách.
nodes.brief:
Gravity is the curvature of spacetime produced by energy and momentum; matter tells spacetime how to curve through Einstein's field equations, and spacetime tells matter how to move along geodesics.

### Concepts
- **Spacetime curvature** — The geometric deviation from flatness that manifests as gravity.
- **Geodesic** — The straightest possible path in curved spacetime, followed by free-falling bodies.
- **Einstein field equations** — The equations relating spacetime curvature to the energy-momentum of matter.
- **Metric tensor** — The field encoding distances and times, the dynamical variable of general relativity.
- **Cosmological constant** — A uniform energy of space driving accelerated expansion, added to the field equations.

nodes.name: Gravitation & Spacetime Solutions
nodes.en: Solutions of the field equations and their physics: black holes, gravitational waves, and the exact geometries that describe stars, collapsed objects and the expanding universe.
nodes.cs: Řešení rovnic pole a jejich fyzika: černé díry, gravitační vlny a přesné geometrie popisující hvězdy, zhroucené objekty a rozpínající se vesmír.

nodes.name: Black Holes
nodes.en: When gravity overwhelms all support, matter collapses to a region from which not even light escapes, bounded by an event horizon and characterised by mass, charge and spin; observations confirm them at stellar and galactic scales.
nodes.cs: Když gravitace přemůže veškerou oporu, hmota se zhroutí do oblasti, z níž neunikne ani světlo, ohraničené horizontem událostí a charakterizované hmotností, nábojem a rotací; pozorování je potvrzují na hvězdných i galaktických škálách.
nodes.brief:
When gravity overwhelms all support, matter collapses to a region from which not even light escapes, bounded by an event horizon and characterised by mass, charge and spin; observations confirm them at stellar and galactic scales.

### Concepts
- **Event horizon** — The boundary beyond which no signal can escape a black hole.
- **Schwarzschild solution** — The exact spacetime of a non-rotating spherical mass.
- **Singularity** — The region of formally infinite curvature at a black hole's centre, where the theory breaks down.
- **Kerr solution** — The spacetime of a rotating black hole, dragging spacetime around it.
- **Hawking radiation** — The predicted thermal emission that lets black holes slowly evaporate.

nodes.name: Gravitational Waves
nodes.en: Accelerating masses radiate ripples of spacetime curvature travelling at the speed of light; first detected in 2015 from merging black holes, they open a new observational window on the most violent events in the universe.
nodes.cs: Zrychlující se hmoty vyzařují vlnění zakřivení časoprostoru šířící se rychlostí světla; poprvé detekované v roce 2015 ze splývajících černých děr otevírají nové pozorovací okno na nejprudší děje ve vesmíru.
nodes.brief:
Accelerating masses radiate ripples of spacetime curvature travelling at the speed of light; first detected in 2015 from merging black holes, they open a new observational window on the most violent events in the universe.

### Concepts
- **Gravitational wave** — A propagating oscillation of spacetime curvature emitted by accelerating masses.
- **Binary inspiral** — The spiralling merger of two compact objects, a strong wave source.
- **Interferometric detection** — The measurement of tiny length changes by laser interferometers such as LIGO.
- **Multi-messenger astronomy** — The combined observation of gravitational waves and electromagnetic or particle signals.

nodes.name: Tests & Applications of Relativity
nodes.en: The experimental confirmation and practical use of relativity, from Mercury's perihelion and light bending to gravitational-wave detection and the relativistic corrections in satellite navigation.
nodes.cs: Experimentální potvrzení a praktické využití relativity, od stáčení perihelia Merkuru a ohybu světla po detekci gravitačních vln a relativistické korekce v družicové navigaci.

nodes.name: Classical Tests of Relativity
nodes.en: General relativity was confirmed by the anomalous precession of Mercury's perihelion, the bending of starlight by the Sun, and the gravitational redshift of light, and continues to pass ever more precise experimental tests.
nodes.cs: Obecná teorie relativity byla potvrzena anomálním stáčením perihelia Merkuru, ohybem hvězdného světla Sluncem a gravitačním rudým posuvem světla a nadále obstává ve stále přesnějších experimentálních testech.
nodes.brief:
General relativity was confirmed by the anomalous precession of Mercury's perihelion, the bending of starlight by the Sun, and the gravitational redshift of light, and continues to pass ever more precise experimental tests.

### Concepts
- **Perihelion precession** — The excess rotation of Mercury's orbit explained by general relativity.
- **Light deflection** — The bending of light by mass, first measured during a solar eclipse.
- **Shapiro delay** — The extra travel time of light passing near a massive body.
- **Frame dragging** — The twisting of spacetime by a rotating mass, measured by orbiting gyroscopes.

nodes.name: Relativity in Navigation & Technology
nodes.en: Satellite navigation must correct for both special-relativistic time dilation from orbital speed and general-relativistic speed-up from weaker gravity, without which position errors would accumulate at kilometres per day.
nodes.cs: Družicová navigace musí korigovat jak speciálně-relativistickou dilataci času z orbitální rychlosti, tak obecně-relativistické zrychlení hodin ze slabší gravitace, bez čehož by chyby polohy narůstaly o kilometry za den.
nodes.brief:
Satellite navigation must correct for both special-relativistic time dilation from orbital speed and general-relativistic speed-up from weaker gravity, without which position errors would accumulate at kilometres per day.

### Concepts
- **GPS time correction** — The net relativistic clock adjustment applied to navigation satellites.
- **Orbital time dilation** — The special-relativistic slowing of a fast-moving satellite clock.
- **Altitude clock speed-up** — The general-relativistic quickening of a clock in weaker gravity.
- **Precision timekeeping** — The use of relativity in atomic clocks and geodesy at sub-nanosecond accuracy.

nodes.en: The atomic nucleus: its structure and binding energy, radioactivity and decay chains, fission and fusion, and applications from reactors to nuclear medicine and radiometric dating. Distinct from Particle Physics, which studies the elementary constituents below the nuclear level.
nodes.cs: Atomové jádro: jeho struktura a vazebná energie, radioaktivita a rozpadové řady, štěpení a fúze a aplikace od reaktorů po nukleární medicínu a radiometrické datování. Liší se od částicové fyziky, která zkoumá elementární složky pod úrovní jádra.
nodes.brief:
Nuclear Physics is the study of the atomic nucleus: its structure and binding energy, the radioactivity and decay chains by which unstable nuclei transform, and the fission and fusion reactions that release nuclear binding energy. Its applications range from power reactors and weapons to nuclear medicine and radiometric dating of rocks and archaeological material.

Within [[01.01]], the nucleus's structure and stability are governed by the quantum-mechanical forces described in more fundamental detail by [[01.01.02]] and by [[01.01.07]], which studies the quarks and gluons the nucleus is built from; Nuclear Physics itself works one level up, treating protons and neutrons as the relevant degrees of freedom. Fusion and heavy-element formation inside stars connect it to [[01.01.08]], and its medical applications share ground with [[01.01.10]]'s use of quantitative physical methods on living tissue.

Nuclear structure, decay processes and reactor physics are covered at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/nuclear/nucon.html) and by the [IAEA's nuclear data and physics resources](https://www.iaea.org/resources/databases).

nodes.name: Nuclear Structure
nodes.en: The composition and organisation of the atomic nucleus: protons and neutrons, binding energy, nuclear size and shape, and the models that explain nuclear stability.
nodes.cs: Složení a uspořádání atomového jádra: protony a neutrony, vazebná energie, velikost a tvar jádra a modely vysvětlující jadernou stabilitu.

nodes.name: Nucleons & Binding Energy
nodes.en: The nucleus is bound protons and neutrons held by the strong nuclear force; the binding energy per nucleon peaks near iron, explaining why fusion of light nuclei and fission of heavy ones both release energy.
nodes.cs: Jádro tvoří vázané protony a neutrony držené silnou jadernou silou; vazebná energie na nukleon dosahuje maxima poblíž železa, což vysvětluje, proč fúze lehkých jader i štěpení těžkých uvolňují energii.
nodes.brief:
The nucleus is bound protons and neutrons held by the strong nuclear force; the binding energy per nucleon peaks near iron, explaining why fusion of light nuclei and fission of heavy ones both release energy.

### Concepts
- **Nucleon** — A proton or neutron, the constituents of the nucleus.
- **Strong nuclear force** — The short-range attraction binding nucleons against electrostatic repulsion.
- **Binding energy** — The energy needed to disassemble a nucleus, reflected in a mass deficit.
- **Mass defect** — The difference between a nucleus's mass and the sum of its free nucleons.
- **Binding-energy curve** — The variation of binding energy per nucleon with mass number, peaking at iron.

nodes.name: Nuclear Models
nodes.en: Complementary models capture nuclear behaviour: the liquid-drop model explains binding and fission energetics, while the shell model explains magic numbers and spins through nucleons filling quantised energy levels.
nodes.cs: Doplňující se modely popisují chování jádra: kapkový model vysvětluje vazebnou energii a energetiku štěpení, zatímco slupkový model vysvětluje magická čísla a spiny pomocí nukleonů zaplňujících kvantované energetické hladiny.
nodes.brief:
Complementary models capture nuclear behaviour: the liquid-drop model explains binding and fission energetics, while the shell model explains magic numbers and spins through nucleons filling quantised energy levels.

### Concepts
- **Liquid-drop model** — A model treating the nucleus as an incompressible charged drop.
- **Semi-empirical mass formula** — The formula estimating binding energy from volume, surface, Coulomb and other terms.
- **Shell model** — A model of nucleons in quantised orbitals explaining stability patterns.
- **Magic numbers** — Nucleon counts giving closed shells and exceptional stability.
- **Nuclear spin** — The total angular momentum of a nucleus, important in magnetic resonance.

nodes.name: Radioactivity & Decay
nodes.en: The spontaneous transformation of unstable nuclei: alpha, beta and gamma decay, decay laws and half-lives, decay chains, and the applications of radioactive dating and tracing.
nodes.cs: Samovolná přeměna nestabilních jader: alfa, beta a gama rozpad, zákony rozpadu a poločasy, rozpadové řady a využití radioaktivního datování a značkování.

nodes.name: Modes of Radioactive Decay
nodes.en: Unstable nuclei shed energy by emitting an alpha particle, converting a neutron and proton through beta decay, or releasing a gamma photon; each mode changes the nucleus in a characteristic way governed by conservation laws.
nodes.cs: Nestabilní jádra se zbavují energie vyzářením částice alfa, přeměnou neutronu a protonu při beta rozpadu nebo uvolněním fotonu gama; každý způsob mění jádro charakteristickým způsobem daným zákony zachování.
nodes.brief:
Unstable nuclei shed energy by emitting an alpha particle, converting a neutron and proton through beta decay, or releasing a gamma photon; each mode changes the nucleus in a characteristic way governed by conservation laws.

### Concepts
- **Alpha decay** — Emission of a helium nucleus, reducing mass number by four.
- **Beta decay** — Conversion of a neutron to a proton or vice versa, emitting an electron or positron and a neutrino.
- **Gamma decay** — Emission of a high-energy photon as an excited nucleus relaxes.
- **Electron capture** — Absorption of an inner electron converting a proton to a neutron.
- **Q-value** — The energy released in a decay, shared as kinetic energy of the products.

nodes.name: Decay Law, Half-Life & Dating
nodes.en: Radioactive decay is a random process with a fixed probability per unit time, giving exponential decay and a characteristic half-life; the steady clock this provides underlies radiometric dating and radioactive tracing.
nodes.cs: Radioaktivní rozpad je náhodný děj s pevnou pravděpodobností za jednotku času, což vede k exponenciálnímu poklesu a charakteristickému poločasu rozpadu; tyto stálé hodiny jsou základem radiometrického datování a radioaktivního značkování.
nodes.brief:
Radioactive decay is a random process with a fixed probability per unit time, giving exponential decay and a characteristic half-life; the steady clock this provides underlies radiometric dating and radioactive tracing.

### Concepts
- **Half-life** — The time for half the nuclei in a sample to decay.
- **Exponential decay law** — The rule that activity falls off exponentially with time.
- **Activity** — The number of decays per unit time in a radioactive sample.
- **Decay chain** — A sequence of decays leading from an unstable parent to a stable end nucleus.
- **Radiometric dating** — Determining age from the ratio of parent to daughter isotopes, as in carbon-14 dating.

nodes.name: Nuclear Reactions
nodes.en: Induced transformations of nuclei: reaction kinematics and cross sections, conservation laws, and the mechanisms of scattering, capture and transmutation.
nodes.cs: Vynucené přeměny jader: kinematika reakcí a účinné průřezy, zákony zachování a mechanismy rozptylu, záchytu a transmutace.

nodes.name: Reactions & Cross Sections
nodes.en: In a nuclear reaction incident particles transform a target nucleus, conserving nucleon number, charge, energy and momentum; the cross section quantifies reaction probability, peaking sharply at resonances.
nodes.cs: Při jaderné reakci dopadající částice přeměňují terčové jádro za zachování počtu nukleonů, náboje, energie a hybnosti; účinný průřez udává pravděpodobnost reakce a dosahuje ostrých maxim při rezonancích.
nodes.brief:
In a nuclear reaction incident particles transform a target nucleus, conserving nucleon number, charge, energy and momentum; the cross section quantifies reaction probability, peaking sharply at resonances.

### Concepts
- **Cross section** — The effective target area measuring the probability of a nuclear reaction.
- **Reaction kinematics** — The application of energy and momentum conservation to reaction products.
- **Compound nucleus** — A short-lived excited nucleus formed and then decaying during a reaction.
- **Resonance** — A sharp peak in cross section when the energy matches a nuclear excited state.
- **Transmutation** — The conversion of one element into another by a nuclear reaction.

nodes.name: Fission, Fusion & Energy
nodes.en: The two routes to nuclear energy: the splitting of heavy nuclei and the merging of light ones, the physics of chain reactions and confinement, and their use in reactors and weapons.
nodes.cs: Dvě cesty k jaderné energii: štěpení těžkých jader a slučování lehkých, fyzika řetězových reakcí a udržení a jejich využití v reaktorech a zbraních.

nodes.name: Nuclear Fission
nodes.en: A heavy nucleus splits into lighter fragments, releasing energy and free neutrons that can sustain a chain reaction; controlled in reactors by moderators and control rods, uncontrolled it drives fission weapons.
nodes.cs: Těžké jádro se rozštěpí na lehčí fragmenty, přičemž uvolní energii a volné neutrony schopné udržet řetězovou reakci; v reaktorech se řídí moderátory a regulačními tyčemi, neřízené pohání štěpné zbraně.
nodes.brief:
A heavy nucleus splits into lighter fragments, releasing energy and free neutrons that can sustain a chain reaction; controlled in reactors by moderators and control rods, uncontrolled it drives fission weapons.

### Concepts
- **Induced fission** — Splitting of a heavy nucleus triggered by neutron absorption.
- **Chain reaction** — A self-sustaining sequence in which fission neutrons induce further fissions.
- **Critical mass** — The minimum amount of fissile material needed to sustain a chain reaction.
- **Moderator** — A material slowing neutrons to increase the fission probability in a reactor.
- **Fission products** — The radioactive fragments and neutrons produced by fission.

nodes.name: Nuclear Fusion
nodes.en: Light nuclei merge into a heavier one, releasing large energy per nucleon but requiring immense temperature and pressure to overcome their electrostatic repulsion; fusion powers the stars and is pursued for terrestrial energy.
nodes.cs: Lehká jádra se slučují do těžšího a uvolňují velkou energii na nukleon, avšak vyžadují obrovskou teplotu a tlak k překonání elektrostatického odpuzování; fúze pohání hvězdy a usiluje se o ni jako o pozemský zdroj energie.
nodes.brief:
Light nuclei merge into a heavier one, releasing large energy per nucleon but requiring immense temperature and pressure to overcome their electrostatic repulsion; fusion powers the stars and is pursued for terrestrial energy.

### Concepts
- **Fusion reaction** — The merging of light nuclei into a heavier one with energy release.
- **Coulomb barrier** — The electrostatic repulsion that fusing nuclei must overcome.
- **Thermonuclear ignition** — The condition where fusion becomes self-sustaining through its own heat.
- **Magnetic confinement** — Holding hot plasma with magnetic fields, as in a tokamak.
- **Inertial confinement** — Compressing fuel by lasers or beams to reach fusion conditions.

nodes.name: Applied & Astrophysical Nuclear Physics
nodes.en: Nuclear physics in practice and in the cosmos: nuclear medicine and radiation detection, radiation protection, and the nucleosynthesis that forges the elements in stars.
nodes.cs: Jaderná fyzika v praxi a ve vesmíru: nukleární medicína a detekce záření, radiační ochrana a nukleosyntéza, která ve hvězdách vytváří prvky.

nodes.name: Nuclear Medicine & Radiation Detection
nodes.en: Radioactive isotopes and radiation are harnessed for imaging and therapy and detected by instruments that convert ionisation into measurable signals; radiation protection limits exposure through shielding, distance and time.
nodes.cs: Radioaktivní izotopy a záření se využívají k zobrazování a léčbě a detekují je přístroje, které převádějí ionizaci na měřitelný signál; radiační ochrana omezuje ozáření pomocí stínění, vzdálenosti a času.
nodes.brief:
Radioactive isotopes and radiation are harnessed for imaging and therapy and detected by instruments that convert ionisation into measurable signals; radiation protection limits exposure through shielding, distance and time.

### Concepts
- **Radiotracer** — A radioactive label following a substance through a body or system.
- **Positron emission tomography** — Imaging that reconstructs positron-emitter distribution in the body.
- **Radiotherapy** — The use of ionising radiation to destroy diseased tissue.
- **Geiger counter** — A detector registering individual ionising events as pulses.
- **Radiation protection** — Limiting dose through shielding, distance and exposure time.

nodes.name: Nucleosynthesis
nodes.en: The chemical elements are forged by nuclear reactions: the lightest in the first minutes of the Big Bang, heavier ones by fusion in stellar cores, and the heaviest by rapid neutron capture in supernovae and neutron-star mergers.
nodes.cs: Chemické prvky vznikají jadernými reakcemi: nejlehčí v prvních minutách velkého třesku, těžší fúzí v jádrech hvězd a nejtěžší rychlým záchytem neutronů v supernovách a při splývání neutronových hvězd.
nodes.brief:
The chemical elements are forged by nuclear reactions: the lightest in the first minutes of the Big Bang, heavier ones by fusion in stellar cores, and the heaviest by rapid neutron capture in supernovae and neutron-star mergers.

### Concepts
- **Big Bang nucleosynthesis** — The formation of hydrogen, helium and lithium in the early universe.
- **Stellar nucleosynthesis** — The building of elements up to iron by fusion in stars.
- **s-process** — Slow neutron capture building heavy nuclei in evolved stars.
- **r-process** — Rapid neutron capture forming the heaviest elements in explosive events.
- **Cosmic abundance** — The observed proportions of the elements set by these processes.

nodes.en: Elementary particles and their interactions: the quarks, leptons and bosons of the Standard Model, symmetries and conservation laws, accelerators and detectors, and open questions such as dark matter and physics beyond the Standard Model.
nodes.cs: Elementární částice a jejich interakce: kvarky, leptony a bosony standardního modelu, symetrie a zákony zachování, urychlovače a detektory a otevřené otázky jako temná hmota a fyzika za standardním modelem.
nodes.brief:
Particle Physics studies the elementary constituents of matter and their interactions: the quarks, leptons and gauge bosons of the Standard Model, the symmetries and conservation laws that constrain their behaviour, and the accelerators and detectors used to probe them. It also frames the field's open questions — dark matter, the matter–antimatter asymmetry, and physics beyond the Standard Model.

Within [[01.01]], Particle Physics sits below [[01.01.06]] in scale: nuclear physics treats protons and neutrons as building blocks, while particle physics resolves them further into quarks bound by the strong force. Its formal apparatus is [[01.01.02.02]], the quantum field theory that unifies special relativity with quantum mechanics, and its unresolved questions about dark matter directly motivate [[01.01.08]]'s cosmological and astrophysical searches for it.

The Standard Model, its particle content and the experimental programs testing it are documented by [CERN](https://home.cern/science/physics/standard-model) and at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/Particles/particles.html).

nodes.name: The Standard Model
nodes.en: The theory of elementary particles and their interactions: the quarks and leptons of matter, the gauge bosons carrying forces, and the Higgs boson that gives them mass.
nodes.cs: Teorie elementárních částic a jejich interakcí: kvarky a leptony tvořící hmotu, kalibrační bosony přenášející síly a Higgsův boson, který jim dodává hmotnost.

nodes.name: Quarks & Leptons
nodes.en: Matter is built from two families of spin-half fermions: six quarks, which combine into protons, neutrons and other hadrons, and six leptons, including the electron and its neutrino, arranged in three generations of increasing mass.
nodes.cs: Hmota je tvořena dvěma rodinami fermionů se spinem jedna polovina: šesti kvarky, které se skládají do protonů, neutronů a dalších hadronů, a šesti leptony, mezi něž patří elektron a jeho neutrino, uspořádanými do tří generací s rostoucí hmotností.
nodes.brief:
Matter is built from two families of spin-half fermions: six quarks, which combine into protons, neutrons and other hadrons, and six leptons, including the electron and its neutrino, arranged in three generations of increasing mass.

### Concepts
- **Quark** — A fermion carrying colour charge that combines to form hadrons, never seen in isolation.
- **Lepton** — A fermion without colour charge, such as the electron, muon, tau and their neutrinos.
- **Generations** — The three families of quarks and leptons, differing mainly in mass.
- **Hadron** — A composite particle of quarks bound by the strong force, either a baryon or a meson.
- **Antiparticle** — A partner with opposite charges to a given particle, produced together with it.

nodes.name: Gauge Bosons & the Higgs
nodes.en: Forces are carried by spin-one gauge bosons — the photon, the gluons and the W and Z — while the spin-zero Higgs boson, discovered in 2012, arises from the field whose interaction endows particles with mass.
nodes.cs: Síly přenášejí kalibrační bosony se spinem jedna — foton, gluony a bosony W a Z — zatímco Higgsův boson se spinem nula, objevený v roce 2012, pochází z pole, jehož interakce dodává částicím hmotnost.
nodes.brief:
Forces are carried by spin-one gauge bosons — the photon, the gluons and the W and Z — while the spin-zero Higgs boson, discovered in 2012, arises from the field whose interaction endows particles with mass.

### Concepts
- **Photon** — The massless carrier of the electromagnetic force.
- **Gluon** — The carrier of the strong force, itself colour-charged.
- **W and Z bosons** — The massive carriers of the weak force.
- **Higgs boson** — The excitation of the Higgs field, confirmed in 2012.
- **Higgs mechanism** — The process giving mass to the weak bosons and fermions through symmetry breaking.

nodes.name: Fundamental Interactions
nodes.en: The forces between particles as gauge field theories: quantum electrodynamics, the strong interaction and quantum chromodynamics, and the weak interaction with electroweak unification.
nodes.cs: Síly mezi částicemi jako kalibrační teorie pole: kvantová elektrodynamika, silná interakce a kvantová chromodynamika a slabá interakce s elektroslabým sjednocením.

nodes.name: Quantum Electrodynamics & Chromodynamics
nodes.en: Quantum electrodynamics describes the electromagnetic interaction of charged particles by photon exchange to extraordinary precision, while quantum chromodynamics describes the strong force among quarks and gluons, with confinement and asymptotic freedom.
nodes.cs: Kvantová elektrodynamika popisuje elektromagnetickou interakci nabitých částic výměnou fotonů s mimořádnou přesností, zatímco kvantová chromodynamika popisuje silnou sílu mezi kvarky a gluony s uvězněním a asymptotickou volností.
nodes.brief:
Quantum electrodynamics describes the electromagnetic interaction of charged particles by photon exchange to extraordinary precision, while quantum chromodynamics describes the strong force among quarks and gluons, with confinement and asymptotic freedom.

### Concepts
- **Quantum electrodynamics** — The quantum field theory of light and charged matter, tested to extreme precision.
- **Feynman diagram** — A pictorial term in the perturbative expansion of a scattering amplitude.
- **Colour charge** — The strong-force analogue of electric charge, carried by quarks and gluons.
- **Confinement** — The property that colour-charged particles cannot exist in isolation.
- **Asymptotic freedom** — The weakening of the strong force at short distances and high energies.

nodes.name: Weak Interaction & Electroweak Unification
nodes.en: The weak interaction changes particle flavour and causes beta decay through massive W and Z bosons; at high energy it merges with electromagnetism into a single electroweak force, unified in the Standard Model.
nodes.cs: Slabá interakce mění vůni částic a způsobuje beta rozpad prostřednictvím hmotných bosonů W a Z; při vysokých energiích splývá s elektromagnetismem do jediné elektroslabé síly sjednocené ve standardním modelu.
nodes.brief:
The weak interaction changes particle flavour and causes beta decay through massive W and Z bosons; at high energy it merges with electromagnetism into a single electroweak force, unified in the Standard Model.

### Concepts
- **Weak interaction** — The short-range force responsible for flavour change and beta decay.
- **Flavour change** — The conversion of one quark or lepton type into another via the weak force.
- **Electroweak unification** — The theory merging electromagnetism and the weak force at high energy.
- **Neutrino interaction** — The exclusively weak coupling that makes neutrinos so penetrating.
- **Parity violation** — The weak force's distinction between left- and right-handed particles.

nodes.name: Symmetries & Conservation Laws
nodes.en: The symmetry principles organising particle physics: conservation laws from continuous symmetries, the discrete symmetries C, P and T, and the spontaneous symmetry breaking behind mass generation.
nodes.cs: Principy symetrie uspořádávající částicovou fyziku: zákony zachování ze spojitých symetrií, diskrétní symetrie C, P a T a spontánní narušení symetrie za vznikem hmotnosti.

nodes.name: Symmetries & Conservation
nodes.en: Every continuous symmetry implies a conserved quantity by Noether's theorem, so energy, momentum and charge conservation reflect symmetries of space, time and gauge; particle physics is organised around such symmetry principles.
nodes.cs: Každá spojitá symetrie implikuje zachovávanou veličinu podle Noetherové věty, takže zachování energie, hybnosti a náboje odráží symetrie prostoru, času a kalibrace; částicová fyzika je uspořádána kolem takových principů symetrie.
nodes.brief:
Every continuous symmetry implies a conserved quantity by Noether's theorem, so energy, momentum and charge conservation reflect symmetries of space, time and gauge; particle physics is organised around such symmetry principles.

### Concepts
- **Noether's theorem** — The correspondence between continuous symmetries and conservation laws.
- **Gauge symmetry** — The local symmetry underlying each fundamental interaction.
- **Conserved quantum numbers** — Quantities such as baryon and lepton number preserved in interactions.
- **Isospin** — An approximate symmetry treating protons and neutrons as two states of one particle.
- **Spontaneous symmetry breaking** — A symmetric theory with an asymmetric ground state, central to mass generation.

nodes.name: Discrete Symmetries & CP Violation
nodes.en: The discrete operations of charge conjugation, parity and time reversal are individually violated by the weak force; their combination CPT is exact, and CP violation is essential to explaining why the universe contains more matter than antimatter.
nodes.cs: Diskrétní operace nábojového sdružení, parity a obrácení času jsou jednotlivě narušovány slabou silou; jejich kombinace CPT je přesná a narušení CP je klíčové pro vysvětlení, proč vesmír obsahuje více hmoty než antihmoty.
nodes.brief:
The discrete operations of charge conjugation, parity and time reversal are individually violated by the weak force; their combination CPT is exact, and CP violation is essential to explaining why the universe contains more matter than antimatter.

### Concepts
- **Parity** — The symmetry under spatial reflection, violated by the weak interaction.
- **Charge conjugation** — The operation exchanging particles with their antiparticles.
- **CP violation** — The small asymmetry between matter and antimatter behaviour.
- **CPT theorem** — The exact combined symmetry required by relativistic quantum field theory.
- **Baryon asymmetry** — The cosmic excess of matter over antimatter that CP violation helps explain.

nodes.name: Experimental Particle Physics
nodes.en: How particles are produced and observed: accelerators and colliders, detector technologies, and the statistical analysis by which discoveries are established.
nodes.cs: Jak se částice vytvářejí a pozorují: urychlovače a srážeče, detekční technologie a statistická analýza, kterou se prokazují objevy.

nodes.name: Accelerators & Colliders
nodes.en: Particle accelerators raise charged particles to high energy with electric and magnetic fields and collide them to create new particles from energy; colliders such as the Large Hadron Collider probe the smallest scales.
nodes.cs: Urychlovače částic zvyšují energii nabitých částic pomocí elektrických a magnetických polí a srážejí je, aby z energie vznikaly nové částice; srážeče, jako je Velký hadronový srážeč, zkoumají nejmenší škály.
nodes.brief:
Particle accelerators raise charged particles to high energy with electric and magnetic fields and collide them to create new particles from energy; colliders such as the Large Hadron Collider probe the smallest scales.

### Concepts
- **Particle accelerator** — A machine boosting charged particles to high energy using electromagnetic fields.
- **Collider** — An accelerator crossing two beams to maximise collision energy.
- **Centre-of-mass energy** — The energy available in a collision to create new particles.
- **Synchrotron** — A circular accelerator with fields ramped in step with rising particle energy.
- **Luminosity** — The measure of collision rate determining how often rare processes occur.

nodes.name: Detectors & Discovery
nodes.en: Layered detectors track charged particles, measure their momentum and energy, and identify them by their signatures; a discovery is claimed only when a signal exceeds background at the stringent five-sigma statistical level.
nodes.cs: Vrstvené detektory sledují nabité částice, měří jejich hybnost a energii a identifikují je podle jejich signatur; objev se prohlašuje teprve tehdy, když signál překročí pozadí na přísné statistické úrovni pěti sigma.
nodes.brief:
Layered detectors track charged particles, measure their momentum and energy, and identify them by their signatures; a discovery is claimed only when a signal exceeds background at the stringent five-sigma statistical level.

### Concepts
- **Tracking detector** — A device recording the paths of charged particles in a magnetic field.
- **Calorimeter** — A detector measuring particle energy by absorbing it in dense material.
- **Particle identification** — Distinguishing particle types from their tracks and energy deposits.
- **Five-sigma criterion** — The statistical threshold conventionally required to claim a discovery.
- **Background rejection** — The removal of known processes to isolate a sought-after signal.

nodes.name: Beyond the Standard Model
nodes.en: The open questions and proposed extensions: neutrino mass and oscillation, the identity of dark matter, supersymmetry and grand unification, and the matter-antimatter asymmetry of the universe.
nodes.cs: Otevřené otázky a navrhovaná rozšíření: hmotnost a oscilace neutrin, povaha temné hmoty, supersymetrie a velké sjednocení a asymetrie hmoty a antihmoty ve vesmíru.

nodes.name: Neutrino Physics
nodes.en: Neutrinos oscillate between flavours as they travel, proving they have tiny nonzero masses unexplained by the Standard Model; their nature, mass ordering and possible role in matter-antimatter asymmetry are active frontiers.
nodes.cs: Neutrina během letu oscilují mezi vůněmi, což dokazuje, že mají nepatrné nenulové hmotnosti, jež standardní model nevysvětluje; jejich povaha, uspořádání hmotností a možná role v asymetrii hmoty a antihmoty jsou aktivní hranice výzkumu.
nodes.brief:
Neutrinos oscillate between flavours as they travel, proving they have tiny nonzero masses unexplained by the Standard Model; their nature, mass ordering and possible role in matter-antimatter asymmetry are active frontiers.

### Concepts
- **Neutrino oscillation** — The periodic change of neutrino flavour in flight, implying nonzero mass.
- **Mass hierarchy** — The unknown ordering of the three neutrino mass states.
- **Majorana neutrino** — The open possibility that the neutrino is its own antiparticle.
- **Sterile neutrino** — A hypothesised neutrino interacting only through gravity and mixing.
- **Neutrinoless double beta decay** — A sought-after rare process that would reveal a Majorana nature.

nodes.name: Dark Matter, Supersymmetry & Unification
nodes.en: The Standard Model omits gravity, dark matter and the origin of its parameters; proposed extensions include supersymmetry, grand unified theories merging the forces, and candidate dark-matter particles, none yet confirmed.
nodes.cs: Standardní model opomíjí gravitaci, temnou hmotu a původ svých parametrů; navrhovaná rozšíření zahrnují supersymetrii, teorie velkého sjednocení slučující síly a kandidátní částice temné hmoty, žádné však dosud nebylo potvrzeno.
nodes.brief:
The Standard Model omits gravity, dark matter and the origin of its parameters; proposed extensions include supersymmetry, grand unified theories merging the forces, and candidate dark-matter particles, none yet confirmed.

### Concepts
- **Dark matter** — The unseen mass whose gravity shapes galaxies, of unknown particle identity.
- **Supersymmetry** — A proposed symmetry pairing fermions with bosons, so far unobserved.
- **Grand unified theory** — A theory merging the strong, weak and electromagnetic forces at high energy.
- **Hierarchy problem** — The puzzle of why the Higgs mass is so far below the unification scale.
- **Proton decay** — A predicted rare decay of the proton in many unified theories, not yet seen.

nodes.en: Physical processes in stars, galaxies and the universe at large: stellar structure and evolution, compact objects, interstellar matter, cosmology and high-energy phenomena. Applies physics to celestial objects; observational sky-mapping and instrumentation sit under the Astronomy branch.
nodes.cs: Fyzikální procesy ve hvězdách, galaxiích a vesmíru jako celku: stavba a vývoj hvězd, kompaktní objekty, mezihvězdná hmota, kosmologie a vysokoenergetické jevy. Aplikuje fyziku na vesmírné objekty; pozorovací mapování oblohy a přístrojová technika patří do větve Astronomie.
nodes.brief:
Astrophysics applies the laws of physics to stars, galaxies and the universe at large: stellar structure and evolution, compact objects such as neutron stars and black holes, the interstellar medium, cosmology, and high-energy phenomena from supernovae to gamma-ray bursts. It is the physical explanation of celestial objects, distinct from the observational sky-mapping and instrumentation that belong to Astronomy.

Within [[01.01]], Astrophysics draws on [[01.01.05]]'s general relativity to model black holes and the expanding universe, on [[01.01.06]]'s fusion and decay processes to explain stellar energy generation and the nucleosynthesis of elements, and on [[01.01.07]]'s Standard Model physics to interpret dark matter and high-energy cosmic events. It sits beside [[01.05]], the sibling branch of Natural Sciences that catalogues and observes the same objects Astrophysics explains physically.

Stellar evolution, compact objects and cosmology are surveyed at [NASA's astrophysics portal](https://science.nasa.gov/astrophysics/) and at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/astro/astcon.html).

nodes.name: Stellar Structure & Evolution
nodes.en: The physics of stars: hydrostatic and thermal balance, energy generation by fusion, the main sequence, and the evolution of stars from birth to their final states.
nodes.cs: Fyzika hvězd: hydrostatická a tepelná rovnováha, uvolňování energie fúzí, hlavní posloupnost a vývoj hvězd od zrození po jejich konečné stavy.

nodes.name: Stellar Structure & Energy Generation
nodes.en: A star is a self-gravitating ball of plasma in hydrostatic equilibrium, its inward gravity balanced by outward pressure sustained by nuclear fusion in the core; energy flows outward by radiation and convection to the surface.
nodes.cs: Hvězda je samogravitující koule plazmatu v hydrostatické rovnováze, jejíž dovnitř mířící gravitaci vyvažuje ven mířící tlak udržovaný jadernou fúzí v jádru; energie proudí ven zářením a konvekcí k povrchu.
nodes.brief:
A star is a self-gravitating ball of plasma in hydrostatic equilibrium, its inward gravity balanced by outward pressure sustained by nuclear fusion in the core; energy flows outward by radiation and convection to the surface.

### Concepts
- **Hydrostatic equilibrium** — The balance between gravity and pressure that keeps a star stable.
- **Proton-proton chain** — The dominant hydrogen-fusion sequence powering low-mass stars.
- **CNO cycle** — A catalytic hydrogen-fusion cycle dominant in more massive stars.
- **Radiative and convective transport** — The two ways energy moves from a star's core to its surface.
- **Luminosity** — The total power a star radiates, set by its mass and structure.

nodes.name: Stellar Evolution
nodes.en: Stars evolve as they exhaust their nuclear fuel, moving off the main sequence to become giants and shedding or exploding their outer layers; the mass of a star determines its lifetime and its final fate.
nodes.cs: Hvězdy se vyvíjejí, jak spotřebovávají jaderné palivo, opouštějí hlavní posloupnost, stávají se obry a odvrhávají nebo vybuchují své vnější vrstvy; hmotnost hvězdy určuje její délku života i konečný osud.
nodes.brief:
Stars evolve as they exhaust their nuclear fuel, moving off the main sequence to become giants and shedding or exploding their outer layers; the mass of a star determines its lifetime and its final fate.

### Concepts
- **Main sequence** — The long stable phase of core hydrogen fusion where most stars spend their lives.
- **Hertzsprung-Russell diagram** — The plot of luminosity against temperature mapping stellar populations.
- **Red giant phase** — The swelling of a star after core hydrogen is exhausted.
- **Planetary nebula** — The ejected envelope of a dying low-mass star, leaving a white dwarf.
- **Mass-lifetime relation** — The rule that more massive stars burn brighter and die far sooner.

nodes.name: Compact Objects
nodes.en: The dense remnants of stellar death: white dwarfs, neutron stars and stellar black holes, supported against gravity by degeneracy pressure or not at all.
nodes.cs: Husté pozůstatky zániku hvězd: bílí trpaslíci, neutronové hvězdy a hvězdné černé díry, držené proti gravitaci degeneračním tlakem, nebo vůbec ne.

nodes.name: White Dwarfs & Neutron Stars
nodes.en: White dwarfs and neutron stars are the collapsed cores of dead stars, held up by electron and neutron degeneracy pressure respectively; each has a maximum mass beyond which collapse continues, the Chandrasekhar and Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff limits.
nodes.cs: Bílí trpaslíci a neutronové hvězdy jsou zhroucená jádra mrtvých hvězd, držená degeneračním tlakem elektronů, respektive neutronů; každý má maximální hmotnost, za níž kolaps pokračuje — Chandrasekharovu a Tolmanovu–Oppenheimerovu–Volkoffovu mez.
nodes.brief:
White dwarfs and neutron stars are the collapsed cores of dead stars, held up by electron and neutron degeneracy pressure respectively; each has a maximum mass beyond which collapse continues, the Chandrasekhar and Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff limits.

### Concepts
- **Degeneracy pressure** — The quantum pressure from the exclusion principle supporting compact stars.
- **Chandrasekhar limit** — The maximum mass of a white dwarf, about 1.4 solar masses.
- **Neutron star** — An ultradense stellar remnant composed largely of neutrons.
- **Pulsar** — A rapidly rotating magnetised neutron star seen as pulses of radiation.
- **Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff limit** — The maximum mass of a neutron star before black-hole collapse.

nodes.name: Stellar Black Holes & Accretion
nodes.en: When collapse exceeds all support a stellar black hole forms; matter falling onto compact objects releases enormous energy as it forms a hot accretion disc, making X-ray binaries and quasars among the brightest sources in the sky.
nodes.cs: Když kolaps překročí veškerou oporu, vzniká hvězdná černá díra; hmota padající na kompaktní objekty uvolňuje při tvorbě horkého akrečního disku obrovskou energii, což činí z rentgenových dvojhvězd a kvasarů jedny z nejjasnějších zdrojů na obloze.
nodes.brief:
When collapse exceeds all support a stellar black hole forms; matter falling onto compact objects releases enormous energy as it forms a hot accretion disc, making X-ray binaries and quasars among the brightest sources in the sky.

### Concepts
- **Stellar black hole** — A black hole formed by the collapse of a massive star's core.
- **Accretion disc** — A disc of infalling matter heated to radiate as it spirals inward.
- **X-ray binary** — A compact object accreting from a companion star, bright in X-rays.
- **Relativistic jet** — A collimated outflow launched from near an accreting compact object.
- **Eddington limit** — The luminosity at which radiation pressure halts further accretion.

nodes.name: Interstellar Medium & Star Formation
nodes.en: The gas and dust between the stars and the collapse that turns it into new stars: molecular clouds, gravitational instability, and the physics of accretion and protostars.
nodes.cs: Plyn a prach mezi hvězdami a kolaps, který jej mění v nové hvězdy: molekulární mračna, gravitační nestabilita a fyzika akrece a protohvězd.

nodes.name: The Interstellar Medium
nodes.en: The space between stars holds gas and dust in phases from cold molecular clouds to hot ionised plasma, enriched by stellar death and cycled into new stars; its physics governs cooling, heating and the chemistry of space.
nodes.cs: Prostor mezi hvězdami obsahuje plyn a prach ve fázích od chladných molekulárních mračen po horké ionizované plazma, obohacovaný zánikem hvězd a znovu zapojovaný do nových hvězd; jeho fyzika řídí ochlazování, ohřev a chemii vesmíru.
nodes.brief:
The space between stars holds gas and dust in phases from cold molecular clouds to hot ionised plasma, enriched by stellar death and cycled into new stars; its physics governs cooling, heating and the chemistry of space.

### Concepts
- **Molecular cloud** — A cold dense region of gas where stars form.
- **Interstellar dust** — Fine solid grains that redden starlight and seed molecule formation.
- **Ionised gas** — Hot plasma around massive stars, glowing as emission nebulae.
- **Interstellar extinction** — The dimming and reddening of starlight by intervening dust.
- **Cosmic cycle of matter** — The recycling of gas from dying stars into new generations.

nodes.name: Star Formation
nodes.en: Stars are born when a molecular cloud becomes gravitationally unstable and fragments and collapses, forming protostars that accrete through discs and ignite fusion once their cores reach the required temperature.
nodes.cs: Hvězdy se rodí, když se molekulární mračno stane gravitačně nestabilním, rozpadne se a zhroutí, přičemž vznikají protohvězdy, které akreují skrze disky a zažehnou fúzi, jakmile jejich jádra dosáhnou potřebné teploty.
nodes.brief:
Stars are born when a molecular cloud becomes gravitationally unstable and fragments and collapses, forming protostars that accrete through discs and ignite fusion once their cores reach the required temperature.

### Concepts
- **Jeans instability** — The condition under which a gas cloud collapses under its own gravity.
- **Protostar** — A forming star still accreting from its birth cloud.
- **Protoplanetary disc** — The disc of gas and dust around a young star from which planets form.
- **Initial mass function** — The distribution of masses with which stars are born.
- **Bipolar outflow** — The jets of gas ejected along the poles of a forming star.

nodes.name: Galactic & Extragalactic Astrophysics
nodes.en: The structure and dynamics of galaxies and the largest systems: galactic rotation and dark matter, active galactic nuclei, and clusters and the cosmic web.
nodes.cs: Struktura a dynamika galaxií a největších soustav: rotace galaxií a temná hmota, aktivní galaktická jádra a kupy galaxií a kosmická síť.

nodes.name: Galaxy Dynamics & Dark Matter
nodes.en: Galaxies are gravitationally bound systems of stars, gas and dark matter; their flat rotation curves reveal far more mass than the visible stars provide, the primary evidence for dark matter haloes surrounding them.
nodes.cs: Galaxie jsou gravitačně vázané soustavy hvězd, plynu a temné hmoty; jejich ploché rotační křivky odhalují mnohem více hmoty, než poskytují viditelné hvězdy, což je hlavní důkaz pro halo temné hmoty, jež je obklopují.
nodes.brief:
Galaxies are gravitationally bound systems of stars, gas and dark matter; their flat rotation curves reveal far more mass than the visible stars provide, the primary evidence for dark matter haloes surrounding them.

### Concepts
- **Rotation curve** — The orbital speed of stars versus distance from a galaxy's centre.
- **Dark matter halo** — The extended invisible mass whose gravity binds a galaxy.
- **Galaxy morphology** — The classification of galaxies as spiral, elliptical or irregular.
- **Galactic dynamics** — The gravitational motion of stars and gas within a galaxy.
- **Galaxy mergers** — The collisions that reshape galaxies and trigger star formation.

nodes.name: Active Galactic Nuclei & Large-Scale Structure
nodes.en: A supermassive black hole accreting at a galaxy's centre powers active galactic nuclei and quasars, while galaxies themselves cluster into filaments and voids forming the cosmic web, the largest structure in the universe.
nodes.cs: Supermasivní černá díra akreující ve středu galaxie pohání aktivní galaktická jádra a kvasary, zatímco samotné galaxie se shlukují do vláken a prázdnot tvořících kosmickou síť, největší strukturu ve vesmíru.
nodes.brief:
A supermassive black hole accreting at a galaxy's centre powers active galactic nuclei and quasars, while galaxies themselves cluster into filaments and voids forming the cosmic web, the largest structure in the universe.

### Concepts
- **Supermassive black hole** — The million-to-billion-solar-mass black hole at a galaxy's core.
- **Quasar** — An extremely luminous active galactic nucleus in the distant universe.
- **Galaxy cluster** — A gravitationally bound assembly of hundreds to thousands of galaxies.
- **Cosmic web** — The filamentary large-scale arrangement of galaxies and dark matter.
- **Gravitational lensing** — The bending of light by mass, used to map dark matter and magnify distant sources.

nodes.name: High-Energy & Multi-Messenger Astrophysics
nodes.en: The most violent phenomena and the many channels that reveal them: supernovae and their remnants, cosmic rays, gamma-ray bursts, and observation through light, particles and gravitational waves.
nodes.cs: Nejprudší jevy a mnoho kanálů, jimiž se projevují: supernovy a jejich pozůstatky, kosmické záření, záblesky gama a pozorování světlem, částicemi a gravitačními vlnami.

nodes.name: Supernovae & Cosmic Rays
nodes.en: Massive stars end in supernova explosions that disperse newly forged elements and drive shock waves accelerating cosmic rays — high-energy particles that pervade the galaxy and strike the Earth's atmosphere.
nodes.cs: Hmotné hvězdy končí výbuchy supernov, které rozptylují nově vytvořené prvky a vytvářejí rázové vlny urychlující kosmické záření — vysokoenergetické částice, jež prostupují galaxií a dopadají na zemskou atmosféru.
nodes.brief:
Massive stars end in supernova explosions that disperse newly forged elements and drive shock waves accelerating cosmic rays — high-energy particles that pervade the galaxy and strike the Earth's atmosphere.

### Concepts
- **Core-collapse supernova** — The explosion of a massive star when its iron core collapses.
- **Type Ia supernova** — The thermonuclear detonation of a white dwarf, a cosmic standard candle.
- **Supernova remnant** — The expanding shocked shell left by a supernova.
- **Cosmic rays** — High-energy charged particles from space, accelerated by shocks and other sources.
- **Shock acceleration** — The mechanism boosting particles to high energy at astrophysical shock fronts.

nodes.name: Multi-Messenger Astrophysics
nodes.en: The universe is now observed through several messengers — electromagnetic radiation across the spectrum, neutrinos, cosmic rays and gravitational waves — whose combination pinpoints events such as neutron-star mergers and gamma-ray bursts.
nodes.cs: Vesmír se nyní pozoruje prostřednictvím několika poslů — elektromagnetického záření napříč spektrem, neutrin, kosmického záření a gravitačních vln — jejichž kombinace přesně určuje děje, jako je splývání neutronových hvězd a záblesky gama.
nodes.brief:
The universe is now observed through several messengers — electromagnetic radiation across the spectrum, neutrinos, cosmic rays and gravitational waves — whose combination pinpoints events such as neutron-star mergers and gamma-ray bursts.

### Concepts
- **Gamma-ray burst** — The brightest electromagnetic explosion, from collapsing stars or merging compact objects.
- **Neutrino astronomy** — Observation of astrophysical neutrinos that escape dense sources.
- **Gravitational-wave astronomy** — The detection of spacetime ripples from merging compact objects.
- **Kilonova** — The optical glow of a neutron-star merger forging heavy elements.
- **Messenger coincidence** — The joint detection of one event across multiple channels, localising its source.

nodes.en: Physics of the Earth: seismology and the planet's internal structure, geomagnetism, the gravity field, the physical mechanics of plate tectonics, and geophysical survey methods. Physical methods applied to Earth — descriptive geology belongs under Earth Sciences.
nodes.cs: Fyzika Země: seismologie a vnitřní stavba planety, geomagnetismus, tíhové pole, fyzikální mechanika deskové tektoniky a geofyzikální průzkumné metody. Fyzikální metody aplikované na Zemi — popisná geologie patří pod Vědy o Zemi.
nodes.brief:
Geophysics is the physics of the Earth: seismology and what seismic waves reveal about the planet's internal structure, geomagnetism, the gravity field, the physical mechanics of plate tectonics, and the survey methods — seismic, gravimetric, magnetic — used to probe the subsurface. It applies physical measurement and theory to the Earth, in contrast to the descriptive, materials-focused approach of geology.

Within [[01.01]], Geophysics shares its subject with [[01.04]] but not its method: Earth Sciences catalogues rocks, landforms and processes descriptively, while Geophysics measures and models them with the tools of [[01.01.04]] (electromagnetic surveying, geomagnetism) and gravitational theory drawn from [[01.01.01]]. Its seismological methods overlap directly with [[01.04.08]], which studies earthquakes and seismic waves from the Earth-science side of the same phenomena.

Geophysical methods and the structure of the Earth's interior are covered at the [USGS](https://www.usgs.gov/programs/earthquake-hazards/science/geophysics) and at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/geophys.html).

nodes.name: Seismology & Earth's Interior
nodes.en: The study of seismic waves and what they reveal about the planet's layered structure: earthquake sources, wave propagation, and the crust, mantle and core.
nodes.cs: Studium seismických vln a toho, co odhalují o vrstevnaté stavbě planety: zdroje zemětřesení, šíření vln a kůra, plášť a jádro.

nodes.name: Seismic Waves & Earthquakes
nodes.en: Earthquakes radiate elastic seismic waves — fast compressional P-waves, slower shear S-waves, and surface waves — whose travel times and paths are recorded on seismographs and used to locate sources and measure magnitude.
nodes.cs: Zemětřesení vyzařují elastické seismické vlny — rychlé podélné vlny P, pomalejší příčné vlny S a povrchové vlny — jejichž časy šíření a dráhy zaznamenávají seismografy a používají se k lokalizaci ohnisek a měření magnituda.
nodes.brief:
Earthquakes radiate elastic seismic waves — fast compressional P-waves, slower shear S-waves, and surface waves — whose travel times and paths are recorded on seismographs and used to locate sources and measure magnitude.

### Concepts
- **P-wave** — The fastest seismic wave, a compressional wave travelling through solids and liquids.
- **S-wave** — A shear wave travelling only through solids, revealing the liquid outer core by its absence.
- **Surface waves** — Slower waves confined to the surface that cause most earthquake damage.
- **Seismograph** — The instrument recording ground motion from seismic waves.
- **Magnitude** — A logarithmic measure of an earthquake's energy release, such as the moment magnitude.

nodes.name: Earth's Internal Structure
nodes.en: Seismic waves refract and reflect at internal boundaries, revealing the Earth's layers: a thin crust, a solid convecting mantle, a liquid outer core generating the magnetic field, and a solid inner core.
nodes.cs: Seismické vlny se lámou a odrážejí na vnitřních rozhraních a odhalují vrstvy Země: tenkou kůru, pevný konvektující plášť, tekuté vnější jádro generující magnetické pole a pevné vnitřní jádro.
nodes.brief:
Seismic waves refract and reflect at internal boundaries, revealing the Earth's layers: a thin crust, a solid convecting mantle, a liquid outer core generating the magnetic field, and a solid inner core.

### Concepts
- **Crust** — The thin outermost rocky layer, continental or oceanic.
- **Mantle** — The thick solid layer that convects slowly over geological time.
- **Outer core** — The liquid iron layer whose motion generates the magnetic field.
- **Inner core** — The solid iron-nickel centre of the Earth.
- **Seismic discontinuity** — A depth where wave speed changes abruptly, marking a layer boundary.

nodes.name: Geomagnetism & the Geodynamo
nodes.en: The Earth's magnetic field, its generation by convection in the liquid outer core, its variations and reversals, and the record it leaves in rocks.
nodes.cs: Magnetické pole Země, jeho vznik konvekcí v tekutém vnějším jádru, jeho proměny a přepólování a záznam, který zanechává v horninách.

nodes.name: The Geomagnetic Field & Geodynamo
nodes.en: The Earth generates a magnetic field by dynamo action in its convecting liquid outer core; the field shields the surface from the solar wind, drifts and weakens over time, and reverses polarity at irregular intervals.
nodes.cs: Země generuje magnetické pole dynamovým působením ve svém konvektujícím tekutém vnějším jádru; pole chrání povrch před slunečním větrem, s časem se posouvá a slábne a v nepravidelných intervalech přepóluje.
nodes.brief:
The Earth generates a magnetic field by dynamo action in its convecting liquid outer core; the field shields the surface from the solar wind, drifts and weakens over time, and reverses polarity at irregular intervals.

### Concepts
- **Geodynamo** — The self-sustaining generation of the magnetic field by core convection.
- **Magnetosphere** — The region of space dominated by the Earth's field, deflecting the solar wind.
- **Geomagnetic reversal** — The occasional flip of the field's north and south polarity.
- **Palaeomagnetism** — The record of past field directions frozen into rocks.
- **Secular variation** — The slow change of the field's strength and direction over decades to centuries.

nodes.name: Gravity, Geodesy & Earth's Shape
nodes.en: The Earth's gravity field and figure: geodesy and reference surfaces, gravity anomalies, isostasy, and the measurement of the planet's shape and rotation.
nodes.cs: Tíhové pole a tvar Země: geodézie a referenční plochy, tíhové anomálie, izostáze a měření tvaru a rotace planety.

nodes.name: Gravity Field & Geodesy
nodes.en: Geodesy measures the Earth's shape, orientation and gravity field; departures of gravity from a uniform ellipsoid, the anomalies, map density variations and the isostatic balance of crustal loads on the mantle.
nodes.cs: Geodézie měří tvar, orientaci a tíhové pole Země; odchylky tíže od rovnoměrného elipsoidu, tíhové anomálie, mapují změny hustoty a izostatickou rovnováhu zatížení kůry na plášti.
nodes.brief:
Geodesy measures the Earth's shape, orientation and gravity field; departures of gravity from a uniform ellipsoid, the anomalies, map density variations and the isostatic balance of crustal loads on the mantle.

### Concepts
- **Geoid** — The equipotential surface of the gravity field approximating mean sea level.
- **Reference ellipsoid** — The smooth mathematical figure approximating the Earth's shape.
- **Gravity anomaly** — The difference between measured and predicted gravity, revealing subsurface density.
- **Isostasy** — The buoyant equilibrium of crustal blocks floating on the denser mantle.
- **Satellite geodesy** — The use of satellites to measure the Earth's shape, gravity and rotation.

nodes.name: Geodynamics & Plate Tectonics
nodes.en: The physics driving the solid Earth's motion: mantle convection, plate tectonics, and the heat flow and deformation that shape the surface.
nodes.cs: Fyzika pohánějící pohyb pevné Země: konvekce v plášti, desková tektonika a tepelný tok a deformace, jež utvářejí povrch.

nodes.name: Plate Tectonics
nodes.en: The Earth's rigid outer shell is broken into plates that move over the ductile mantle, interacting at boundaries where they spread apart, collide or slide past one another, driving earthquakes, volcanism and mountain building.
nodes.cs: Tuhý vnější obal Země je rozlámán na desky, které se pohybují po tvárném plášti a vzájemně na sebe působí na rozhraních, kde se rozestupují, srážejí nebo se míjejí, čímž pohánějí zemětřesení, vulkanismus a horotvornou činnost.
nodes.brief:
The Earth's rigid outer shell is broken into plates that move over the ductile mantle, interacting at boundaries where they spread apart, collide or slide past one another, driving earthquakes, volcanism and mountain building.

### Concepts
- **Lithospheric plate** — A rigid segment of the outer Earth that moves as a unit.
- **Divergent boundary** — A boundary where plates spread apart and new crust forms.
- **Convergent boundary** — A boundary where plates collide, forming subduction zones or mountains.
- **Transform boundary** — A boundary where plates slide horizontally past each other.
- **Subduction** — The sinking of one plate beneath another into the mantle.

nodes.name: Mantle Convection & Earth's Heat
nodes.en: Heat from the planet's formation and radioactive decay drives slow convection in the solid mantle, the engine of plate motion; measured surface heat flow constrains the planet's thermal history and internal dynamics.
nodes.cs: Teplo z formování planety a z radioaktivního rozpadu pohání pomalou konvekci v pevném plášti, motor pohybu desek; měřený povrchový tepelný tok omezuje tepelnou historii planety a její vnitřní dynamiku.
nodes.brief:
Heat from the planet's formation and radioactive decay drives slow convection in the solid mantle, the engine of plate motion; measured surface heat flow constrains the planet's thermal history and internal dynamics.

### Concepts
- **Mantle convection** — The slow creeping flow of the solid mantle transporting heat outward.
- **Radiogenic heat** — Heat produced by the decay of radioactive isotopes in the Earth.
- **Heat flow** — The rate at which heat escapes through the Earth's surface.
- **Mantle plume** — An upwelling of hot mantle feeding volcanic hotspots.
- **Rheology** — The deformation behaviour of rock that lets the solid mantle flow over long times.

nodes.en: Physical principles and methods applied to living systems: molecular motors, membrane and nerve biophysics, the energetics of protein folding, and quantitative techniques such as spectroscopy, microscopy and modelling of biological processes. Physics-first, in contrast to Biochemistry's chemistry-first view of life.
nodes.cs: Fyzikální principy a metody aplikované na živé systémy: molekulární motory, biofyzika membrán a nervů, energetika sbalování proteinů a kvantitativní techniky jako spektroskopie, mikroskopie a modelování biologických procesů. Přístup vycházející z fyziky, na rozdíl od biochemie, která na život nahlíží primárně chemicky.
nodes.brief:
Biophysics applies the principles and quantitative methods of physics to living systems: the mechanics of molecular motors, the electrical and structural biophysics of membranes and nerves, the energetics of protein folding, and techniques such as spectroscopy, microscopy and computational modelling used to study biological processes. It is a physics-first view of life, asking how physical law constrains and explains biological structure and function.

Within [[01.01]], Biophysics draws on the quantum-mechanical treatment of molecular bonding from [[01.01.02]] and on the statistical-mechanical machinery of [[01.01.03]] to explain energy flow and folding in biomolecules. It sits opposite [[01.02.06]], which studies the same living molecules from a chemistry-first perspective — reaction mechanisms and metabolic pathways rather than physical forces and energetics — and it overlaps with [[01.01.06]]'s nuclear-medicine applications wherever physical methods are used diagnostically on tissue.

Biophysical methods and molecular-scale biology are introduced at [HyperPhysics](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/biology/biocon.html) and the [Biophysical Society's education resources](https://www.biophysics.org/education).

nodes.name: Molecular Biophysics
nodes.en: The physics of biological macromolecules: protein structure and folding, molecular motors, and the forces and energetics that govern molecular machines.
nodes.cs: Fyzika biologických makromolekul: struktura a sbalování bílkovin, molekulární motory a síly a energetika řídící molekulární stroje.

nodes.name: Protein Structure & Folding
nodes.en: Proteins fold from linear chains into specific three-dimensional shapes that determine their function, driven toward a free-energy minimum by hydrophobic, electrostatic and hydrogen-bonding forces; misfolding underlies several diseases.
nodes.cs: Bílkoviny se sbalují z lineárních řetězců do specifických trojrozměrných tvarů určujících jejich funkci, vedeny k minimu volné energie hydrofobními, elektrostatickými a vodíkovými vazbami; chybné sbalení je základem několika onemocnění.
nodes.brief:
Proteins fold from linear chains into specific three-dimensional shapes that determine their function, driven toward a free-energy minimum by hydrophobic, electrostatic and hydrogen-bonding forces; misfolding underlies several diseases.

### Concepts
- **Protein folding** — The process by which a polypeptide chain adopts its functional three-dimensional structure.
- **Free-energy landscape** — The funnel of conformational energies guiding a protein to its native state.
- **Hydrophobic effect** — The tendency of nonpolar groups to cluster away from water, driving folding.
- **Conformational change** — A functional shape change of a protein in response to binding or signals.
- **Misfolding** — Incorrect folding producing aggregates linked to disease.

nodes.name: Molecular Motors & Machines
nodes.en: Molecular motors convert chemical energy into directed motion and force, walking along filaments or rotating within membranes; they power muscle contraction, intracellular transport and the synthesis of the cell's energy currency.
nodes.cs: Molekulární motory přeměňují chemickou energii na řízený pohyb a sílu, kráčejí podél filament nebo rotují v membránách; pohánějí svalový stah, nitrobuněčný transport a syntézu energetické měny buňky.
nodes.brief:
Molecular motors convert chemical energy into directed motion and force, walking along filaments or rotating within membranes; they power muscle contraction, intracellular transport and the synthesis of the cell's energy currency.

### Concepts
- **Molecular motor** — A protein converting chemical energy into mechanical work.
- **ATP hydrolysis** — The energy-releasing reaction powering most molecular motors.
- **Cytoskeletal transport** — The motor-driven movement of cargo along filaments within cells.
- **Brownian ratchet** — A mechanism rectifying random thermal motion into directed movement.
- **Rotary motor** — A membrane machine such as the one synthesising the cell's energy currency by rotation.

nodes.name: Membrane & Neural Biophysics
nodes.en: The physics of cell membranes and excitable cells: ion transport and membrane potentials, the action potential, and the electrical signalling of nerves.
nodes.cs: Fyzika buněčných membrán a dráždivých buněk: transport iontů a membránové potenciály, akční potenciál a elektrická signalizace nervů.

nodes.name: Membrane Potentials & Ion Transport
nodes.en: Cell membranes maintain electrical potentials by selectively transporting ions through channels and pumps; the resulting gradients store energy and set the resting potential from which excitable cells generate signals.
nodes.cs: Buněčné membrány udržují elektrické potenciály selektivním transportem iontů skrze kanály a pumpy; vzniklé gradienty ukládají energii a určují klidový potenciál, z něhož dráždivé buňky vytvářejí signály.
nodes.brief:
Cell membranes maintain electrical potentials by selectively transporting ions through channels and pumps; the resulting gradients store energy and set the resting potential from which excitable cells generate signals.

### Concepts
- **Membrane potential** — The voltage difference across a cell membrane.
- **Ion channel** — A membrane protein allowing specific ions to flow down their gradient.
- **Ion pump** — A protein using energy to move ions against their gradient.
- **Nernst potential** — The equilibrium voltage for an ion set by its concentration ratio.
- **Resting potential** — The steady membrane voltage of an unstimulated cell.

nodes.name: The Action Potential & Nerve Signalling
nodes.en: Excitable cells fire action potentials — rapid, self-propagating reversals of membrane potential driven by voltage-gated ion channels — which carry electrical signals along nerves, as captured by the Hodgkin-Huxley model.
nodes.cs: Dráždivé buňky generují akční potenciály — rychlé, samovolně se šířící obrácení membránového potenciálu poháněná napěťově řízenými iontovými kanály — které přenášejí elektrické signály podél nervů, jak popisuje Hodgkinův–Huxleyův model.
nodes.brief:
Excitable cells fire action potentials — rapid, self-propagating reversals of membrane potential driven by voltage-gated ion channels — which carry electrical signals along nerves, as captured by the Hodgkin-Huxley model.

### Concepts
- **Action potential** — A rapid all-or-nothing reversal of membrane potential that propagates along a cell.
- **Voltage-gated channel** — An ion channel that opens in response to membrane voltage.
- **Hodgkin-Huxley model** — The quantitative model of the action potential from ionic currents.
- **Saltatory conduction** — The jumping of nerve impulses between gaps in the myelin sheath, speeding signalling.
- **Synaptic transmission** — The passing of a signal between cells at a synapse.

nodes.name: Biomechanics
nodes.en: The mechanics of living matter: the material properties of tissues, the forces in movement and locomotion, and fluid flow in circulation and respiration.
nodes.cs: Mechanika živé hmoty: materiálové vlastnosti tkání, síly při pohybu a lokomoci a proudění tekutin v oběhu a dýchání.

nodes.name: Tissue Mechanics & Locomotion
nodes.en: Biomechanics applies the mechanics of materials and forces to living tissue: bones, muscles and tendons carry loads and store elastic energy, and the physics of levers and gaits governs how organisms move.
nodes.cs: Biomechanika aplikuje mechaniku materiálů a sil na živou tkáň: kosti, svaly a šlachy nesou zatížení a ukládají pružnou energii a fyzika pák a chůze určuje, jak se organismy pohybují.
nodes.brief:
Biomechanics applies the mechanics of materials and forces to living tissue: bones, muscles and tendons carry loads and store elastic energy, and the physics of levers and gaits governs how organisms move.

### Concepts
- **Tissue elasticity** — The stress-strain response of biological materials such as bone and tendon.
- **Muscle mechanics** — The force-length and force-velocity behaviour of contracting muscle.
- **Lever systems** — The skeletal arrangements of bones and muscles acting as levers.
- **Gait** — The mechanical pattern of legged locomotion optimising energy and stability.
- **Scaling laws** — How mechanical demands change with organism size.

nodes.name: Biological Fluid Dynamics
nodes.en: The flow of biological fluids follows the physics of hydrodynamics adapted to living systems: blood circulates through branching vessels, air moves through the lungs, and microorganisms swim at low Reynolds number where viscosity dominates.
nodes.cs: Proudění biologických tekutin se řídí fyzikou hydrodynamiky přizpůsobenou živým systémům: krev obíhá větvícími se cévami, vzduch proudí plícemi a mikroorganismy plavou při nízkém Reynoldsově čísle, kde převládá viskozita.
nodes.brief:
The flow of biological fluids follows the physics of hydrodynamics adapted to living systems: blood circulates through branching vessels, air moves through the lungs, and microorganisms swim at low Reynolds number where viscosity dominates.

### Concepts
- **Haemodynamics** — The physics of blood flow through the circulatory system.
- **Poiseuille flow** — The pressure-driven laminar flow of fluid through a tube.
- **Low Reynolds number** — The viscosity-dominated regime in which microorganisms swim.
- **Respiratory flow** — The mechanics of air movement in breathing.
- **Wall shear stress** — The frictional force of flowing fluid on vessel walls, sensed by cells.

nodes.name: Biophysical Techniques
nodes.en: The physical methods that image and probe living systems: structural determination, single-molecule and optical techniques, and the physics of medical imaging.
nodes.cs: Fyzikální metody zobrazující a zkoumající živé systémy: určování struktury, jednomolekulové a optické techniky a fyzika lékařského zobrazování.

nodes.name: Structural & Single-Molecule Methods
nodes.en: Physical techniques resolve biological structure and dynamics down to single molecules: X-ray crystallography, cryo-electron microscopy and nuclear magnetic resonance yield atomic structures, while optical tweezers and single-molecule fluorescence probe individual molecules at work.
nodes.cs: Fyzikální techniky rozlišují biologickou strukturu a dynamiku až na úroveň jednotlivých molekul: rentgenová krystalografie, kryoelektronová mikroskopie a nukleární magnetická rezonance dávají atomární struktury, zatímco optická pinzeta a jednomolekulová fluorescence zkoumají jednotlivé molekuly při práci.
nodes.brief:
Physical techniques resolve biological structure and dynamics down to single molecules: X-ray crystallography, cryo-electron microscopy and nuclear magnetic resonance yield atomic structures, while optical tweezers and single-molecule fluorescence probe individual molecules at work.

### Concepts
- **X-ray crystallography** — Determining atomic structure from the diffraction of X-rays by a crystal.
- **Cryo-electron microscopy** — Imaging frozen biomolecules at near-atomic resolution by electron microscopy.
- **Nuclear magnetic resonance** — Probing molecular structure and dynamics through nuclear spin resonance.
- **Optical tweezers** — Trapping and manipulating single molecules or particles with focused light.
- **Single-molecule fluorescence** — Observing individual molecules by the light they emit.

nodes.name: Physics of Medical Imaging
nodes.en: Medical imaging applies physics to see inside the body without surgery: X-ray and computed tomography use ionising radiation, magnetic resonance imaging uses nuclear spins in magnetic fields, and ultrasound uses reflected sound waves.
nodes.cs: Lékařské zobrazování aplikuje fyziku k pohledu dovnitř těla bez operace: rentgen a výpočetní tomografie využívají ionizující záření, magnetická rezonance využívá jaderné spiny v magnetickém poli a ultrazvuk využívá odražené zvukové vlny.
nodes.brief:
Medical imaging applies physics to see inside the body without surgery: X-ray and computed tomography use ionising radiation, magnetic resonance imaging uses nuclear spins in magnetic fields, and ultrasound uses reflected sound waves.

### Concepts
- **Computed tomography** — Reconstruction of cross-sectional images from many X-ray projections.
- **Magnetic resonance imaging** — Imaging based on the nuclear magnetic resonance of tissue protons.
- **Ultrasound imaging** — Imaging from the echoes of high-frequency sound waves.
- **Contrast mechanism** — The physical property that distinguishes tissues in an image.
- **Spatial resolution** — The finest detail an imaging method can resolve.

nodes.name: Quantum Gravity & Unification
nodes.en: Approaches to a quantum theory of gravity and the unification of general relativity with quantum field theory — 19 research pillars from string theory and loop quantum gravity to holography, causal sets and the swampland.
nodes.cs: Přístupy ke kvantové teorii gravitace a ke sjednocení obecné relativity s kvantovou teorií pole — 19 výzkumných pilířů od teorie strun a smyčkové kvantové gravitace přes holografii a kauzální množiny až po swampland.

nodes.name: Asymptotic Safety
