- **BMV gravity-induced entanglement witness** — Bose et al. and Marletto-Vedral (2017): two ~10^-14 kg masses each in spatial superposition, coupled only via gravity, become entangled only if gravity is non-classical, because local operations and classical communication cannot create entanglement. Observing entanglement would witness the quantum nature of (linearized) gravity. Challenged in 2025-26 by claims that classical gravity can also enta
- **Classical-gravity-entanglement debate (2025-26)** — Aziz-Howl (Nature 2025) claim classical gravitational interaction can transmit quantum information and generate entanglement by physically local processes, undermining BMV as a clean quantum-gravity test. Marletto-Oppenheim-Vedral-Wilson (2025) and Gundhi et al. counter that the model either produces no entanglement (discarded amplitudes) or mediates it via quantized matter, not gravity. Unresolve

nodes.name: Classical Stochastic Gravity & Spacetime Diffusion
nodes.en: Alternative frameworks in which spacetime remains fundamentally classical but stochastically fluctuating, such as stochastic gravity and post-quantum classical gravity, predict metric diffusion bounded below by decoherence rates, generating experimentally testable noise signatures in interferometers and mechanical resonators.
nodes.brief:
Alternative frameworks in which spacetime remains fundamentally classical but stochastically fluctuating, such as stochastic gravity and post-quantum classical gravity, predict metric diffusion bounded below by decoherence rates, generating experimentally testable noise signatures in interferometers and mechanical resonators.

### Concepts
- **Stochastic gravity** — Extension of semiclassical gravity that incorporates fluctuations of the stress tensor via the noise kernel, leading to the Einstein-Langevin equation with a Gaussian stochastic source. Captures the second moment <T T> and, to leading order in large N, reproduces the quantum correlation functions of gravitons coupled to N matter fields.
- **Post-quantum classical gravity** — A consistent framework coupling a fundamentally classical, stochastically fluctuating spacetime metric to quantum matter, evading no-go theorems by abandoning reversibility. Predicts unavoidable metric diffusion and gravitational decoherence, experimentally constrained by mass-localization (diffusion) measurements.
- **Decoherence-diffusion trade-off** — Any consistent (Markovian, CPTP) classical-quantum dynamics obeys a trade-off in which the metric diffusion coefficient D_2 is bounded below by the back-reaction coupling D_1 and the inverse decoherence (Lindblad) coefficients D_0 (Oppenheim et al. 2023, Eq. 23: 2 D_2 >= D_1 D_0^-1 D_1^dagger). Long coherence times (small decoherence) force strong metric diffusion, giving a double experimental squ
- **Spacetime diffusion** — Stochastic fluctuation of the metric required if gravity is fundamentally classical. Parametrized by a diffusion coefficient bounded by LISA Pathfinder, torsion balances, atom interferometry and nanomechanical resonators. Atom interferometry pushes below the ultralocal discrete model bound; the nonlocal continuous model survives by ~1 order of magnitude.

nodes.name: Cosmological Constant Problem
nodes.en: Quantum field theory's vacuum-energy predictions overshoot the observed dark-energy density by dozens of orders of magnitude, a naturalness crisis sharpened by Weinberg's no-go theorem, which shows that no simple dynamical adjustment mechanism can generically restore a flat solution.
nodes.brief:
Quantum field theory's vacuum-energy predictions overshoot the observed dark-energy density by dozens of orders of magnitude, a naturalness crisis sharpened by Weinberg's no-go theorem, which shows that no simple dynamical adjustment mechanism can generically restore a flat solution.

### Concepts
- **Cosmological constant problem** — Quantum field theory predicts a vacuum energy density up to ~120 orders of magnitude (~60 after enforcing Lorentz invariance) larger than the observed dark-energy value Lambda ~ 10^-122 M_Pl^4. The most severe naturalness/fine-tuning problem in physics, connecting UV vacuum energy to IR cosmology.
- **Vacuum / zero-point energy** — The zero-point energy of quantum fields that should gravitate and contribute to Lambda. Its calculated magnitude vastly exceeds observation, and its cutoff-dependence (vs the observed cutoff-independent Lambda) is the core of the cosmological constant problem. Sorkin's causal-set prediction Lambda ~ +/- 1/sqrt(V) gives the right order of magnitude from fluctuations.
- **Weinberg no-go theorem** — Weinberg (1989): no local field theory including classical gravity can have a flat (Lambda=0) solution for generic parameter values without fine-tuning; adjustment mechanisms via a scalar field generically fail (the field runs to zero or no static solution exists). Extended to quantum gravity (2017); evaded only by nonlocal gravity, anthropics, or unknown symmetry.

nodes.name: Singularities & Their Resolution
nodes.en: The Penrose-Hawking theorems show that generic classical spacetimes are geodesically incomplete, and quantum-gravity proposals such as the loop-quantum-cosmology big bounce aim to replace these singularities with finite, Planckian-curvature regions, though resolution in full inhomogeneous gravity remains open.
nodes.brief:
The Penrose-Hawking theorems show that generic classical spacetimes are geodesically incomplete, and quantum-gravity proposals such as the loop-quantum-cosmology big bounce aim to replace these singularities with finite, Planckian-curvature regions, though resolution in full inhomogeneous gravity remains open.

### Concepts
- **Penrose-Hawking singularity theorems** — Under generic energy conditions and causality assumptions, GR predicts geodesic incompleteness (singularities). The theorems rely on classical energy conditions that may fail in quantum regimes, motivating but not guaranteeing singularity resolution by quantum gravity.
- **Geodesic incompleteness** — The defining technical content of a singularity: timelike or null geodesics that cannot be extended to arbitrary affine parameter, signaling breakdown of the classical spacetime description (not necessarily curvature blow-up).
- **Singularity resolution** — Replacement of a classical singularity by a region of large-but-finite curvature or a non-singular bounce in a more complete theory. Loop quantum cosmology gives a robust big bounce at Planckian curvature in symmetry-reduced models; species-scale arguments show some singularities (BTZ) instead worsen, while null singularities can resolve. Whether resolution survives in full inhomogeneous gravity i
- **Big bounce** — In loop quantum cosmology the big-bang singularity is replaced by a quantum bounce when curvature reaches ~Planckian values; the bounce is generically asymmetric (pre- and post-bounce branches differ, effective constants such as Newton's G can be rescaled). Provides an explicit example of singularity resolution.

nodes.name: Emergent & Entropic Gravity
nodes.en: The thesis that the spacetime metric and its Einstein dynamics are not fundamental variables but a collective/effective, low-energy description of a deeper microscopic theory, analogous to how hydrodynamics or elasticity emerges from molecular physics.
nodes.cs: Teze, že metrika časoprostoru a její Einsteinova dynamika nejsou fundamentální proměnné, nýbrž kolektivní/efektivní nízkoenergetický popis hlubší mikroskopické teorie, obdobně jako hydrodynamika či pružnost vyvstávají z molekulární fyziky.

nodes.name: Sakharov Induced Gravity
nodes.en: Sakharov induced gravity is Sakharov's 1967 proposal that the Einstein-Hilbert action arises as a one-loop effective term generated by quantum matter fields on a curved background, with the metric-elasticity analogy picturing spacetime curvature as an elastic stiffness (stress-strain response) of the vacuum.
nodes.cs: Sacharovova indukovaná gravitace je Sacharovův návrh z roku 1967, podle něhož Einsteinova-Hilbertova akce vzniká jako jednosmyčkový efektivní člen generovaný kvantovými poli hmoty na zakřiveném pozadí, doplněný analogií metrické elasticity, která zakřivení prostoročasu chápe jako pružnou odezvu (napětí-deformace) vakua.
nodes.brief:
Covers Sakharov's original 1967 proposal that the Einstein-Hilbert action is a one-loop effective term generated by quantum matter fields on a curved background, together with the metric-elasticity analogy that pictures curvature as inducing a stiffness (stress-strain response) of the vacuum.

### Concepts
- **Induced gravity (Sakharov)** — Sakharov's 1967 idea that the Einstein-Hilbert term arises as a one-loop effective action of quantum matter fields on a curved background; Newton's constant and the cosmological constant are determined by the UV cutoff and field spectrum (metric elasticity of the vacuum).
- **Metric elasticity** — Sakharov's analogy in which curvature shifts the zero-point energy of the vacuum, producing a stiffness of space proportional to the Ricci scalar R, just like stress in an elastic continuum.

nodes.name: Horizon Thermodynamics and Einstein's Equations
nodes.en: Jacobson's derivation of the Einstein field equations as a thermodynamic equation of state, built from the Clausius relation applied across local Rindler causal horizons at Unruh temperature, and its non-equilibrium extension incorporating internal entropy production and viscosity.
nodes.brief:
Jacobson's derivation of the Einstein field equations as a thermodynamic equation of state, built from the Clausius relation applied across local Rindler causal horizons at Unruh temperature, and its non-equilibrium extension incorporating internal entropy production and viscosity.

### Concepts
- **Einstein equation as equation of state** — Jacobson's 1995 result that G_{munu}+Lambda g_{munu}=8 pi G T_{munu} is a macroscopic thermodynamic consistency condition (an equation of state) for horizon thermodynamics rather than a fundamental equation of motion.
- **Clausius relation delta Q = T dS** — Thermodynamic relation between heat flux across a horizon and entropy change; the engine of Jacobson's derivation when imposed for all local causal horizons.
- **Local Rindler / causal horizon** — For every point and null direction there exists a locally accelerating observer with a horizon, to which one assigns Unruh temperature T = hbar a / 2 pi c k_B and entropy S = eta A.
- **Unruh temperature** — A uniformly accelerated (Rindler) observer with proper acceleration a perceives the Minkowski vacuum as a thermal bath at temperature T_U = hbar a / (2 pi c k_B). Bogoliubov coefficients between Rindler and Minkowski modes yield an exact Bose-Einstein distribution; equivalent to the KMS condition at inverse temperature 2pi/a. Demonstrates the observer-dependence of the particle concept.
- **Non-equilibrium spacetime thermodynamics** — Eling-Guedens-Jacobson 2006 extension where dS = delta Q/T + d_i S with an internal entropy production term (bulk/shear viscosity of the horizon), required to obtain f(R) gravity and interpreted as tidal heating.

nodes.name: Holographic Principle and Black Hole Entropy
nodes.en: The foundational holographic conjecture that a region's physical degrees of freedom scale with its boundary area rather than its volume, paired with black hole thermodynamics, the paradigm case establishing that horizons carry entropy and temperature.
nodes.brief:
The foundational holographic conjecture that a region's physical degrees of freedom scale with its boundary area rather than its volume, paired with black hole thermodynamics, the paradigm case establishing that horizons carry entropy and temperature.

### Concepts
- **Holographic principle / screen** — Conjecture that the number of fundamental degrees of freedom describing physics in a spatial region is bounded by the area of its boundary in Planck units (N ~ A/4 l_P^2), not its volume; a full quantum-gravity description lives on a lower-dimensional boundary. Proposed by 't Hooft (1993) and Susskind (1994).
- **black-hole-thermodynamics**

nodes.name: Verlinde-Padmanabhan Entropic Gravity
nodes.en: The entropic-force program in which gravity emerges from an equipartition of holographic surface degrees of freedom and their departure from bulk degrees of freedom, driving both Newtonian gravity and cosmic expansion, culminating in the CosMIn count that reproduces the observed cosmological constant.
nodes.brief:
The entropic-force program in which gravity emerges from an equipartition of holographic surface degrees of freedom and their departure from bulk degrees of freedom, driving both Newtonian gravity and cosmic expansion, culminating in the CosMIn count that reproduces the observed cosmological constant.

### Concepts
- **Entropic force** — A macroscopic force F = T dS/dx driving a system toward higher-entropy configurations (like polymer elasticity); Verlinde's 2010 mechanism by which Newtonian gravity and Einstein equations are recovered.
- **Equipartition law** — E = (1/2) N k_B T: each surface (horizon) degree of freedom carries (1/2) k_B T of energy; used by Verlinde and Padmanabhan to define horizon temperature and recover Newton's law.
- **Holographic equipartition (Padmanabhan)** — In any static spacetime N_sur = N_bulk; the departure from this equality, dV/dt = L_P^2 (N_sur - N_bulk), drives cosmic expansion and reproduces the Friedmann equation.
- **Emergence of cosmic space** — Padmanabhan's proposal that the expansion of the universe is the quest of spacetime to reach holographic equipartition; the Hubble volume evolves so that surface and bulk degrees of freedom equalize.
- **CosMIn (cosmic information)** — A dimensionless conserved count of modes crossing the Hubble radius across the three cosmic epochs; the single postulate N = 4 pi reproduces the observed cosmological constant Lambda l_P^2 approx 3.4e-122.

nodes.name: Entanglement-Based Derivations of Gravity
nodes.en: Approaches deriving semiclassical gravity from the structure of quantum entanglement itself: Jacobson's entanglement-equilibrium hypothesis, the modular Hamiltonian formalism underlying entanglement temperature and the first law, and Bianconi's quantum-relative-entropy action for the metric.
nodes.brief:
Approaches deriving semiclassical gravity from the structure of quantum entanglement itself: Jacobson's entanglement-equilibrium hypothesis, the modular Hamiltonian formalism underlying entanglement temperature and the first law, and Bianconi's quantum-relative-entropy action for the metric.

### Concepts
- **Entanglement equilibrium (Jacobson 2015)** — Hypothesis that vacuum entanglement entropy of a small geodesic ball is maximal at fixed volume in a locally maximally symmetric state; stationarity of total entropy is equivalent to the semiclassical Einstein equation.
- **Modular Hamiltonian** — The self-adjoint generator K = -log Delta of the modular flow sigma_t = Delta^{it} (.) Delta^{-it}. For a thermal/reduced state it plays the role of an entanglement (or 'entanglement') Hamiltonian; modular flow is generically nonlocal, but for the Rindler wedge in the vacuum (Bisognano-Wichmann) it is the local Lorentz boost and K = 2 pi (boost generator), giving the Unruh temperature. Variations 
- **Gravity from quantum relative entropy (Bianconi)** — Bianconi's 2024/2025 action given by the quantum relative entropy between the spacetime metric and the metric induced by matter; an auxiliary G-field (Lagrange multipliers) yields a dressed Einstein-Hilbert action with an emergent small positive Lambda.

nodes.name: Emergent Dark Matter and MOND Phenomenology
nodes.en: Verlinde's 2016 proposal that dark-matter effects arise from the volume-law entanglement entropy of de Sitter space acting elastically on baryonic matter, its connection to and empirical tension with MOND and the observed radial acceleration relation in galaxies.
nodes.brief:
Verlinde's 2016 proposal that dark-matter effects arise from the volume-law entanglement entropy of de Sitter space acting elastically on baryonic matter, its connection to and empirical tension with MOND and the observed radial acceleration relation in galaxies.

### Concepts
- **Apparent dark matter (Verlinde 2016)** — Not a particle but an elastic, memory-like response of the dark-energy entropy to baryonic matter; produces extra gravity reproducing flat rotation curves and MOND-like behaviour with a_0 = c H_0.
- **MOND (Modified Newtonian Dynamics)** — Empirical modification of gravity below an acceleration scale a_0 yielding flat rotation curves; Verlinde's emergent gravity reproduces the MOND formula for point masses but differs (factor ~2) for extended bodies.
- **Radial acceleration relation (RAR)** — Tight empirical correlation between observed and baryonic acceleration in galaxies with negligible intrinsic scatter; used by Lelli et al. to challenge Verlinde's emergent gravity, which predicts intrinsic thickness.
- **Volume-law entropy of de Sitter** — In de Sitter space the thermal entropy of the cosmological horizon, spread through the volume, gives a term proportional to V that overtakes the area law beyond the Hubble scale; source of Verlinde's extra dark gravity.

nodes.name: Analogue and Condensed-Matter Gravity
nodes.en: Laboratory and condensed-matter analogues of curved spacetime — sonic dumb-hole horizons, superfluid Fermi-point defects, and the broader hydrodynamic view that general relativity is the emergent long-wavelength limit of an underlying many-body quantum system.
nodes.brief:
Laboratory and condensed-matter analogues of curved spacetime — sonic dumb-hole horizons, superfluid Fermi-point defects, and the broader hydrodynamic view that general relativity is the emergent long-wavelength limit of an underlying many-body quantum system.

### Concepts
- **Analogue gravity** — Framework in which perturbations (sound, light, quasiparticles) in a moving fluid or BEC propagate on an emergent effective curved metric; lets laboratory systems mimic horizons and Hawking radiation.
- **Acoustic metric / dumb hole** — Unruh's effective metric for sound in a flowing fluid; a sonic (dumb-hole) horizon forms where flow speed equals sound speed |v| = c_s, with an associated analogue Hawking temperature from the surface gravity (velocity gradient).
- **Fermi point (Volovik)** — A topologically stable defect in momentum space (as in superfluid 3He-A) near which relativistic fields, gauge symmetry and effective gravity emerge from the quantum vacuum.
- **Gravity as hydrodynamics / geometro-hydrodynamics** — View (Hu, Volovik) that general relativity is the long-wavelength, low-energy hydrodynamic/kinetic limit of an underlying many-body quantum system, with semiclassical gravity one rung above.

nodes.name: Entanglement & Emergence of Spacetime (It from Qubit)
nodes.en: The research program treating quantum entanglement as the generative substrate of spacetime geometry, using holographic entanglement entropy, modular Hamiltonians, tensor-network models of bulk reconstruction, and computational complexity to derive gravitational dynamics and locality directly from the structure of quantum information ('it from qubit').
nodes.cs: Výzkumný program chápající kvantovou provázanost jako generativní substrát geometrie prostoročasu, který pomocí holografické entropie provázanosti, modulárních Hamiltoniánů, tenzorových sítí popisujících rekonstrukci bulku a výpočetní složitosti odvozuje gravitační dynamiku a lokalitu přímo ze struktury kvantové informace ('it from qubit').

nodes.name: Holographic Entanglement Entropy Formulas
nodes.en: The core dictionary translating boundary entanglement entropy into bulk geometry: the Ryu-Takayanagi minimal-surface prescription, its covariant HRT extension to time-dependent states, and the FLM one-loop correction that adds bulk entanglement across the surface.
nodes.brief:
The core dictionary translating boundary entanglement entropy into bulk geometry: the Ryu-Takayanagi minimal-surface prescription, its covariant HRT extension to time-dependent states, and the FLM one-loop correction that adds bulk entanglement across the surface.

### Concepts
- **Entanglement entropy** — Von Neumann entropy S_A = -Tr(rho_A ln rho_A) of the reduced density matrix of a subregion A; measures quantum correlations between A and its complement. In QFT it obeys an area law with a UV cutoff and is the central quantity translated into geometry in holography.
- **Ryu-Takayanagi formula** — S_A = Area(gamma_A)/4G_N: entanglement entropy of a boundary region equals the area of the bulk minimal codimension-2 surface anchored on the boundary of A and homologous to A. Geometrizes entanglement.
- **HRT covariant entanglement entropy** — Covariant generalization of Ryu-Takayanagi to time-dependent states: the bulk surface is extremal (not minimal) in the full Lorentzian geometry, with the smallest-area extremum chosen; reduces to RT on static slices.
- **FLM quantum correction** — Faulkner-Lewkowycz-Maldacena one-loop quantum correction to RT: S_A = Area/4G_N + S_bulk, where S_bulk is the entanglement entropy of bulk fields across the RT surface.

nodes.name: Gravity from Entanglement Thermodynamics
nodes.en: The thesis, spanning Van Raamsdonk's qualitative argument and Jacobson-style derivations, that classical spacetime connectivity and the Einstein equations themselves emerge from the thermodynamics of entanglement, with the first law of entanglement providing the precise quantum analog of dE = T dS.
nodes.brief:
The thesis, spanning Van Raamsdonk's qualitative argument and Jacobson-style derivations, that classical spacetime connectivity and the Einstein equations themselves emerge from the thermodynamics of entanglement, with the first law of entanglement providing the precise quantum analog of dE = T dS.

### Concepts
- **First law of entanglement** — delta S_A = delta <H_A>: to first order in perturbations, the change in entanglement entropy equals the change in expectation value of the modular Hamiltonian H_A; the exact quantum analog of dE = T dS. For ball-shaped regions H_A is a local integral of the stress tensor.
- **Einstein equations from entanglement** — Faulkner et al. 2014: the set of first laws of entanglement for all ball-shaped boundary regions is equivalent, via RT, to the linearized Einstein equations about pure AdS; nonlinear extension recovers full Einstein equations at second order. Gravity emerges from entanglement thermodynamics.
- **Entanglement builds geometry (Van Raamsdonk)** — Thesis that classically connected spacetime emerges from quantum entanglement; disentangling the degrees of freedom of two regions makes them pull apart and pinch off, quantified by standard entanglement measures.
- **emergent-gravity-jacobson**

nodes.name: Algebraic Quantum Gravity & Modular Theory
nodes.en: A von Neumann algebra reformulation of holography in which the JLMS relation, entanglement-wedge reconstruction, and the type III-to-type II crossed-product transition together recast generalized entropy and subregion duality as statements about modular flow and operator algebras.
nodes.brief:
A von Neumann algebra reformulation of holography in which the JLMS relation, entanglement-wedge reconstruction, and the type III-to-type II crossed-product transition together recast generalized entropy and subregion duality as statements about modular flow and operator algebras.

### Concepts
- **JLMS relation** — Jafferis-Lewkowycz-Maldacena-Suh: the boundary (CFT) modular Hamiltonian of a region equals the bulk modular Hamiltonian of its entanglement wedge plus the area operator over 4G, K_bdy = A/4G + K_bulk. Boundary modular flow is dual to bulk modular flow. Recast algebraically, JLMS is the statement that the area operator is the central/affiliated operator distinguishing the type II crossed-product e
- **von Neumann algebra crossed product / type II** — Including the gravitational constraint changes the algebra of observables in a subregion from type III_1 to type II, permitting a well-defined (renormalized) entropy; gives an algebraic origin of A/4G and a framework for islands, firewalls and emergent time via modular flow.
- **Subregion-subalgebra duality** — The holographic correspondence between a boundary subregion's algebra and the bulk algebra of its entanglement wedge: bulk operators are reconstructed as elements of the boundary subalgebra (entanglement-wedge reconstruction), and modular flows match (JLMS). Phrased algebraically it makes the von Neumann type and center of the subalgebra the carrier of bulk geometric information (wedge, horizon, a

nodes.name: Emergent Time & Pseudo-Entropy
nodes.en: Approaches to deriving the time direction itself from quantum information, using tools like half-sided modular flow and the complex-valued pseudo-entropy of timelike entanglement to probe temporal emergence in cosmological and de Sitter holography.
nodes.cs: Přístupy odvozující směr času přímo z kvantové informace pomocí nástrojů jako jednostranný modulární tok (half-sided modular flow) a komplexní pseudoentropie časupodobného provázání, sloužící ke zkoumání emergence času v kosmologické a de Sitterově holografii.
nodes.brief:
Approaches to deriving the time direction itself from quantum information, using tools like half-sided modular flow and the complex-valued pseudo-entropy of timelike entanglement to probe temporal emergence in cosmological and de Sitter holography.

### Concepts
- **Emergent time** — Open problem of deriving the time coordinate (not just spatial directions) from quantum information; approached via half-sided modular flow, Connes cocycle, and pseudo-entropy / timelike entanglement in cosmological and de Sitter holography.
- **Pseudo-entropy / timelike entanglement** — Generalization of entanglement entropy to non-Hermitian transition density matrices; generically complex-valued, used as a probe of the emergence of time in cosmological and de Sitter holography.

nodes.name: Holographic Tensor Networks & Error Correction
nodes.en: Discrete and quantum-information-theoretic models of AdS/CFT, in which bulk logical qubits are redundantly encoded into boundary degrees of freedom via quantum error correction, and explicit tensor-network constructions like the HaPPY code and MERA realize emergent bulk geometry from entanglement structure.
nodes.cs: Diskrétní modely korespondence AdS/CFT z pohledu teorie kvantové informace, v nichž jsou logické qubity bulku redundantně zakódovány do stupňů volnosti na hranici pomocí kvantové korekce chyb; konkrétní konstrukce tenzorových sítí, jako HaPPY kód a MERA, realizují emergentní geometrii bulku z provázané struktury.
nodes.brief:
Discrete and quantum-information-theoretic models of AdS/CFT, in which bulk logical qubits are redundantly encoded into boundary degrees of freedom via quantum error correction, and explicit tensor-network constructions like the HaPPY code and MERA realize emergent bulk geometry from entanglement structure.

### Concepts
- **Holographic quantum error correction** — Bulk logical qubits are redundantly encoded into boundary physical qubits; explains subregion duality, radial commutativity and the resilience of bulk reconstruction to boundary erasures.
- **HaPPY code** — Holographic tensor-network code of perfect tensors (pentagons/hexagons) on a hyperbolic lattice (Pastawski-Yoshida-Harlow-Preskill 2015); an exactly solvable toy model of AdS/CFT obeying RT and negative tripartite information.
- **MERA tensor network** — Multiscale entanglement renormalization ansatz for critical ground states; its emergent radial (RG-scale) direction is interpreted as a discrete slice of AdS (Swingle 2012), realizing holographic geometry from entanglement renormalization.
- **Tensor network holography** — General program building emergent bulk geometry from tensor networks whose link structure encodes entanglement; includes MERA, HaPPY, hyperinvariant networks, and lifted/random networks reproducing RT.

nodes.name: Entropy Inequalities & Bit Threads
nodes.en: The geometric and information-theoretic constraints obeyed by holographic entanglement entropy, encompassing the holographic entropy cone of realizable entropy vectors and its max-flow/min-cut reformulation via Planck-thickness bit threads.
nodes.cs: Geometrická a teoreticko-informační omezení, jimž podléhá holografická provázaná entropie, zahrnující holografický entropický kužel realizovatelných entropických vektorů a jeho přeformulaci pomocí max-flow/min-cut řezů skrze bitová vlákna planckovské tloušťky.
nodes.brief:
The geometric and information-theoretic constraints obeyed by holographic entanglement entropy, encompassing the holographic entropy cone of realizable entropy vectors and its max-flow/min-cut reformulation via Planck-thickness bit threads.

### Concepts
- **Bit threads** — Max-flow/min-cut reformulation of RT (Freedman-Headrick 2016): S_A is the maximum flux of a divergence-free, norm-bounded vector field through A; Planck-thickness threads represent bilateral entanglement and naturally implement monogamy and nesting.
- **Holographic entropy cone** — The set of linear inequalities (subadditivity, strong subadditivity, monogamy of mutual information, and higher-party inequalities) characterizing which entropy vectors are realizable by RT/HRT minimal surfaces; a necessary condition for a state to have a classical geometric dual. The covariant (HRT) cone was proven equivalent to the static (RT) cone in 2026 via a no-short-cut theorem.

nodes.name: Eternal Black Holes, Complexity & Wormholes
nodes.en: The physics of the two-sided eternal AdS black hole dual to the thermofield double state, including the Wheeler-DeWitt patch underlying complexity=action/volume proposals and the Gao-Jafferis-Wall traversable wormhole realizing ER=EPR as quantum teleportation.
nodes.cs: Fyzika oboustranné věčné AdS černé díry duální ke stavu tepelného pole (thermofield double state), včetně Wheelerovy–DeWittovy oblasti (Wheeler-DeWitt patch), na níž stojí hypotézy complexity=action/volume, a průchodné červí díry Gao–Jafferise–Walla realizující ER=EPR jako kvantovou teleportaci.
nodes.brief:
The physics of the two-sided eternal AdS black hole dual to the thermofield double state, including the Wheeler-DeWitt patch underlying complexity=action/volume proposals and the Gao-Jafferis-Wall traversable wormhole realizing ER=EPR as quantum teleportation.

### Concepts
- **Thermofield double (TFD)** — Entangled pure state of two CFT copies whose bulk dual is the two-sided eternal AdS black hole; the Einstein-Rosen bridge length grows with time, motivating complexity = volume / action proposals.
- **Wheeler-DeWitt patch** — Bulk spacetime region whose gravitational action equals the boundary state's complexity in the complexity=action conjecture; bounded by null sheets from the boundary slice.
- **Traversable wormhole** — An Einstein-Rosen bridge rendered traversable by negative average null energy from a double-trace deformation coupling the two boundaries (Gao-Jafferis-Wall 2017); interpreted via ER=EPR as quantum teleportation; cannot violate causality.

nodes.name: Quantum Focusing & Entropy Bounds
nodes.en: Semiclassical generalized-entropy bounds on light-sheets and null congruences, running from the covariant Bousso bound through the quantum focusing conjecture that unifies it with classical focusing, down to the quantum null energy condition it implies.
nodes.cs: Semiklasické meze zobecněné entropie na světelných plachtách (light-sheets) a nulových kongruencích, od kovariantní Boussovy meze přes kvantovou fokusační hypotézu, jež ji sjednocuje s klasickým fokusačním teorémem, až po jí implikovanou kvantovou nulovou energetickou podmínku.
nodes.brief:
Semiclassical generalized-entropy bounds on light-sheets and null congruences, running from the covariant Bousso bound through the quantum focusing conjecture that unifies it with classical focusing, down to the quantum null energy condition it implies.

### Concepts
- **Quantum focusing conjecture (QFC)** — Bousso-Wall conjecture that the quantum expansion (the derivative of generalized entropy per unit area along a null congruence) is non-increasing. Unifies the Bousso bound with classical focusing at the semiclassical level and implies the quantum null energy condition.
- **Quantum null energy condition (QNEC)** — A local bound stating that the null energy density at a point is bounded below by the second variation of the entanglement entropy of a region whose boundary passes through that point. Proven from relative entropy/modular flow and implied by the quantum focusing conjecture; the semiclassical replacement of the classical null energy condition.
- **Covariant entropy (Bousso) bound** — Entropy on a light-sheet does not exceed A/4G_N; the generalized (quantum) Bousso bound follows from QFC.

nodes.name: SYK Model & Near-AdS2 Holography
nodes.en: The Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model of random-interacting Majorana fermions and its low-energy Schwarzian effective dynamics, which saturate the maximal chaos bound and provide the best-controlled solvable dual to near-AdS2 Jackiw-Teitelboim gravity.
nodes.cs: Sachdevův–Ye–Kitaevův model náhodně interagujících Majoranových fermionů a jeho nízkoenergetická efektivní Schwarzova dynamika, které saturují mez maximálního chaosu a poskytují nejlépe kontrolovaný řešitelný duál pro téměř-AdS2 Jackiwovu–Teitelboimovu gravitaci.
nodes.brief:
The Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model of random-interacting Majorana fermions and its low-energy Schwarzian effective dynamics, which saturate the maximal chaos bound and provide the best-controlled solvable dual to near-AdS2 Jackiw-Teitelboim gravity.

### Concepts
- **Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev (SYK) model** — Quantum mechanics of N Majorana fermions with random all-to-all q-body interactions in (0+1)D. In the large-N limit it is dominated by melonic diagrams, develops an emergent conformal (reparametrization) symmetry in the IR with operator dimension Delta=1/q, saturates the chaos bound with Lyapunov exponent lambda_L = 2 pi/beta, and is holographically dual to near-AdS2 / Jackiw-Teitelboim gravity.
- **Schwarzian action** — Low-energy effective action S = -(N alpha_S/J) integral {f,tau} dtau of the reparametrization (soft) mode f(tau) describing the spontaneous + explicit breaking of conformal symmetry in SYK at large but finite coupling. It is maximally chaotic and is the boundary mode of near-AdS2 dilaton (Jackiw-Teitelboim) gravity.
- **Near-AdS2 / JT gravity holography** — Duality between nearly-AdS2 Jackiw-Teitelboim gravity and the Schwarzian sector of SYK at low temperature; the simplest controllable model of emergent holography and gravitational dynamics.
- **Chaos bound (Lyapunov)** — Maldacena-Shenker-Stanford bound lambda_L <= 2pi/beta on the Lyapunov growth of out-of-time-order correlators; saturated by black holes and the SYK model (maximal chaos).

nodes.name: Quantum Gravity Phenomenology & Experimental Tests
nodes.en: The search for observable, testable signatures of quantum gravity in existing or near-future experiments and astrophysical data, spanning Lorentz-invariance violation and modified dispersion relations, gravitationally induced entanglement between massive quantum systems, Planck-scale spacetime-fluctuation noise, and gravitational-wave probes of horizon-scale quantum structure.
nodes.cs: Hledání pozorovatelných, testovatelných projevů kvantové gravitace v současných či blízkých budoucích experimentech a astrofyzikálních datech, zahrnující porušení Lorentzovy invariance a modifikované disperzní relace, gravitačně indukované provázání mezi hmotnými kvantovými systémy, šum z planckovských fluktuací prostoročasu a gravitačně-vlnové testy kvantové struktury na škále horizontu.

nodes.name: Lorentz Violation & Dispersion Theory
nodes.en: Theoretical frameworks proposing quantum-gravity-induced departures from exact Lorentz invariance, including modified dispersion relations, explicit Lorentz Invariance Violation, doubly/deformed special relativity with its relative-locality consequence, and the Standard-Model Extension that parametrizes such operators by mass dimension.
nodes.cs: Teoretické rámce navrhující kvantově-gravitačně indukované odchylky od přesné Lorentzovy invariance, včetně modifikovaných disperzních relací, explicitního porušení Lorentzovy invariance, dvojitě speciální relativity (doubly/deformed special relativity) s jejím důsledkem relativní lokality a rozšíření Standardního modelu (Standard-Model Extension), které tyto operátory parametrizuje podle hmotnostní dimenze.
nodes.brief:
Theoretical frameworks proposing quantum-gravity-induced departures from exact Lorentz invariance, including modified dispersion relations, explicit Lorentz Invariance Violation, doubly/deformed special relativity with its relative-locality consequence, and the Standard-Model Extension that parametrizes such operators by mass dimension.

### Concepts
- **Modified Dispersion Relation (MDR)** — A Planck-suppressed deformation of E^2 = p^2 c^2 + m^2 c^4 of the form E^2 = p^2 c^2 [1 +/- (E/E_QG)^n], with n=1 (linear) or n=2 (quadratic) and E_QG the sought quantum-gravity energy scale. Implies an energy-dependent photon group velocity v(E) = c[1 -/+ ((n+1)/2)(E/E_QG)^n]. Core object of all time-of-flight phenomenology.
- **Lorentz Invariance Violation (LIV)** — Explicit breaking of Lorentz symmetry by a preferred frame/scale, systematically parametrized by Standard-Model Extension (SME) coefficients organized by operator mass dimension (4,5,6,...). Dimension-5 and -6 operators generate modified dispersion and vacuum birefringence. Constrained by GRB time-of-flight, UHECR thresholds, neutrinos, and polarization.
- **Doubly/Deformed Special Relativity (DSR) and kappa-Poincare** — A quantum-gravity-motivated deformation of special relativity adding a second invariant scale (E_Pl) WITHOUT a preferred frame, realized algebraically by the kappa-Poincare Hopf algebra. Implies relative locality. Crucially, unlike LIV some DSR models predict NO observable time-of-flight delay for massless particles.
- **Relative Locality** — Principle replacing absolute locality in DSR/curved-momentum-space models: coincidence of events is observer-dependent, with curvature in momentum space at the Planck scale. Has the consequence that energy-dependent arrival-time delays can vanish despite a deformed dispersion relation, complicating LIV interpretation of GRB data.
- **Standard-Model Extension (SME)** — An effective field theory framework cataloging all Lorentz- and CPT-violating operators by mass dimension, providing the standard parametrization (coefficients) against which GRB, UHECR, neutrino, and laboratory LIV bounds are reported.

nodes.name: Astrophysical Tests of Lorentz Violation
nodes.en: Observational probes that constrain LIV/MDR using cosmic messengers, spanning photon time-of-flight delays, vacuum birefringence of polarized light, and reaction-threshold anomalies measured via gamma-ray bursts, ultra-high-energy cosmic rays, and neutrino spectra.
nodes.cs: Observační testy omezující porušení Lorentzovy invariance a modifikované disperzní relace (LIV/MDR) pomocí kosmických poslů, zahrnující zpoždění doby letu fotonů, vakuovou dvojlomnost polarizovaného světla a anomálie reakčních prahů měřené pomocí záblesků záření gama, kosmického záření ultravysokých energií a neutrinových spekter.
nodes.brief:
Observational probes that constrain LIV/MDR using cosmic messengers, spanning photon time-of-flight delays, vacuum birefringence of polarized light, and reaction-threshold anomalies measured via gamma-ray bursts, ultra-high-energy cosmic rays, and neutrino spectra.

### Concepts
- **Time-of-Flight Delay** — Energy-dependent difference in arrival time of photons (or neutrinos) emitted simultaneously from a distant source, accumulated over cosmological distance: Delta t ~ ((n+1)/2)(E_h^n - E_l^n)/E_QG^n times a redshift integral. Non-observation yields lower bounds on E_QG. Primary observable for Fermi-LAT and LHAASO GRB studies.
- **Vacuum Birefringence** — For a CPT-odd (dimension-5) LIV operator, left- and right-circular polarizations propagate at different speeds, rotating the linear polarization vector by an energy-dependent angle Delta theta ~ xi (E^2-E0^2) d / (2 E_Pl). High observed polarization of distant GRBs excludes large xi; more sensitive than time-of-flight by ~1/omega, giving the tightest propagation bound (xi < ~2.4e-15).
- **Threshold Anomaly** — LIV shifts the energy thresholds of particle reactions (GZK photopion production p + gamma_CMB -> Delta; pair production gamma gamma -> e+ e-), distorting observed UHECR and TeV gamma-ray spectra and the cosmogenic neutrino flux. A complementary, source-model-independent LIV probe.
- **UHECR and Neutrino LIV Constraints** — Ultra-high-energy cosmic rays (Auger) and astrophysical neutrinos (IceCube) constrain LIV: LIV would suppress GZK photopion interactions, modify the highest-energy spectrum, and shift the cosmogenic neutrino peak. Energy-dependent neutrino-photon delays can reach M_QG >~ 1e26 eV (linear) / 1e19 eV (quadratic) in principle.

nodes.name: Gravitationally Induced Entanglement Theory
nodes.en: The theoretical machinery underlying gravity-mediated entanglement between spatially superposed masses: the entangling mechanism itself, the accumulated gravitational phase, the two-mode-squeezing Hamiltonian that generates it, and the witness observables used to certify entanglement without full tomography.
nodes.cs: Teoretický aparát stojící za gravitačně zprostředkovaným provázáním mezi prostorově superponovanými hmotami: samotný mechanismus provázání, akumulovaná gravitační fáze, dvoumódový stlačovací hamiltonián, který jej generuje, a svědecké pozorovatelné veličiny (witness observables) používané k certifikaci provázání bez úplné tomografie.
nodes.brief:
The theoretical machinery underlying gravity-mediated entanglement between spatially superposed masses: the entangling mechanism itself, the accumulated gravitational phase, the two-mode-squeezing Hamiltonian that generates it, and the witness observables used to certify entanglement without full tomography.

### Concepts
- **Gravitationally Induced Entanglement (GIE / QGEM)** — Generation of entanglement between two spatially superposed masses via their mutual gravitational interaction (Bose et al., Marletto-Vedral 2017). Argued to witness the quantum nature of gravity, but recent work shows classical/post-quantum gravity models can also produce it, weakening theory-independent no-go claims.
- **Gravitational Phase (BMV)** — The relative phase Delta phi = G m^2 Delta t / (hbar d) acquired between interferometer branches due to the Newtonian potential difference; entanglement is detectable when Delta phi reaches O(1) rad within the coherence time. For m~1e-14 kg, d~200 um, Delta t~1 s this is order radians.
- **Two-Mode Squeezing (gravitational entangler)** — Expansion of the Newtonian interaction yields a bilinear coupling H_int ~ (G m1 m2 / d^3) x1 x2, a two-mode squeezing Hamiltonian that entangles the positions/momenta of the two masses. The mechanism underlying GIE even in variants without spin-based Stern-Gerlach splitting.
- **Entanglement Witness** — An observable whose value outside a classical bound certifies the presence of entanglement. In BMV, spin correlations such as <sigma_x^(1) sigma_y^(2)> witness gravity-mediated entanglement without full state tomography.

nodes.name: BMV Experimental Platforms & Decoherence Limits
nodes.en: Concrete experimental routes toward testing gravitationally induced entanglement and gravity quantization, from the Bose-Marletto-Vedral nanodiamond interferometer, levitated optomechanics, and atom interferometry (MAGIS-100) to single-graviton quantum sensing, together with the gravitational decoherence mechanisms that set the dominant sensitivity limits on such experiments.
nodes.cs: Konkrétní experimentální cesty k testování gravitačně indukovaného provázání a kvantování gravitace, od nanodiamantového interferometru Bose–Marletto–Vedral, levitované optomechaniky a atomové interferometrie (MAGIS-100) až po kvantovou detekci jednotlivých gravitonů, spolu s mechanismy gravitační dekoherence, které určují dominantní meze citlivosti takových experimentů.
nodes.brief:
Concrete experimental routes toward testing gravitationally induced entanglement and gravity quantization, from the Bose-Marletto-Vedral nanodiamond interferometer, levitated optomechanics, and atom interferometry (MAGIS-100) to single-graviton quantum sensing, together with the gravitational decoherence mechanisms that set the dominant sensitivity limits on such experiments.

### Concepts
- **Bose-Marletto-Vedral (BMV) Experiment** — Concrete GIE realization: two ~1e-14 kg masses (NV-center nanodiamonds) placed in adjacent Stern-Gerlach matter-wave interferometers, separated by >~200 um (to suppress Casimir-Polder), held in superposition ~250 um wide for ~1 s; a spin entanglement witness certifies gravity-mediated entanglement.
- **Gravitational Decoherence** — Loss of quantum coherence induced by gravity: either fundamental objective collapse (Diosi-Penrose) or universal decoherence from gravitational time dilation (Pikovski et al.). Sets the dominant limit on BMV-type experiments and is itself a testable quantum-gravity-adjacent prediction.
- **Universal Decoherence from Time Dilation** — Pikovski-Zych-Costa-Brukner mechanism: gravitational time dilation couples a composite particle's internal energy to its center of mass, decohering its position without any external environment. Earth's g already suffices for micrometre objects, offering a gravitational route to the quantum-to-classical transition.
- **Levitated Optomechanics** — Levitated nanoparticles/nanodiamonds cooled to the motional ground state in optical/magnetic/electric traps; the leading route to high-mass quantum superpositions (10^6 amu and above) needed for BMV-type tests, with electron diffraction giving ~1000x larger momentum splitting than two-photon recoil.
- **Atom Interferometry / MAGIS-100** — A 100 m matter-wave atomic gradiometer (Fermilab, NuMI shaft) using ultracold strontium to search for ultralight (wave-like) dark matter, test quantum mechanics on macroscopic scales and the equivalence principle, and pathfind mid-band (0.1-10 Hz) GW detection. Laser lab completed 2026; multi-year commissioning.
- **Single-Graviton Detection** — Gravito-phononic analog of the photoelectric effect: stimulated absorption of a single graviton in a macroscopic quantum resonator, resolvable with quantum sensing (Tobar et al. 2024). A possible first experimental clue of gravity quantization, though stimulated processes also occur classically.

nodes.name: Planck-Scale Spacetime Fluctuations
nodes.en: Phenomenology built around a fundamental minimal length at the Planck scale, including the generalized uncertainty principle and cross-theory minimal-length arguments, and the holographic and geontropic noise signatures searched for with precision interferometers such as the Holometer and GQuEST.
nodes.cs: Fenomenologie postavená na fundamentální minimální délce na Planckově škále, včetně zobecněného principu neurčitosti a argumentů pro minimální délku napříč teoriemi, a holografických a geontropických šumových signatur hledaných pomocí přesných interferometrů, jako jsou Holometer a GQuEST.
nodes.brief:
Phenomenology built around a fundamental minimal length at the Planck scale, including the generalized uncertainty principle and cross-theory minimal-length arguments, and the holographic and geontropic noise signatures searched for with precision interferometers such as the Holometer and GQuEST.

### Concepts
- **Holographic Noise** — Hogan's conjecture that transverse positions of bodies fluctuate randomly by ~Planck length per Planck time (Planckian geometric indeterminacy), giving a flat (frequency-independent) strain power spectral density set only by the Planck time. Measured by the Fermilab Holometer (two cross-correlated 40 m Michelson interferometers).
- **Generalized Uncertainty Principle (GUP)** — Deformation Delta x Delta p >= (hbar/2)(1 + beta (Delta p)^2 + ...) implying a minimal measurable length Delta x_min = beta_0 ell_Pl. Common to string theory, LQG, and noncommutative geometry; constrained in macroscopic oscillators, interferometers, and LIGO (currently beta_0 <~ 1e6-1e10).
- **Minimal Length** — The existence of a smallest measurable distance ~ell_Pl, a shared feature of string theory (string length ell_s), loop quantum gravity (discrete area), and noncommutative geometry. Phenomenologically encoded via GUP and modified dispersion relations.
- **Planck Scale** — The natural quantum-gravity scale: E_Pl = sqrt(hbar c^5/G) ~ 1.22e19 GeV, ell_Pl ~ 1.6e-35 m, t_Pl ~ 5.4e-44 s. ~15 orders of magnitude above LHC, motivating amplification strategies (cosmological distance, high energy, macroscopic quantum coherence) in phenomenology.
- **Geontropic Noise (Verlinde-Zurek effect)** — Predicted correlated fluctuation of an interferometer arm length arising from fluctuations of the modular (entanglement) energy of holographic degrees of freedom on the boundary of a causal diamond: <Delta K^2> = <K> gives <Delta L^2> ~ ell_Pl L with a characteristic (non-flat, IR-dominated) spectral shape. Unlike Hogan's flat holographic noise it can evade Holometer bounds; targeted by the GQuEST

nodes.name: Gravitational-Wave Tests of Quantum Gravity
nodes.en: Black-hole merger and gravitational-wave observables used to probe quantum-gravity and beyond-GR physics, including post-ringdown echoes from horizon-scale quantum structure, quasinormal-mode spectroscopy, graviton mass bounds from waveform dispersion, and the memory effect linked to the soft-graviton theorem and BMS supertranslation symmetry.
nodes.cs: Pozorovatelné veličiny spojené se splynutím černých děr a gravitačními vlnami, používané ke zkoumání kvantové gravitace a fyziky za rámcem obecné relativity, včetně dozvuků (echoes) po odeznění (ringdown) pocházejících z kvantové struktury na škále horizontu, spektroskopie kvazinormálních módů, mezí hmotnosti gravitonu z disperze vlnové formy a paměťového efektu spojeného s teorémem měkkého gravitonu a supertranslační BMS symetrií.
nodes.brief:
Black-hole merger and gravitational-wave observables used to probe quantum-gravity and beyond-GR physics, including post-ringdown echoes from horizon-scale quantum structure, quasinormal-mode spectroscopy, graviton mass bounds from waveform dispersion, and the memory effect linked to the soft-graviton theorem and BMS supertranslation symmetry.

### Concepts
- **Gravitational Wave Echoes** — Repeated pulses following ringdown, predicted if the classical horizon is replaced by quantum structure (firewall, fuzzball, exotic compact object) reflecting waves at ~ell_Pl from the horizon. Searched for in LIGO/Virgo/KAGRA data; no statistically significant detection, first model-independent late-time QNM bounds set.
- **Ringdown / Black-Hole Spectroscopy** — Measurement of quasinormal-mode (QNM) frequencies after a binary black-hole merger, testing the Kerr metric and the no-hair theorem and constraining beyond-GR (quadratic gravity, dynamical Chern-Simons, scalar-Gauss-Bonnet). GW250114 (O4) provided a high-SNR ringdown consistent with GR.
- **Graviton Mass Bound** — A nonzero graviton mass m_g modifies GW dispersion E^2 = p^2 c^2 + m_g^2 c^4, causing frequency-dependent delay in the waveform. LIGO/Virgo (GWTC-3) bounds m_g <~ 1.3e-23 eV/c^2; GW170817-GRB170817A coincidence gives |v_g - c|/c <~ 1e-15.
- **Gravitational Wave Memory** — Permanent shift in the relative position of test masses after a GW passes; mathematically the Fourier transform of Weinberg's soft graviton theorem (Strominger-Zhiboedov). Its detection would test the soft-graviton/BMS-supertranslation structure underlying scattering amplitudes.
- **Soft Theorem / BMS Supertranslations** — The memory-soft-symmetry triangle: GW memory = soft graviton theorem = BMS supertranslation Ward identity. A deep link between an observable GW phenomenon and the asymptotic symmetries and soft structure of gravitational scattering amplitudes.

nodes.name: Group Field Theory & Tensor Models
nodes.en: A quantum field theory for a field phi: G^d -> C (or R) defined over d copies of a Lie group G. Field arguments represent parallel transports on the d boundary faces of an elementary d-simplex; the interaction vertex glues d+1 fields into a d-simplex, so Feynman graphs are dual to simplicial complexes (triangulations) and Feynman amplitudes equal spin-foam amplitudes. The partition function is a non-perturbative sum over simplicial topologies and geometries.
nodes.cs: Kvantová teorie pole pro pole φ: G^d → C (resp. R) definované nad d kopiemi Lieovy grupy G. Argumenty pole reprezentují paralelní přenosy na d hraničních stěnách elementárního d-simplexu; interakční vrchol lepí d+1 polí do d-simplexu, takže Feynmanovy grafy jsou duální k simpliciálním komplexům (triangulacím) a Feynmanovy amplitudy se rovnají amplitudám spinových pěn. Partiční funkce je neporuchový součet přes simpliciální topologie a geometrie.

nodes.name: Matrix & Tensor Model Foundations
nodes.en: The combinatorial ancestors of group field theory: random matrix models generate triangulated 2D surfaces and 2D quantum gravity via a genus expansion, while their rank-d generalization, tensor models (including the colored variant), produces random triangulations of higher-dimensional pseudomanifolds as the purely combinatorial skeleton of GFT.
nodes.cs: Kombinatoričtí předchůdci teorie grupového pole: modely náhodných matic generují triangulované 2D plochy a 2D kvantovou gravitaci prostřednictvím rozvoje podle rodu (genus expansion), zatímco jejich zobecnění řádu d, tenzorové modely (včetně barevné varianty), produkují náhodné triangulace vícedimenzionálních pseudovariet jako čistě kombinatorickou kostru teorie grupového pole (GFT).
nodes.brief:
The combinatorial ancestors of group field theory: random matrix models generate triangulated 2D surfaces and 2D quantum gravity via a genus expansion, while their rank-d generalization, tensor models (including the colored variant), produces random triangulations of higher-dimensional pseudomanifolds as the purely combinatorial skeleton of GFT.

### Concepts
- **Tensor Model** — A 0-dimensional probabilistic/combinatorial model for a rank-d tensor T_{a_1...a_d} with indices a_i in {1,...,N}, generalizing random matrix models (d=2) to higher rank. Generates random triangulations of d-dimensional pseudomanifolds as Feynman graphs; the combinatorial 'skeleton' of GFT without geometric (group) data.
- **Matrix Model of 2D Gravity** — Integral over N x N Hermitian matrices whose Feynman graphs are dual to random triangulated 2D surfaces, organized by a genus (topological) expansion F ~ sum_g N^{2-2g} F_g. In the double-scaling limit it provides a non-perturbative definition of 2D quantum gravity / 2D string theory; pure gravity is governed by a Painleve I equation.
- **Colored Tensor Model** — Gurau's variant with d+1 distinct fields ('colors'), each vertex containing exactly one field of each color. Coloring guarantees that dual complexes are simplicial pseudomanifolds in any dimension and enables a well-defined topological 1/N expansion via the Gurau degree.
- **two-d-quantum-gravity**

nodes.name: Melonic Dominance & Large-N Dynamics
nodes.en: The topological expansion machinery that controls tensor and GFT models at large index N: the Gurau degree organizes the 1/N expansion, melonic graphs dominate at leading order and are resummable via Schwinger-Dyson gap equations, and the double-scaling limit and emergent conformal/maximally-chaotic dynamics link this structure to the SYK model.
nodes.brief:
The topological expansion machinery that controls tensor and GFT models at large index N: the Gurau degree organizes the 1/N expansion, melonic graphs dominate at leading order and are resummable via Schwinger-Dyson gap equations, and the double-scaling limit and emergent conformal/maximally-chaotic dynamics link this structure to the SYK model.

### Concepts
- **Gurau Degree** — A non-negative integer omega assigned to a colored tensor graph, defined as the sum of genera of its jackets (globally embedded ribbon subgraphs). It plays the role that genus plays for matrix models, controlling the 1/N expansion via the power N^{d - (2/(d-1)!) omega}. It is a combinatorial invariant of the graph, not a topological invariant of the dual manifold.
- **Melonic Dominance** — The phenomenon that graphs of Gurau degree omega=0 ('melons', dual to triangulations of the sphere S^d, built by iterated insertion of pairs of vertices joined by d parallel edges) dominate the leading order of the large-N / 1/N expansion. Melonic sums are explicitly resummable and yield closed Schwinger-Dyson (gap) equations. Shared by tensor models and the SYK model.
- **1/N Expansion** — Systematic topological expansion of free energy / correlators in inverse powers of the index range N, organizing the sum over geometries by suppressing more complicated topologies. For matrices organized by genus; for tensors by the Gurau degree; for SYK/tensor QM by a melonic limit (e.g. g^2 N^3 fixed).
- **Double Scaling Limit** — A coordinated limit of large index N together with tuning the coupling to its critical value, retaining all orders of the topological expansion to give a non-perturbative result. For matrix models yields 2D gravity (Painleve I); for tensor models in D<6 it is stable and resums melons plus subleading 'cherry trees'.
- **Melonic Schwinger-Dyson (Gap) Equation** — Self-consistent equation for the two-point function in a melonically dominated model: G = 1/(K - Sigma) with self-energy Sigma(t1,t2) = g^2 [G(t1,t2)]^{q-1}. In the IR (kinetic term dropped) it admits a conformal solution G ~ |t|^{-2Delta} with Delta=1/q, giving emergent conformal symmetry shared by SYK and tensor models.
- **maximal-chaos**

nodes.name: Spin Foam & Simplicial Gravity Models
nodes.en: Concrete geometric GFT models whose Feynman amplitudes reproduce spin-foam state sums for simplicial quantum gravity, from Boulatov's original SU(2) model and its Ooguri extension to the Ponzano-Regge 3D state sum and the 4D Barrett-Crane model built on simplicity-constrained BF theory.
nodes.brief:
Concrete geometric GFT models whose Feynman amplitudes reproduce spin-foam state sums for simplicial quantum gravity, from Boulatov's original SU(2) model and its Ooguri extension to the Ponzano-Regge 3D state sum and the 4D Barrett-Crane model built on simplicity-constrained BF theory.

### Concepts
- **Boulatov-Ooguri Model** — The original GFT: Boulatov (1992) defined a field theory over three copies of SU(2) (with gauge invariance) whose Feynman amplitudes reproduce the Ponzano-Regge model of 3D gravity; Ooguri (1992) extended it to 4D BF theory over four copies of SU(2). Backbone of all geometric GFT models.
- **Spin Foam** — A 2-complex with faces carrying group irreducible representations and edges carrying intertwiners; the 'history' of a spin network. GFT Feynman amplitudes equal spin-foam amplitudes, so GFT is the generating functional that also sums over the underlying complexes and topologies.
- **Ponzano-Regge Model** — State-sum model for 3D Euclidean quantum gravity assigning SU(2) 6j-symbols to tetrahedra of a triangulated 3-manifold. The Boulatov GFT generates its triangulations and amplitudes as Feynman diagrams.
- **Barrett-Crane Model** — A 4D spin-foam model of quantum gravity obtained by imposing simplicity constraints on BF theory; arises from a Boulatov-Ooguri GFT over the homogeneous space SO(4)/SO(3) (Euclidean) and admits a complete Lorentzian version with spacelike, timelike and lightlike tetrahedra encoding local causal structure.

nodes.name: GFT Condensate Cosmology
nodes.en: The condensate-cosmology program built on GFT's reinterpretation as a second quantization of loop quantum gravity: coherent condensates of 'atoms of space' yield effective, relationally time-parametrized Friedmann dynamics that replace the classical big-bang singularity with a quantum bounce.
nodes.brief:
The condensate-cosmology program built on GFT's reinterpretation as a second quantization of loop quantum gravity: coherent condensates of 'atoms of space' yield effective, relationally time-parametrized Friedmann dynamics that replace the classical big-bang singularity with a quantum bounce.

### Concepts
- **GFT Condensate** — A coherent / squeezed state of a large number of GFT quanta in the same single-particle geometric configuration, analogous to a Bose-Einstein condensate ('atoms of space'). A macroscopically homogeneous condensate describes a homogeneous quantum cosmology; its hydrodynamics yields effective cosmological dynamics.
- **Effective Friedmann Equation (GFT)** — A generalization of the Friedmann equations derived from GFT condensate hydrodynamics in the Gross-Pitaevskii approximation, including quantum-gravity corrections. For a single-spin condensate (V'/3V)^2 = (4 pi G/3)(1 - rho/rho_c) + ..., recovering classical GR for rho << rho_c and producing a bounce at rho = rho_c.
- **Quantum Bounce** — Resolution of the big-bang/big-crunch singularity: the GFT condensate volume reaches a non-zero turning point (V'=0) at a critical Planckian density rho_c, replacing the singularity by a bounce. A repulsive divergent term -Q^2/rho^3 prevents the condensate amplitude from vanishing. Qualitatively matches loop quantum cosmology.
- **Relational Time (Clock Field)** — A minimally coupled massless scalar field phi used as a physical clock; the GFT condensate dynamics and effective Friedmann equations are expressed relationally with respect to phi (derivatives d/dphi), bypassing the problem of time and the absence of background coordinates.
- **Second Quantization of LQG** — Reformulation in which GFT fields are creation/annihilation operators for 'atoms of space' (open spin-network nodes dual to d-simplices). The Fock space of many such quanta reproduces the LQG kinematical Hilbert space; the GFT action encodes the canonical LQG dynamics.

nodes.name: Emergent Spacetime & Phase Transitions
nodes.en: The conceptual and thermodynamic framework for how continuum spacetime arises from pre-geometric GFT degrees of freedom, treating geometrogenesis and emergent cosmology as manifestations of a condensation or critical-point phase transition in the underlying quantum-gravity system.
nodes.brief:
The conceptual and thermodynamic framework for how continuum spacetime arises from pre-geometric GFT degrees of freedom, treating geometrogenesis and emergent cosmology as manifestations of a condensation or critical-point phase transition in the underlying quantum-gravity system.

### Concepts
- **Geometrogenesis / Emergence of Spacetime** — The hypothesis that smooth, continuum spacetime is not fundamental but emerges from a large number of pre-geometric, non-spatiotemporal GFT quanta, analogous to a condensed-matter system (a 'spacetime fluid'). Spacetime emergence is decoupled from the ontology of the fundamental entities.
- **Phase Transition / Geometrogenesis Transition** — Proposal that continuum spacetime emerges as a phase (condensate or critical point) of the underlying GFT system, reached via a quantum-gravity analogue of a thermodynamic limit. Landau-Ginzburg mean-field and FRG analyses determine critical dimensions and conditions for a continuous transition.
- **emergent-cosmology**

nodes.name: TGFT Renormalization & Asymptotic Freedom
nodes.en: The quantum-field-theoretic analysis of tensorial group field theories, covering the renormalizability of TGFT actions under multiscale power counting and the resulting asymptotic-freedom behavior of melonic couplings alongside infrared Wilson-Fisher-type fixed points.
nodes.brief:
The quantum-field-theoretic analysis of tensorial group field theories, covering the renormalizability of TGFT actions under multiscale power counting and the resulting asymptotic-freedom behavior of melonic couplings alongside infrared Wilson-Fisher-type fixed points.

### Concepts
- **Tensorial Group Field Theory (TGFT)** — GFT/tensor field theory with tensorial (combinatorially non-trivial, non-melonically-bonded) interactions, suited to renormalization and FRG analysis; may include both non-local group degrees of freedom and local (scalar/clock) degrees of freedom. Exhibits asymptotic freedom and Wilson-Fisher-type fixed points.
- **GFT/TGFT Renormalization** — Adaptation of QFT renormalization (multiscale analysis, power counting, Wick ordering by subtracting melonic tadpoles) to GFT/TGFT. Rank-4 tensor field theory is renormalizable to all orders; 4D Abelian U(1) TGFT with gauge invariance is super-renormalizable for any polynomial interaction.
- **Asymptotic Freedom (TGFT)** — Generic feature of renormalizable tensorial GFTs: the leading (melonic) coupling has beta function beta(lambda) ~ -lambda^3, so the coupling decreases in the UV. Wavefunction renormalization at one loop is crucial. Coexists with IR Wilson-Fisher-type fixed points.

nodes.name: Holographic Principle & AdS/CFT
nodes.en: The correspondence between gravitational theories in a bulk spacetime and non-gravitational quantum field theories on its boundary, anchored by the Maldacena (AdS/CFT) duality and the covariant entropy bounds that motivate it. Spans the AdS/CFT dictionary, holographic renormalization-group flow, entanglement and bulk reconstruction, extensions to flat-space and de Sitter holography, integrable and thermal/transport applications, and black-hole microstate counting — distinct from the loop-based and causal-set approaches to quantum gravity elsewhere in this branch.
nodes.cs: Korespondence mezi gravitačními teoriemi v objemovém (bulk) prostoročase a negravitačními kvantovými teoriemi pole na jeho hranici, zakotvená v Maldacenově dualitě AdS/CFT a kovariantních entropických mezích, které ji motivují. Zahrnuje slovník AdS/CFT, holografický tok renormalizační grupy, provázanost a rekonstrukci bulku, rozšíření na plochý prostor a de Sitterovu holografii, integrabilní a transportní aplikace i počítání mikrostavů černých děr — na rozdíl od smyčkových a kauzálně-množinových přístupů ke kvantové gravitaci jinde v této větvi.

nodes.name: Entropy Bounds & Holographic Principle
nodes.en: The foundational information-theoretic bounds that motivate holography: the Bekenstein bound on entropy within a spherical region, its covariant (Bousso) generalization to light sheets in arbitrary spacetimes, and the light-sheet construction itself that makes the covariant bound geometrically precise.
nodes.brief:
The foundational information-theoretic bounds that motivate holography: the Bekenstein bound on entropy within a spherical region, its covariant (Bousso) generalization to light sheets in arbitrary spacetimes, and the light-sheet construction itself that makes the covariant bound geometrically precise.

### Concepts
- **Bekenstein Bound** — Universal upper bound on the entropy of a physical system of energy E enclosed in a sphere of radius R: S <= 2 pi k R E / (hbar c). Saturated by a black hole; implies finite information content in finite regions.
- **Covariant Entropy Bound (Bousso Bound)** — Generalized holographic bound (Bousso 1999): entropy on a light sheet (congruence of non-expanding null rays orthogonal to a surface B of area A) satisfies S <= A/4G. Valid in general spacetimes including cosmology, where the spacelike entropy bound fails.
- **Light Sheet** — Central geometric object of the Bousso bound: a null hypersurface generated by a congruence of non-expanding light rays emitted orthogonally from a surface; its entropy flux is bounded by the surface area over 4G.

nodes.name: AdS/CFT Dictionary Foundations
nodes.en: The core statement and defining vocabulary of the Maldacena duality, including the GKP-Witten prescription equating bulk and boundary partition functions, the mass-dimension map between bulk fields and boundary operators, and the 't Hooft large-N coupling that controls the transition between weakly coupled gravity and strongly coupled gauge theory.
nodes.brief:
The core statement and defining vocabulary of the Maldacena duality, including the GKP-Witten prescription equating bulk and boundary partition functions, the mass-dimension map between bulk fields and boundary operators, and the 't Hooft large-N coupling that controls the transition between weakly coupled gravity and strongly coupled gauge theory.

### Concepts
- **AdS/CFT Correspondence** — Exact duality (Maldacena 1997) between quantum gravity / string theory on (d+1)-dimensional anti-de Sitter space and a conformal field theory without gravity on its d-dimensional conformal boundary. Prototype: type IIB strings on AdS5 x S5 dual to N=4 super-Yang-Mills with gauge group SU(N).
- **GKP-Witten Dictionary** — Precise prescription (Gubser-Klebanov-Polyakov and Witten 1998): the CFT generating functional with boundary source phi_0 equals the bulk gravity partition function with that boundary condition, Z_CFT[phi_0] = Z_grav[phi -> phi_0]. Each CFT operator maps to a bulk field; correlators are computed from the on-shell gravity action (Witten diagrams).
- **Field-Operator Map / Mass-Dimension Relation** — Dictionary entry relating a bulk field's mass m to the conformal dimension Delta of the dual boundary operator: Delta(Delta - d) = m^2 L^2. Light fields map to low-dimension (relevant) operators; the Breitenlohner-Freedman bound m^2 L^2 >= -d^2/4 allows stable tachyonic fields.
- **'t Hooft Limit and Coupling** — Large-N planar limit with lambda = g_YM^2 N fixed as N -> infinity. In AdS5/CFT4: L^4/alpha'^2 = 4 pi g_s N = g_YM^2 N = lambda. Strong CFT coupling (lambda >> 1) maps to classical bulk gravity; weak coupling to highly curved stringy geometry.

nodes.name: Holographic RG Flow & c-Theorem
nodes.en: Radial evolution in the AdS bulk reinterpreted as renormalization-group flow on the boundary theory, formalized by the holographic c-theorem's monotonicity result and illustrated by the T-Tbar deformation, which realizes a finite radial cutoff as an irrelevant boundary deformation.
nodes.brief:
Radial evolution in the AdS bulk reinterpreted as renormalization-group flow on the boundary theory, formalized by the holographic c-theorem's monotonicity result and illustrated by the T-Tbar deformation, which realizes a finite radial cutoff as an irrelevant boundary deformation.

### Concepts
- **Holographic RG Flow** — Identification of the bulk radial coordinate with the boundary energy scale: motion into the AdS interior is RG flow toward the IR. Domain-wall (kink) solutions interpolating between AdS vacua of different radius realize RG flows between fixed points; the holographic c-theorem follows from the null energy condition.
- **Holographic c-Theorem** — Monotonicity of a c-function along RG flow proven holographically: the gravity dual is the second law for causal horizons, and the holographic c-function is the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy density of the dynamical causal horizon.
- **T-Tbar Deformation** — Solvable irrelevant deformation of a 2D CFT by the dimension-4 T-Tbar operator; holographically it places the boundary theory on a Dirichlet wall at finite bulk radius, realizing a finite cutoff and connecting to dS/dS holography. The RG flow equation matches the radial Hamilton-Jacobi equation of bulk gravity.

nodes.name: Holographic Entanglement & Bulk Reconstruction
nodes.en: The 'spacetime from entanglement' program: the Ryu-Takayanagi formula geometrizes entanglement entropy, entanglement-wedge (subregion) duality sharpens which bulk region a boundary subregion encodes, HKLL bulk reconstruction and holographic quantum error correction explain how that encoding is redundant and protected, and tensor-network toy models (MERA, HaPPY codes) reproduce these features discretely.
nodes.brief:
The 'spacetime from entanglement' program: the Ryu-Takayanagi formula geometrizes entanglement entropy, entanglement-wedge (subregion) duality sharpens which bulk region a boundary subregion encodes, HKLL bulk reconstruction and holographic quantum error correction explain how that encoding is redundant and protected, and tensor-network toy models (MERA, HaPPY codes) reproduce these features discretely.

### Concepts
- **Ryu-Takayanagi Formula** — Holographic entanglement entropy: the entanglement entropy of a boundary region equals Area(gamma)/(4G) for the minimal bulk surface gamma homologous to it. Geometrizes entanglement, connects spacetime geometry to quantum information, and underlies the 'spacetime from entanglement' program.
- **Bulk Reconstruction (HKLL)** — Procedure (Hamilton-Kabat-Lifschytz-Lowe) expressing a local bulk operator as an integral of boundary CFT operators against a smearing function supported on boundary points spacelike-separated from the bulk insertion. Reveals the bulk's quantum-error-correcting structure.
- **Subregion / Entanglement-Wedge Duality** — A boundary subregion's density matrix encodes (and allows reconstruction of) the bulk physics within its entanglement wedge, the bulk region bounded by the RT/HRT surface. Sharpens locality of the holographic map.
- **Holographic Quantum Error Correction** — Almheiri-Dong-Harlow insight that AdS/CFT realizes operator-algebra quantum error correction: bulk (logical) information is redundantly encoded on the boundary (physical) qubits and protected against erasure of large boundary regions. Modeled by tensor networks (HaPPY code).
- **Tensor Network (Holographic)** — Discrete networks of tensors (MERA, HaPPY perfect-tensor codes, random tensor networks) that reproduce key holographic features: RT formula, negative tripartite information, error-correcting bulk reconstruction. Toy models of emergent geometry from entanglement.

nodes.name: Flat-Space & de Sitter Holography
nodes.en: Extensions of the holographic principle beyond anti-de Sitter space: dS/CFT and static-patch proposals for de Sitter, celestial holography recasting flat-space scattering as a 2D CFT, the soft-theorem/memory/BMS infrared triangle underlying it, and the Carrollian ultra-relativistic reformulation of the same physics.
nodes.brief:
Extensions of the holographic principle beyond anti-de Sitter space: dS/CFT and static-patch proposals for de Sitter, celestial holography recasting flat-space scattering as a 2D CFT, the soft-theorem/memory/BMS infrared triangle underlying it, and the Carrollian ultra-relativistic reformulation of the same physics.

### Concepts
- **dS/CFT and de Sitter Holography** — Conjectured duality (Strominger 2001) between gravity on de Sitter space and a non-unitary Euclidean CFT on the future spacelike boundary; alternative static-patch holography places dual degrees of freedom on the stretched horizon. Far less understood than AdS/CFT.
- **Celestial Holography** — Program recasting the 4D gravitational S-matrix in asymptotically flat space as correlators of a 2D celestial CFT on the celestial sphere. Soft theorems become Ward identities of infinite-dimensional asymptotic (BMS) symmetries; celestial amplitudes are Mellin transforms of momentum-space amplitudes.
- **Soft Theorems / Memory / BMS** — Infrared triangle of asymptotically flat gravity: soft graviton theorems, gravitational memory effect, and BMS (super-translation / super-rotation) asymptotic symmetries are equivalent. They underpin celestial holography and the celestial symmetry algebra (w_{1+infinity}).
- **BMS Asymptotic Symmetry** — Bondi-Metzner-Sachs symmetry: the infinite-dimensional asymptotic symmetry group of asymptotically flat spacetime at null infinity (supertranslations and superrotations). Matching conditions between past and future null infinity constrain gravitational scattering and underlie celestial holography.
- **Carrollian Holography** — Holography of flat / de Sitter space formulated via Carrollian CFTs (the c -> 0 ultra-relativistic limit of conformal symmetry) living on null infinity or the dS boundary; complementary to the celestial (Lorentzian 2D CFT) approach.

nodes.name: Integrability & Exact AdS/CFT Tests
nodes.en: Exact, nonperturbative machinery that turns AdS/CFT into a precision-tested duality: integrability of planar N=4 SYM and its Quantum Spectral Curve solve the spectral problem at any coupling, supersymmetric localization reduces partition functions to matrix models, and the conformal bootstrap constrains CFT data via crossing and unitarity.
nodes.brief:
Exact, nonperturbative machinery that turns AdS/CFT into a precision-tested duality: integrability of planar N=4 SYM and its Quantum Spectral Curve solve the spectral problem at any coupling, supersymmetric localization reduces partition functions to matrix models, and the conformal bootstrap constrains CFT data via crossing and unitarity.

### Concepts
- **Integrability of Planar N=4 SYM** — Planar N=4 super-Yang-Mills is integrable: its one-loop dilatation operator is the Hamiltonian of an integrable spin chain, and the Quantum Spectral Curve gives exact anomalous dimensions at any 't Hooft coupling, providing the most precise quantitative tests of AdS/CFT.
- **Quantum Spectral Curve** — Integrability-based framework giving the exact planar spectrum of N=4 SYM (and the dual string) as the solution of a Riemann-Hilbert problem for a finite set of Q-functions; interpolates from weak to strong coupling.
- **Supersymmetric Localization** — Technique (Pestun 2007) reducing supersymmetric partition functions and Wilson loops on spheres to finite-dimensional matrix integrals, computable exactly at finite coupling; yields exact AdS/CFT checks such as the N^{3/2} free energy of ABJM.
- **Conformal Bootstrap** — Nonperturbative program constraining CFT data using crossing symmetry and unitarity; analytic (Lorentzian inversion formula) and numerical versions test and constrain holographic CFTs, with bulk locality emerging from large-spin/heavy-operator structure.

nodes.name: Holographic Thermal & Transport Physics
nodes.en: Phenomenological applications of gauge/gravity duality to strongly coupled real-world matter: the KSS viscosity bound and its near-saturation by the quark-gluon plasma in heavy-ion collisions, and the broader AdS/CMT program modeling strange metals and holographic superconductors with charged black holes.
nodes.brief:
Phenomenological applications of gauge/gravity duality to strongly coupled real-world matter: the KSS viscosity bound and its near-saturation by the quark-gluon plasma in heavy-ion collisions, and the broader AdS/CMT program modeling strange metals and holographic superconductors with charged black holes.

### Concepts
- **KSS Viscosity Bound** — Holographic lower bound eta/s >= hbar/(4 pi k_B) on the shear-viscosity-to-entropy-density ratio (Kovtun-Son-Starinets), saturated in Einstein gravity. Quark-gluon plasma at RHIC/LHC sits near this bound (eta/s ~ 0.1-0.3), the 'most perfect fluid'.
- **Holographic Quark-Gluon Plasma** — Application of AdS/CFT to the strongly coupled quark-gluon plasma produced in heavy-ion collisions; holography predicts near-perfect-fluid transport (eta/s ~ 1/4 pi), jet quenching, and thermalization, qualitatively matching RHIC/LHC data.
- **AdS/CMT (Holographic Condensed Matter)** — Application of gauge/gravity duality to strongly correlated condensed-matter systems without quasiparticles: holographic superconductors, strange metals, non-Fermi liquids, and momentum-relaxing transport, modeled by charged AdS black holes at finite density.

nodes.name: Black Hole Microstates, Chaos & Information
